Requirements-gathering ran via multiple AskUserQuestion rounds (all director-answered); then Phases 1–3 were built, deployed, and director-verified PASS. **(a.119) remains DEFERRED/queued — NOT closed.** **What shipped (5 deploys):** **Phase 1 (`b134b5d`)** = R1 last-column right-edge resize (a `ColumnResizeHandle` on the trailing row-actions `th` targeting the last visible data column) + R3 removed the redundant blue "Comprehensive Competitor Analysis" button in `CompetitionScrapingViewer` (duplicate of the top "Comprehensive Analysis" nav tab); no schema change. **Phase 2 (`e0661ba` + FF1 `b27841f` + FF2 `1f18308`)** = R2 + R4 — removed the table's inner `overflow:auto`/`maxHeight` scroll box so the WHOLE PAGE scrolls both axes via the browser's own scrollbars; `<thead>` stays `position:sticky;top:0` so the header now pins to the WINDOW (D1 REVISED — director picked whole-page window-scrollbars over the earlier floating-bar idea after Phase-1 feedback); FF1 = shrink-wrap the table wrapper (`width:max-content`) + a 48px trailing gap so the page scrolls to the full table width and the last-column resize edge is grabbable; FF2 = grey panel shrink-wraps to full table width (grey bg/border extends right) + `MAX_COLUMN_WIDTH` raised 600→4000 + auto-scroll-during-resize in `ColumnResizeHandle` (document-space width tracking `[clientX+scrollX]` + `requestAnimationFrame` `window.scrollBy` near the right edge) so the last column drags as far right as needed with the page following; no schema change. **Phase 3 (`7a10ba4`)** = R5 drag-reorder columns by the header (each header gets a ⠿ grip; a horizontal `SortableContext` sharing the ONE `DndContext` with the vertical row-reorder list, id-discriminated in `handleDragEnd`) + the WHOLE main-table layout (column order + show/hide + widths + competitor row order + font size) is now SHARED across the Project: NEW additive Prisma model `ProjectTablePreferences` (`projectId @unique`, incl. NEW `columnOrder Json`) + NEW handler `project-table-preferences.ts` (project-keyed GET/PUT with SEED-ON-READ from the user's `UserTablePreferences` so the pre-existing layout is preserved) + NEW route `/competition-scraping/project-table-preferences` + NEW pure helper `src/lib/competition-scraping/column-order.ts` (`applyColumnOrder` + `moveColumnKey`, +11 node:test) + shared-types `ProjectTablePreferences`; the per-user `UserTablePreferences` is untouched (still backs the per-user By-Category/By-Type page layouts). Director chose drag-the-headers (not the Columns-box list) + share-everything (all 5 settings). `main` went `69da40c → b134b5d → e0661ba → b27841f → 1f18308 → 7a10ba4` (five clean ff-merges); both branches at `7a10ba4` pre-doc-batch. **FIVE Rule 9 deploy gates this session** (Phase 1, Phase 2, FF1, FF2, Phase 3), all director "Deploy now"; the Phase 3 gate also named + authorized the `prisma db push`. **Many Rule 14f non-deploy pickers (requirements-gathering/design), all director-answered; notable OVERRIDES of the Recommended option:** (a) "editable in the table too" for the Phase-5 category cells (over read-only); (b) "shared across the Project" for ALL table settings (over per-user); (c) whole-page window scrollbars over the earlier-picked floating bar (D1 revised). **Schema-change-in-flight flag NO at entry → YES (Phase 3) → FLIPPED YES→NO at the Phase 3 deploy push** (`prisma db push` applied the additive `ProjectTablePreferences` table to prod in 1.27s, zero data loss); **NO at exit; NEXT session = NO at entry.** **Closes:** NONE — (a.119) was DEFERRED by director (NOT closed) and remains queued. **NEW polish item P-54 created** (main-page table enhancements, 9 reqs, 5-phase plan); Phases 1–3 (R1–R5) ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-06-01-b. **Opens (a.120) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-54 Phase 4 — the "Sort By" grouping box** (group rows by Platform / Category / Type as banner-row groups, reusing the By-Category/By-Type pattern; D2 in the spec; resolve open question Q-H first — confirm a "None"/flat default + how grouping coexists with column reorder + per-column sort + row drag) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; then Phase 5 = the text/image/video dynamic category columns (R7/R8/R9; D3–D9; stacked aligned sub-rows, editable-in-table, refetch-on-return, shown-by-default, locked column pairs; likely a schema change; Q-F + Q-I open). One-hundred-and-eighty-first Claude Code session. `session_2026-05-30-b_p49-w5-category-page-interactive-batch` — BUILD + DEPLOY session: TWO builds, TWO deploys, TWO Rule 9 deploy gates; both branches at `469e5c6` pre-doc-batch. **Build `5f07f24` = the interactive batch** — (1) banner-row layout restructure: the category name moved onto its OWN shaded banner row (drag-grip + name + hide-category ✕ on the left; the two category-level AI cells at the right), with every competitor row beneath it so the FIRST competitor is now draggable; (2) two-level @dnd-kit drag-to-reorder in a SINGLE DndContext — drag whole categories (uncategorized pinned last, not draggable) via prefixed `cat:<key>` sortable ids in an outer SortableContext + drag competitors within a category via per-category nested SortableContexts, with each per-competitor multi-`<tr>` sub-row block travelling together via one shared transform; (3) hide-with-restore: hide a competitor or a whole category from THIS page only, with a "Hidden on this page" restore panel that brings them back (never deletes data elsewhere); (4) a NEW per-user/per-Project page-layout "memory" area — an additive nullable `UserTablePreferences.categoryTableLayout` Json column `{ categoryOrder, rowOrderByUrlId, hiddenUrlIds, hiddenCategoryKeys }` threaded through the shared-types wire shape + the existing `/table-preferences` GET/PUT handler (strict validation; `Prisma.DbNull` clears), REUSING the existing endpoint — NO new route. Build `5f07f24` was 9 files +1268/-186; `prisma db push` 1.29s, additive nullable, zero data loss. **Build `469e5c6` (FF1) = fix category drag below the fold** — added `measuring={{ droppable: { strategy: MeasuringStrategy.Always } }}` + `autoScroll={{ threshold: { x: 0, y: 0.25 }, acceleration: 15 }}` to the DndContext (1 file, +10) — director Phase 4 reported category drag failed when the target sat below the fold (the `overflowX:hidden` + floating-scrollbar container silently failed to auto-scroll / re-measure off-screen drop targets); the fix makes it auto-scroll vertically and re-measure off-screen targets. ff-merge ranges `0c9f361..5f07f24` then `5f07f24..469e5c6` to main; ping-ponged to workflow-2 after each. **TWO NEW reusable PATTERNS memorialized** (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-30-b + REVIEWS_PHASE_2_DESIGN §B 2026-05-30-b): (1) "Two-level nested @dnd-kit drag in ONE DndContext via prefixed sortable ids + per-group SortableContexts" — outer SortableContext uses `cat:<key>` ids, each group nests a per-group SortableContext of member ids, one `onDragEnd` disambiguates by the `cat:` prefix + guards member drags to same-group targets (+ maps a category dropped onto a member row to that member's group for forgiving drops); (2) "Off-screen drop targets in an `overflowX:hidden` scroll container need `MeasuringStrategy.Always` + explicit `autoScroll`" — a plain `overflow:auto` container auto-scrolls on @dnd-kit defaults, but an `overflowX:hidden` + floating-horizontal-scrollbar container silently fails to; fix = Always droppable re-measuring + explicit vertical `autoScroll` with the X axis disabled (this is the FF1 root cause). **FOUR Rule 14f decisions this session — 4/4 = 100% Yes-to-Recommended** (memory-drawer storage shape = one additive nullable column on `UserTablePreferences` + banner-row layout = shaded banner with grip+name+hide on the left + the two category AI boxes at the right + interactive-batch deploy gate + FF1 deploy gate); running cumulative was 140/143 → +4 → **144/147 = 98.0% Yes-to-Recommended**. **Schema-change-in-flight flag YES at entry → FLIPPED YES→NO at the interactive-batch deploy push (`5f07f24`); exit NO** (additive nullable column, zero data loss per Rule 23 + `feedback_destructive_ops_confirmation.md`). **Closes (a.115) RECOMMENDED-NEXT** = P-49 W5 Category page "interactive batch" ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-30-b (`5f07f24` + FF1 `469e5c6`). **Opens (a.116) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-49 W5 Category page Session 2 — the two Category AI flows** (bulleted dedup + non-bulleted prose) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`: Q-E AI prompt content drafted jointly with director at that session's start per `feedback_plan_output_shape_before_building.md`; per-review row alignment via real `rowSpan` sub-rows so each review's star sits beside its summary with aligned borders; column-width server-side persistence fixed via a `hasLoadedPrefs` load-before-save gate. **Build `9b1d023` = a vertical-scrollbar-overlap fix** — `scrollbar-gutter: stable` + 48px trailing scroll space so the scrollbar never covers the table's right edge / rightmost resize handle. **TWO NEW reusable PATTERNS memorialized** (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-30 + REVIEWS_PHASE_2_DESIGN §B 2026-05-30): (1) "rowSpan sub-rows to align per-item data across two adjacent columns" — independent stacked cells in separate `<td>`s can't align across columns; making each item a real `<tr>` with the non-per-item columns using `rowSpan` does; (2) "Debounced merge-save must gate on initial-load-complete" — a debounced prefs save that GETs-merges-PUTs will wipe saved prefs if it fires before the initial load populates local state; gate it on a `hasLoaded` flag. **SEVEN Rule 14f decisions this session — 7/7 = 100% Yes-to-Recommended** (3 Block-1 planning questions [Q-A/B per-review-stacked + Q-C reuse prose flow + Q-D mirror xlsx] + 1 first-deploy gate + 3 refinement-round decisions [sequencing=polish-now-interactive-next + removal=hide-with-restore + scope=this-page-only]); (1) **URL (competitor) rows** drag to reorder; each competitor's child review rows ride along; persisted in the SHARED per-user `UserTablePreferences.rowOrder` array — the SAME field the Competitor Content Table uses, so the order tracks across both pages (honors director Q5 → A "reflects everywhere"); NO schema change. (2) **Review rows within a URL** drag to reorder; persisted in the per-page `CapturedReview.sortRankInReviewsTable` column shipped in Deploy 1. The existing `reviews-reorder.ts` handler gained a `field` discriminator (default `'sortRank'` = back-compat with the URL-detail-page order; `'sortRankInReviewsTable'` for this page) so the two orders never collide — NO new route (route count stayed 68). Rendered rows now sort by the rank fields; the Excel export follows the on-screen order too. **CRITICAL Rule 3 (code-wins-over-docs) finding:** the spec (Q5 → A, §3 "Drag-to-reorder", §3 schema notes) named `CompetitorUrl.sortRank` as the URL-row persistence column, claiming it was "the SAME column used by the Competitor Content Table" — **that column never existed.** Code-truth audit at Deploy 2 planning found NO `sortRank` field on the `CompetitorUrl` model in `prisma/schema.prisma`; the Competitor Content Table (`UrlTable.tsx`) actually persists URL order via the per-user `UserTablePreferences.rowOrder` string[] array; the Reviews Analysis page deliberately prefixes its other table-preferences keys (`reviewsTable:`) to AVOID collision — but `rowOrder` is a single shared scalar. Surfaced to the director via the design picker; director chose to keep the "reflects everywhere" behavior via the shared `rowOrder` field (no schema change). NEW read-only 3-column traceability table on the URL detail page (`ReviewsTraceabilityTable.tsx`) — Category / Complaint / source reviews (full review text via title+body merge + star count); category cells rowspan-grouped; legacy `{ summary }` rows fall back to flattened text. NEW pure helpers `reviews-traceability.ts` (parse + buildRows + mergeReviewTitleBody). `UrlDetailContent.tsx` fetches the latest PER_PRODUCT analysis via the existing `GET /review-analysis` route, renders the table on top + relabels the free-text box "Your notes — Captured Reviews" below it. FF1 commit `889667e` (4 files +318/-54) was the ONLY Phase-4 redirect this session: director reported many "Your Analysis" + notes autosave boxes showed "Save failed — HTTP 500" / "Failed to resolve project workflow", "a page refresh fixes it" — root cause = those rich-text boxes PATCH on every debounced edit; each request runs `verifyProjectWorkflowAuth` → a `projectWorkflow.upsert` DB write; during/after a heavy AI summarization run the Supabase connection pool transiently saturates → the upsert throws → route 500s → box stranded until refresh (NOT caused by the Fix D table work — a pre-existing transient-infra exposure the heavy bulleted run surfaced). FF1 fix = NEW shared `src/lib/rich-text/save-with-retry.ts` that transparently retries transient failures (HTTP 5xx + network throws) with exponential backoff (500/1000/2000ms, 4 attempts; 4xx not retried; generation-cancel hook so a newer keystroke supersedes), applied to BOTH `PerItemAnalysisBox` + `OverallAnalysisBox` — a brief DB hiccup now self-heals silently. Director Phase 4 verbatim verdict: table feature "Both worked"; FF1 "Pass". Per-Review flow shipped + Phase-4-verified is now the canonical pattern; Sessions 3+ reuse it with grouping shape + prompt flow changing. **FF#1 root cause** (`ecf292d`): Table 2 page expected `{ items: CompetitorUrl[] }` from GET /urls + `{ items: CapturedReview[] }` from GET /urls/[urlId]/reviews; both endpoints actually return the bare array directly (consistent with existing CompetitionScrapingViewer shape); two-line client-side unwrap fix. **FF#2 root cause** (`d713712`): Phase 4 surfaced a transparency gap — director could fire the Per-Review Summarize batch loop but had no way to see what was being sent to Claude; v1 modal showed config + progress but NOT the actual system prompt or user message; fix-forward shipped expandable "View prompts" panel inside the modal (collapsed by default) showing verbatim PER_REVIEW_SUMMARIZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT + first-batch user message preview built via buildPerReviewBatchUserMessage using actual loaded review data + footer note explaining subsequent batches reuse the same shape with different reviews. **FF#3 root cause** (`cd6478b`): Phase 4 review of v1 output surfaced a content-shape redirect — v1's 1-2 sentence plain-prose third-person neutral summaries were correctly-formed JSON but the prose form was burying load-bearing facts under filler the reviewer themselves had dismissed; v2 prompt rewrite makes the "summary" field a newline-separated flat-bullet list containing ONLY critical info (main stance + strongest specific claim/complaint/praise/use case/experience pattern) with non-critical filler explicitly stripped out (parenthetical asides + mild observations the reviewer themselves dismissed + generic positive/negative comments not tied to specific signals + repeated points); typically 1-4 bullets per review; reviews with no critical signal emit single bullet "- (no critical signal)"; cache-key version bump via NEW `PER_REVIEW_SUMMARIZE_PROMPT_VERSION = 'v2'` constant included in the per-review cache hash so v1's cached summaries don't get served stale when v2 is requested + UI line-break rendering via whiteSpace pre-wrap on the summary cell. **Closes (a.103) RECOMMENDED-NEXT** = P-49 W5 Session 2 — per-batch server endpoint scaffold + Per-Review Summarize end-to-end ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-27 end-to-end on vklf.com. **Opens (a.104) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-49 W5 Session 3 — rewrite remaining 6 of 7 flow builders into `prompts.ts` + start Tables 3 + 4 (By Category + By Type) per §B 2026-05-27 design lock + add their AI run buttons + modals + browser batch loops + first live e2e runs on those 6 flows** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. NEW `scrapeOneStar` + `scrapeCustomersSayBlock` private orchestration helpers. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-25 (**W#2 polish P-48 Session 1 (Diagnostic) ✅ DONE 2026-05-25 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — pure diagnostic session on screen-recording playback stutters reported during 2026-05-24-f verification; ZERO code lands; root cause empirically identified via `ffprobe` + EBML inspection of 3 SCREEN_RECORDING webm files in production + `curl` HEAD/Range tests of Supabase Storage delivery — missing WebM duration metadata in MediaRecorder-produced container headers (every file shows `Duration: N/A` per ffprobe; EBML inspector confirms `format: duration: NOPTS`; canonical MediaRecorder limitation — EBML header written at recording-start before duration is known, never patched after `stop()`); pre-Session-1 working hypothesis "uncapped MediaRecorder bitrate" empirically FALSIFIED (`record-controller.ts:42` already exports `VIDEO_BITS_PER_SECOND_DEFAULT = 2_500_000`; actual files run 0.50 / 1.35 / 0.67 Mbps — well below the cap); pre-Session-1 file path drift corrected (`content-script/screen-recorder.ts` → actual `screen-recording/record-controller.ts`); Session 2 fix path locked = add `fix-webm-duration` npm dep (~3 KB library) + ~10 LOC in `record-controller.ts` post-stop / pre-`onStopped` injecting the JS-known duration into the EBML Segment Info Duration tag** — one-hundred-and-fifty-fifth Claude Code session. **Headline outcome:** the screen-recording stutter is NOT a capture-side bitrate problem (cap is already shipped + files come in well under) NOR a delivery-side problem (Supabase Storage returns `accept-ranges: bytes` + 206 Partial Content on range + Cloudflare edge cache hits on re-requests + 124 Mbps throughput Codespace → file) — it IS missing WebM duration metadata which makes the HTML5 video player see `videoElement.duration === Infinity` on load, breaking its pre-buffer-planning math and causing reactive chunk-fetching that surfaces as visible stutter; Session 2 fix is mechanical (a 3 KB well-known library exists exactly for this — yusitnikov/fix-webm-duration) + small (~10 LOC + new unit-test cases) + predictable. **ONE Rule 14f forced-picker fired** during diagnostic shaping (capture-flow scoping after I incorrectly reported "no webm rows in DB" — director answered *"I've definitely created P-45 screen recordings; they're missing from the DB"* — turned out to be MY query bug: initial filter `sourceType: 'DIRECT_BYTES'` excluded the 3rd enum value `SCREEN_RECORDING`; re-query found 3 rows). **ZERO Rule 9 gates fired** (pure diagnostic; no main push). **Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO** entire session — diagnostic-only; no code; no schema. Pre-build + post-diagnostic /scoreboard NOT run (no code change to verify); baselines unchanged from prior session (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / **558 ext UNCHANGED** / **786 src/lib UNCHANGED** / **62 routes UNCHANGED**); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per Rule 27 non-build-session convention. **Closes (a.89) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-48 Session 1 (Diagnostic) ✅ DIAGNOSED 2026-05-25**; **opens (a.90) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-48 Session 2 (Implementation) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`** — small build + deploy session (one new tiny runtime dep + ~10 LOC in `record-controller.ts` + new test cases + ONE Rule 9 deploy gate + Phase-4 real-Chrome verification by director on a NEW recording made post-Session-2-deploy). After P-48 closes, next-next sessions step through P-43 mechanical prevention small fix → P-26 below-fold scroll evaluation → P-27 re-evaluation → W#2 graduation step → THEN STOP AND EXPLICITLY ASK DIRECTOR for next round of competition-scraping additions per director's verbatim 2026-05-24-f directive. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-25** capturing (a) P-48 hypothesis empirically falsified — capture-side bitrate cap already shipped; root cause is missing WebM duration metadata; (b) **NEW reusable Pattern "ffprobe-first diagnostic reveals hypothesis-bust before any code lands — empirical inspection of artifacts in their actual production state preempts speculative implementation"** — when a polish item has a working hypothesis based on plausible reasoning, inspect the actual production artifacts FIRST via the cheapest available probe (here: `ffprobe` against the 3 webm files + `curl -I --range` against signed URLs) BEFORE drafting Session 2 fix scope; pairs with `feedback_recommendation_style.md` most-thorough/reliable since empirical inspection IS the most-reliable diagnostic; (c) MEDIUM informational sub-observation — query-bug false-alarm where filtering on a 2-of-3 enum value created a false "data integrity bug" report to director (mechanical prevention candidate: when querying tables with multi-value discriminator enums, query ALL values OR `groupBy by sourceType` to surface enum coverage upfront before drilling into subset); **Previously updated:** 2026-05-24-d (**W#2 polish P-47 Shadow DOM refactor Session 1 (content-script video-capture-form mount strategy refactor from `document.body` + 80-event-listener band-aid to open Shadow DOM root with FORM_CHROME_CSS injected inside the shadow + band-aid deletion) ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-24-d on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` via build commit `d08f673` (2 files +203/-220, net -17 LOC)** — pure CODE session on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; NOT pushed to main this session (next session is the bundled W5 + P-47 deploy session per the end-of-session §4 Step 1c picker outcome); build commit sits on workflow branch alongside the 2 prior W5 build commits (`3c981be` + `41172f1`) all awaiting the next deploy session. One-hundred-and-fifty-second Claude Code session. **Headline outcome:** the content-script video-capture form now mounts inside an open Shadow DOM root attached to a fixed-positioned host `<div>` in `document.body`, rather than appending the backdrop directly into `document.body`; events fired inside the shadow root do not surface to page-level handlers by default, so the per-input event-isolation band-aid shipped in P-45 Build #2 2026-05-22-i (20 events × 4 inputs = 80 listeners on each form open) is no longer needed and has been deleted (the `applyAggressiveEventIsolation` function + its 4 call sites + its 20-line context comment, 62 LOC total); `styles.ts` extracts a new exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant containing the `.plos-cs-form-*` + `.plos-cs-chip-*` rules previously inlined in `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS` and re-interpolates them back via `${FORM_CHROME_CSS}` so the other forms (image / text / url-add) that still mount to `document.body` continue to receive these rules via the host-page `<head>` stylesheet without drift between the two consumption sites; `video-capture-form.ts` imports `FORM_CHROME_CSS` + creates the host `<div>` with `position:fixed inset:0 z-index:999998` + `attachShadow({mode:'open'})` + injects `<style>FORM_CHROME_CSS</style>` inside the shadow root + appends the backdrop inside the shadow root; `destroy()` removes the host instead of the backdrop; `focusNewCategoryInput()` reads `shadow.activeElement` instead of `document.activeElement` (since `document.activeElement` returns the host element for any descendant focused inside the shadow); Escape-to-close `keydown` listener stays on `document` (keydown events from inside the shadow root compose up through the host into the document tree). **ONE §4 Step 1c forced-picker fired this session (end-of-session next-session scope only — director's session-start directive *"please defer any real world testing that needs to be done by me and work on the next item otherwise road map for workflow#"* was resolved per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` without a re-confirmation picker since the launch-prompt task was already the recommended default).** Picker offered (A) Bundled W5 + P-47 deploy to main (Recommended) / (B) P-26 below-fold scroll capture Session 1 / (C) P-43 mechanical prevention small fix / (D) question first; intercepts clicks on `<a href="#url/<urlId>">` in both editable + read-only modes via ProseMirror's `handleClick` prop; extracts urlId via the pure helper; calls `options.onInternalLinkClick(urlId)` callback) + NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/components/LinkToUrlPicker.tsx` (~304 LOC toolbar dropdown — lazy-loads URL list via `authFetch` on first open; case-insensitive search filter; on pick inserts text + applies Link mark with `#url/<urlId>` href) + MODIFIED `RichTextEditor.tsx` (+90/-1 LOC) — accepts optional `projectId` prop; uses `useRouter` from `next/navigation`; refs for `projectId` + `router` so the click handler always sees current values; registers `UrlReferenceExtension` with `handleInternalLinkClick` callback; extends `Link.configure`'s `isAllowedUri` to accept `#url/` hrefs (otherwise Link extension's default would reject the non-http href and strip the mark); surfaces `LinkToUrlPicker` in 'full' toolbar only when `projectId` provided; inline `<style>` tag with `InternalLinkStyles` component for distinct styling (icon prefix + same blue underlined per session-start picker outcome) + MODIFIED `AnalysisEditor.tsx` (+7 LOC) accepts + passes-through `projectId` prop + MODIFIED `AnalysisReadView.tsx` (+5 LOC) accepts + passes-through `projectId` prop + MODIFIED `comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (+2 LOC) passes `projectId` (already in scope via `useParams()`) to both children. Build commit `5854eff` — 8 files +786/-1 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; NOT pushed to main since Session 2 is a build session, not a deploy session. One-hundred-and-forty-ninth Claude Code session. **Headline outcome:** the per-Project Comprehensive Competitor Analysis page now supports inline internal hyperlinks — director can write `#url/<urlId>` shorthand inline OR use the new "Link to URL" toolbar dropdown to pick a URL from the current Project's competitor URL list + insert a hyperlink without typing shorthand manually; clicking the rendered hyperlink in either edit or read mode navigates to the matching URL detail page; styled with a small 🔗 emoji glyph prefix to signal internal-URL navigation while keeping the same blue underlined treatment as external links. **TWO Rule 14f forced-pickers fired at session start** — (1) **Session 2 scope:** director picked **"Extension + Link-to-URL picker (Recommended)"** over "Extension only, picker deferred" + "Picker only (NOT recommended)" per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` — most thorough bundle since the picker is the user-facing affordance that makes the shorthand discoverable + ergonomic. (2) **Visual styling of internal hyperlinks:** director picked **"Distinct — small URL icon prefix + same blue (Recommended)"** over "Identical — same blue underlined" + "Distinct — different color" — same blue underlined as external links BUT prefixed with a small 🔗 emoji glyph signaling "internal URL navigation"; implemented via inline `<style>` tag scoped to `.plos-rt-editor a[href^="#url/"]::before { content: "🔗 "; ... }`. **ZERO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution** per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` — extension-vs-Mark choice, ProseMirror `handleClick` over a custom Mark click handler, ref-based projectId/router pattern, and lazy-load URL list timing were all "most thorough/reliable" defaults. **ONE new reusable Pattern memorialized this session** — **"Custom TipTap extension via `addProseMirrorPlugins` for click interception of shorthand hrefs without a custom Mark type"** — when a feature needs to recognize + intercept clicks on a non-standard href shape (here: `#url/<urlId>` shorthand), the lowest-cost design is an Extension (not a new Mark) that adds a ProseMirror plugin via `handleClick`, reads the raw href via `getAttribute('href')` (not `link.href` which is browser-resolved), and delegates the resolution to a consumer-supplied callback; the existing Link mark is reused (just extend `isAllowedUri` to accept the shorthand prefix); a separate visual signal (CSS attribute selector + `::before` content) handles distinctness without a Mark-level type discriminator; pairs with W4 S1's Pattern A "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance" + W4 S1's Pattern B "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer" as the third W4-arc Pattern. **§A frozen** per Rule 18 — today's `#url/<urlId>` shorthand handling consumes §A.4's example syntax + §A.5's "custom TipTap extension" guidance; no §A amendment. **Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO** entire session — no `prisma db push`; pure client-side TipTap extension on top of the W4 S1 editor wrapper + page route + components. All 6 /scoreboard checks GREEN at new baselines (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / 558 ext UNCHANGED / **783 src/lib +19 from baseline 764** — exact match with 19 new url-reference-helpers.test.ts cases / **62 routes UNCHANGED** — no new route paths); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention. **Closes (a.82) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-46 Workstream 4 Session 2**; **opens (a.83) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-46 Workstream 4 deploy session** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — Phase-4 deploy ff-merging `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` carrying W4 Sessions 1+2 build commits + doc-batch commits → Vercel auto-redeploy → ping-pong sync → Phase-4 director real-Chrome cross-platform verify across the new Comprehensive Analysis page surface (Edit/Done toggle + per-Project rich-text doc + internal-hyperlink TipTap extension + "Link to URL" picker dropdown + navigation back to URL detail pages). **§4 Step 1c forced-picker NOT FIRED** — next-session task unambiguous per §C.4 Session 3 spec (deploy session) + the "Multi-session workstream deploy gate timing" reusable Pattern memorialized 2026-05-23-c (deploy session lands after the LAST build session that contains user-visible UI). **`session_2026-05-25_p46-workstream-4-session-2-internal-hyperlink-tiptap-extension`** — PURE CODE session; One-hundred-and-forty-eighth Claude Code session. **Headline outcome:** the per-Project Comprehensive Competitor Analysis page enters its W#4 first build — director can navigate from the Competition Data page via a new standalone button, lands on a per-Project rich-text doc that reads + writes against the W1 `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` Prisma model + persists every edit with optimistic save-lifecycle + `lastEditedBy` audit trail. **TWO Rule 14f forced-pickers fired at session start** — (1) **Session 1 scope:** director picked **Full §C.4 scope (recommended)** over "Conservative pointer scope" + "Handler-only this session" — the §C.4 binding spec was the source of truth; yesterday's NEXT_SESSION.md pointer had defaulted to a more conservative Session 1 scope; Rule 14a tightening directed surfacing the divergence to director. **This is the FIRST APPLICATION of the Rule 14a tightening shipped 2026-05-24** (any UI-shape spec given mid-build must be echoed into binding docs + read back per Rule 14a BEFORE implementation lands). (2) **Navigation surface placement:** director picked **Standalone button above the bar (recommended)** over "Tab strip" + "Button inside ColumnVisibilityBar" — §A.4's "tab at the top" wording resolved cleanly to a standalone button row above `ColumnVisibilityBar`. **ZERO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution** per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`. **TWO new reusable Patterns memorialized this session.** **Pattern A — "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance"** — when an edit affordance needs the same save-lifecycle shape at TWO scope levels (per-row vs. per-Project), extract parallel components that share structure but differ on the per-scope wire details (PerItemAnalysisBox per-row PATCH `{ analysis }` minimal variant + AnalysisEditor per-Project PUT `{ contentJson }` full variant both wrap the same `RichTextEditor`; both have the same Saving/Saved/Failed status-indicator structure; both use a generation-counter ref for stale-response protection); pairs with W2 S1's "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" Pattern (2026-05-25) + W2 S3's "OverallAnalysisBox parallel component" Pattern (2026-05-27) as meta-shape — when a shared per-action lifecycle (debounced persist + status indicator + retry on error) is needed at N scopes (per-row / per-category / per-Project), each new scope adds a thin parallel component that wraps the same editor primitive; the save-lifecycle structure stays uniform via shared property shape, while per-scope wire details (method, body, scope key) parameterize via props. **Pattern B — "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer"** — §A.5 mentions `generateHTML` for non-editor read views; AnalysisReadView instead uses `RichTextEditor readOnly=true` (the wrapper supports a `readOnly` prop since W2 S1); rationale: identical typography between edit and read modes without a duplicate render path; the editor's overhead is one ProseMirror instance per mount, negligible for a single per-Project doc; a separate static renderer would risk style drift between the two paths; worth memorializing as a reusable Pattern for future read-view/edit-view siblings. **Implementation-detail observation (informational; NOT a slip):** §C.4 names the editor wrapper `TipTapEditor.tsx` but the wrapper W2 S1 actually shipped is named `RichTextEditor.tsx`; the rename was captured in W2 S1's §B 2026-05-25 entry; today consumed the actual filename; no §A amendment needed. **Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO** entire session (no `prisma db push`; consumes the `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` Prisma model that's been live since W1's 2026-05-24 + deployed since W2's 2026-05-23-c). no new route paths created); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention. **Closes (a.81) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-46 Workstream 4 Session 1**; **opens (a.82) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-46 Workstream 4 Session 2** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — internal-hyperlink TipTap extension per §C.4 Session 2 spec (custom extension recognizing `#url/<urlId>` shorthand + resolving to URL detail page navigation; may also polish hyperlink-insertion affordance UI on the editor via a "Link to URL" button helper). **§4 Step 1c forced-picker NOT FIRED** — next-session task unambiguous per §C.4 Session 2 spec (internal-hyperlink TipTap extension). **`session_2026-05-24-b_p46-workstream-4-session-1-comprehensive-analysis-page`** — PURE CODE session; POST validates `clientId` (non-empty string) + `starRating` (integer in [1, 5]) + `body` (non-empty string) + `tags` (string array) + `reviewerName` (string|null) + `reviewDate` (ISO date string|null|empty→null) + `analysis` (trust-boundary via `isValidAnalysisPayload`) + `source` (defaults to 'manual'); 404 on parent-URL-not-found; 201 happy; 200 idempotent on Prisma P2002 unique-constraint violation (looks up existing row by `clientId` and returns it). **NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/reviews-by-id.ts`** (~290 LOC) is the DI seam for the per-record PATCH + DELETE handlers — sets a NEW PRECEDENT relative to text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId] (which were direct-shape with no DI seam); per-record review handlers extracted to a DI seam because the analysis-field trust boundary + the per-field allowlist warrant unit coverage at the handler layer without needing to mock Next.js (node:test loads the handler directly via `node --test --experimental-strip-types`). PATCH allowlist: `starRating` (integer 1-5) / `body` (non-empty string) / `reviewerName` (string|null) / `reviewDate` (ISO date string|null) / `tags` (string[]) / `analysis` (trust-boundary via `isValidAnalysisPayload`); ownership check via `findFirst` with the relation filter `competitorUrl: { projectWorkflowId }`; P2025 errors map to 404 (PATCH) or 200 idempotent (DELETE). **32 new node:test cases** across the 2 new handler test files (17 url-reviews + 15 reviews-by-id). **MODIFIED `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts`** (~82 LOC) — was W1's 501-stub; now a thin shim adapting `makeUrlReviewsHandlers` to NextRequest/NextResponse + CORS, mirroring the text route shim exactly. **NEW `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts`** (~80 LOC) — per-record PATCH + DELETE thin shim at the SHALLOW PRECEDENT PATH matching text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId]. **DELETED `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts`** — Workstream 1's nested-path 501-stub. Removed because review IDs are globally unique so the deeper path adds no security and the shallower path keeps the per-record PATCH/DELETE surface uniform across all 4 capture types (the collection route at `urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts` keeps the deeper path because parent-URL ownership lookup naturally lives there, matching `urls/[urlId]/text/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/images/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/videos/route.ts` precedent). **NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/CapturedReviewAddModal.tsx`** (~479 LOC) — manual-add modal mirroring `CapturedTextAddModal.tsx` structure but with a 1-5 star-rating picker widget (5 buttons rendered as ☆ / ★ with click-to-select + "Pick 1–5" / "N of 5" inline label) + body textarea + reviewer-name text input + native HTML date input + tags input (comma-separated → string[]); **Previously updated:** 2026-05-27 (**W#2 polish P-46 Workstream 2 (URL detail page redesign) Session 3 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-27 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — third build session of the P-46 Workstream 2 implementation arc (Session 3 of 3-5 estimated per §C.2); completed the "Analysis surface" arc across all capture levels via a new `OverallAnalysisBox` component shipping URL-level Overall Competitor Analysis + per-category Overall Analysis boxes (one per capture category — Text / Image / Video) at the bottom of each capture subsection + one URL-level Overall Competitor Analysis at the bottom of the URL detail page; extended the existing `urls/[urlId]` PATCH route allowlist for the two new fields with a new `isValidOverallAnalysesBag` trust-boundary guard that strict-rejects unknown category keys to catch typos at the boundary; bag-merge over replace at the PATCH ensures saving one category doesn't wipe sibling categories. Build commit `4773b62` — 5 files +452/-1 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; NOT pushed to main since Session 3 is a build session, not a deploy session. **Headline outcome:** the URL detail page now carries a per-category Overall Analysis text box at the bottom of `CapturedTextSubsection` + `CapturedImagesGallery` + `CapturedVideosGallery` (one rich-text editor per capture category synthesizing all items in that category) + one URL-level Overall Competitor Analysis text box at the bottom of the URL detail page (synthesizing all categories) — all consuming the same Session 1 `RichTextEditor` wrapper with `variant='minimal'`, all persisting to the Workstream-1 schema columns `CompetitorUrl.overallCompetitorAnalysis` (Text?) + `CompetitorUrl.overallAnalyses` (Json bag keyed by category) without needing any new schema. **NEW `OverallAnalysisBox.tsx`** (~200 LOC) is a parallel component to Session 1's `PerItemAnalysisBox.tsx` (not a reuse-via-props extension) — same save-lifecycle shape (Saving… / ✓ Saved / Save failed indicators + generation-counter stale-response handling) but different wire body shape driven by a `field` discriminator prop: `{ kind: 'overallCompetitorAnalysis' }` emits `{ overallCompetitorAnalysis: <doc> }`; `{ kind: 'overallAnalyses', category: 'text'|'image'|'video'|'reviews' }` emits `{ overallAnalyses: { [category]: <doc> } }`. **NEW `isValidOverallAnalysesBag` trust-boundary guard in `tiptap-helpers.ts`** (+37 LOC) strict-rejects non-objects/null/arrays/primitives + strict-rejects unknown category keys (catches typos like `txet` at the boundary instead of silently writing them through) + requires each known-category value pass `isValidAnalysisPayload`; new `OVERALL_ANALYSES_CATEGORIES` constant + `OverallAnalysesCategory` type export. **Existing `urls/[urlId]` PATCH route allowlist extended** (+65 LOC) for two new fields — `overallCompetitorAnalysis` via existing `isValidAnalysisPayload` + `overallAnalyses` via new `isValidOverallAnalysesBag`. The `overallAnalyses` field MERGES the incoming partial bag onto the existing row's bag (reads `existingRow.overallAnalyses` first, then spreads incoming on top) so per-category boxes saving only their own slot don't wipe sibling categories. **`UrlDetailContent.tsx` modified** (+65/-1) — threads `overallAnalysisInitial` prop through to all 3 subsections (Text / Image / Video); each subsection renders an `OverallAnalysisBox` at the bottom (category=text/image/video); page-level `OverallAnalysisBox` for `overallCompetitorAnalysis` renders after `CapturedVideosGallery` at page bottom. **10 new node:test cases for `isValidOverallAnalysesBag`** in `tiptap-helpers.test.ts` (+88 LOC) pinning down the strict-shape guard's contract (empty bag → true; one known category → true; all four known categories → true; null → false; array → false; primitive string/number/bool → false; unknown key like 'txet' → false; known key + null value → false; known key + array value → false; known key + primitive value → false). Test count 628 → 638 (+10). **Director picked Option A at the Rule 14f session-start scope picker between 3 §C.2-aligned candidates** (A URL-level + per-category Overall Analysis recommended / B Captured Reviews UI alt / C URL-level structural fields alt) — completes the "Analysis surface" arc across all capture levels (per-item Sessions 1-2 + per-category + URL-level this session) before moving to remaining §C.2 work (Captured Reviews + new structural URL-level fields) in Sessions 4-5. **NO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution** per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` (bag-merge over replace at PATCH was the only valid choice — replace would wipe sibling categories; **Previously updated:** 2026-05-22-h (**W#2 polish P-44 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-h on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — closes the multi-reproduction `wxt build` + `wxt zip` parent-process hang class via build commit `57ed383` (5 files changed +61/-11 — 2 NEW programmatic-API wrapper scripts + `package.json` script rewires + `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` KNOWN ISSUE → wrapper-note swaps). **Headline fix:** every prior `wxt build` + `wxt zip` invocation left the Node process alive past artifact completion because wxt 0.20.25 → Vite 8.0.11 → Rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 (the new Rust-based bundler that replaced Rollup in Vite 8) spawns worker threads that aren't released when the build promise resolves — Node's event loop retains active handles and never drains, so wxt's CLI awaits the build then hangs forever on the natural event-loop drain that never happens. Fix shape: NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` (16 LOC) + NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (16 LOC) — both import the programmatic API (`import { build } from 'wxt'; await build(); process.exit(0);` with browser-flag passthrough + try/catch for non-zero exit on error) and force-exit with `process.exit(0)` after the promise resolves, bypassing the broken event-loop drain entirely; safe because all userland work has completed by the time we exit (dist artifacts on disk, success log printed, zip artifact written). `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` 4 scripts rewired through the wrappers: `build` + `build:firefox` + `zip` + `zip:firefox`. **Scope expansion beyond ROADMAP entry's narrow `wxt build` shape per Rule 24:** ROADMAP entry P-44 (line 205) names the bug as the `wxt build` parent-process hang. The fix expanded to ALSO cover `wxt zip` because `wxt zip` internally calls `internalBuild()` from the same `core/utils/building/internal-build.mjs` (verified by reading `core/zip.mjs`); both commands exhibit the identical hang signature for the identical root cause; both are invoked from `npm run` scripts; fixing build but not zip would have left the deploy session's Step 8 (fresh extension zip) still hanging. Symmetric fix across both wrappers is the most-thorough Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation choice. **Root-cause investigation (the empirical path):** read wxt's CLI internals (`node_modules/wxt/dist/cli/commands.mjs`, `core/build.mjs`, `core/builders/vite/index.mjs`) — `isRolldownVersion(vite.version)` returns `true` for Vite ≥8; Vite 8.0.11's package.json confirms rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 dependency + the `@rolldown/binding-linux-x64-gnu` native binding present; wxt 0.20.26 + 0.21.0 changelogs reviewed via GitHub releases — no relevant fix upstream; Vite upstream issue #18224 documents related "Vite can hang the node.js process" pattern. **Pre-fix reproduction confirmed:** `rm -rf .output && timeout 45 npm run build` hung past 45s (exit 124 = timeout), dist visible at +6s, process still alive at +36s. **Post-fix verification (from `extensions/competition-scraping/`):** `npm run build` cold (after `rm -rf .output`) — 4s, exit 0, dist valid; `npm run zip` cold — 3s, exit 0, 202,601-byte zip artifact valid at `.output/competition-scraping-extension-0.1.0-chrome.zip`; `npm run build` warm (no clean) — 4s, exit 0; 3x rapid-fire `npm run build` — 3s each, all exit 0 (rules out flakiness). Pre-end-of-session /scoreboard ran from `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub`: Check 1 root tsc clean / Check 2 extension tsc clean / Check 3 extension `npm test` **558/558** (exact baseline) / Check 4 src/lib node:test **590/590** (exact baseline) / Check 5 `npm run build` **57 routes** (exact baseline) / Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention. All GREEN. No regressions. **Does NOT close (a.67) RECOMMENDED-NEXT** — (a.67) was P-45 Build #2, which remains deferred + unchanged in scope; this session is the THIRD polish-detour in a row ahead of P-45 Build #2's deploy session (2026-05-22-f P-42 backup-memory hook fix; 2026-05-22-g P-43 absolute-paths discipline; 2026-05-22-h P-44 wxt build/zip hang fix). All three detours triggered by director's standing directive at session start to "defer any testing in this session and work on the next item that does not require manual testing." While running Check 3 of /scoreboard inline (not via the slash-command verbatim), Claude typed `npm test 2>&1 | tail -10` WITHOUT the absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping &&` prefix that the scoreboard.md template specifies; the shell ended up at `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub` and got "Missing script: test" loud-fail; caught immediately, re-ran with the absolute cd, got the correct 558/558. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-22-f (**W#2 polish P-42 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-f on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook field-name fix + sibling `track-edited-docs.sh` fix + NEW Layer-3b mirror-staleness SessionStart canary landed via build commit `2e3270d` (4 files changed +178/-4). **Empirical root-cause confirmation (the headline finding):** added temporary diagnostic instrumentation to `backup-memory-dir.sh` that wrote raw PostToolUse stdin to `/tmp/plos-hook-debug.log`, triggered a disposable memory-file Write (`_diagnostic_test_p42.md`), captured the actual JSON stdin shape, and confirmed Claude Code's PostToolUse hook stdin uses the field name `.tool_name` — NOT `.tool`. Both `backup-memory-dir.sh` + the sibling `track-edited-docs.sh` were using `jq -r '.tool // empty'`, which always resolved to `""`, so the Edit/Write gate inside each hook always exited without doing work. **Three P-42 reproductions across 2026-05-20-b, 2026-05-20-c, and 2026-05-21 all stemmed from this single field-name mismatch** — diagnosed + fixed + integration-tested + cleaned up post-test within this session. Diagnostic instrumentation + test files (`_diagnostic_test_p42.md` + `_integration_test_p42.md`) cleaned up post-test before commit. **Fixes shipped:** (a) `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` jq selector `.tool` → `.tool_name` + hook-contract comment updated to document the empirically-confirmed stdin shape; (b) `.claude/hooks/track-edited-docs.sh` same fix (sibling hook had identical bug; the `.claude/session-modified-docs.log` had been 0-byte since May 20 because of the same root cause — captured per Rule 24 as scope-adjacent to P-42 since the same bug class surfaced during diagnosis); (c) NEW `.claude/hooks/check-memory-mirror-staleness.sh` SessionStart canary (~175 LOC) for Layer-3b defense-in-depth — compares source memory dir against `.codespace-backup/memory/` mirror on every session start + emits 🚨 alert via `additionalContext` if file count / presence / per-file size diverges (tested in 3 scenarios — healthy / source-only file / size mismatch — all expected outputs); (d) `.claude/settings.json` wires the new canary into the SessionStart hooks array alongside existing `inject-next-session-pointer.sh` + `check-memory-dir-status.sh`. **Layer architecture after this ship:** Layer 1 MECHANICAL = `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook (NOW WORKING after this session's fix); Layer 2 PROCEDURAL = Rule 29 + `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh`; Layer 3 DETECTION = `check-memory-dir-status.sh` SessionStart canary for SOURCE-MISSING / SOURCE-EMPTY / SOURCE-PARTIAL-LOSS; **NEW Layer 3b DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH this session = `check-memory-mirror-staleness.sh`** for SOURCE-vs-MIRROR DIVERGENCE (catches drift if Layer 1 ever regresses OR if some other path leaves the mirror stale); Layer 4 RECOVERY = `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh`. sink is `onRegionPicked(rect)` instead of `captureAndCrop`; banner copy announces recording + 3-min cap) and `recording-indicator-overlay.ts` (per §C.15; region indicator with thin red dashed border + `pointer-events:none` + floating toolbar at top-center with REC ● badge + pulsing `plos-cs-rec-pulse` CSS keyframe + M:SS countdown + Stop + Cancel buttons + PREPARING state for the dialog-up window); (c) **9 file edits wiring the foundation into the user-visible surface** — `messaging.ts` (new EnterVideoRegionRecordModeMessage + SubmitVideoScreenRecordingRequestUploadRequest + SubmitVideoScreenRecordingFinalizeRequest + their response envelopes + extended `isContentScriptMessage` + `isBackgroundRequest` type-guards), `api-bridge.ts` (`submitVideoScreenRecordingRequestUpload` + `submitVideoScreenRecordingFinalize` helpers proxying through background), `background.ts` (`CONTEXT_MENU_RECORD_VIDEO` id `plos-add-record-video` with title locked **"Record video for PLOS"** per §C.19 #1 + `onClicked` branch + `handleSubmitVideoScreenRecordingRequestUpload` + `handleSubmitVideoScreenRecordingFinalize` proxying to existing `requestVideoUpload` + `finalizeVideoUpload` routes), `orchestrator.ts` (new `enter-video-region-record-mode` handler opens region-record overlay → on region picked creates RecordController + indicator overlay → wires onTick/onStopped/onCanceled to drive the indicator + open `video-capture-form` on stop → surfaces capture-failure toasts on dialog-dismissed / recorder-error), `video-capture-form.ts` (new `kind:'screen-recording'` branch with preview via `<video controls>` from `Blob` `createObjectURL` + source-kind banner showing size + duration + Save calls `uploadScreenRecording()` smart-client orchestrator + extracts first-frame thumbnail first + `URL.revokeObjectURL` on form destroy via stashed `_plosBlobUrl`), `styles.ts` (CSS for video-region-record-overlay + recording-indicator region + recording-indicator toolbar + `plos-cs-rec-pulse` keyframe), `captured-video-validation.ts` (bytes-required gate broadened symmetrically to accept `SCREEN_RECORDING` paralleling `DIRECT_BYTES` branch — the prior `if (draft.sourceType === 'DIRECT_BYTES')` becomes `if (draft.sourceType === 'DIRECT_BYTES' || draft.sourceType === 'SCREEN_RECORDING')`), and `record-controller.test.ts` (+5 canvas-crop cases); plus the 3 new test files mirroring the §C.18 enumeration — `thumbnail-extraction.test.ts` (8 cases) + `video-region-record-overlay.test.ts` (8 cases) + `recording-indicator-overlay.test.ts` (12 cases). **Previously updated:** 2026-05-22-c (**W#2 polish P-27 Captured-videos feature BUILD #9 DEVTOOLS-COOPERATIVE DEBUGGING + P-45 SCREEN-RECORDING DESIGN-PIVOT SESSION — Bug #12 Save-fails-Network-unreachable diagnosed via 1-step DevTools cooperation (root cause: failing fetch URL is `blob:https://www.amazon.com/...` with `net::ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND`; Amazon + Ebay use Media Source Extensions; blob URLs are unreachable from the background SW; Build #8's `retryOnTransportError` helper couldn't have ever cleared it because the failure is at the URL-fetchability layer not the transport layer). Director's escape-hatch question at the fix-direction picker surfaced **screen recording as a 4th universal solution** that bypasses the entire blob: + cross-origin walker + native-controls quirk class of bugs by going through the user's screen as the source-of-truth. Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact. **Three mid-build judgment calls captured in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-22 entry (per Rule 18 append-only):** (1) DIRECT_BYTES thumbnail assertion strictness — picked the "tolerate both paths" approach per §A.12 NULL-thumbnail fallback. The spec tracks `phase2ThumbnailFired` boolean; the headless Playwright env may not always reach `readyState>=2` for the canvas frame-grab against mocked video bytes, so a hard-assert that Phase-2b thumbnail PUT fires would be over-strict; the spec asserts conditional Phase-2b method + Content-Type only when it fired, and conditionally asserts finalize's `thumbnailStoragePath` (present iff Phase 2b fired). Empirically Phase-2b DID fire in headless mode on this run; the conditional structure is forward-compatible if a future Chromium upgrade flips that behavior. Reversible. (2) Embed-platform name capitalization assertion — picked code-truth over branding aesthetics per Rule 3. The form's source-kind banner reads `"Recognized as ${capitalize(embedPlatform)}"` per `video-capture-form.ts:182-184`, which capitalizes the first letter of the helper's lowercase 'youtube' → 'Youtube' (single-capital, NOT 'YouTube' camel-case). The spec uses `toContainText('Recognized as Youtube')` to match the form's exact rendering. If the form later changes to use 'YouTube' for branding correctness, this spec assertion + the form code need to update in lockstep; captured for future awareness; no scope-add. (3) Right-click capture-phase listener dispatch via Playwright `page.dispatchEvent('#hero-video', 'contextmenu')` — distinct from the image spec's approach. The image orchestrator at `orchestrator.ts:896` reads `msg.srcUrl || lastRightClickImageSrc || ''` (SW→tab message can carry srcUrl directly); the video orchestrator at line 927 ONLY reads `lastRightClickVideoResult` (no msg.srcUrl fallback). So the video spec MUST dispatch a real `contextmenu` event on the target FIRST so the orchestrator's capture-phase listener populates the snapshot; THEN the SW→tab open message reads it. Implementation: `await page.dispatchEvent('#hero-video', 'contextmenu')` BEFORE the `serviceWorker.evaluate` chrome.tabs.sendMessage retry loop. Documented in the spec file header comment for future maintainers. **Pre-end-of-session scoreboard (all GREEN — only Playwright delta):** root tsc clean (unchanged) / extension tsc clean (unchanged) / `npm run build` **57 routes** (unchanged) / src/lib node:test **589/589** (unchanged) / extension `npm test` **482/482** (unchanged) / **Playwright 91 → 94 (+3 NEW cases — all GREEN at first run)** in 2.0 min total runtime. **§4 Step 1c forced-picker NOT FIRED as a separate decision** — per design doc §A.2 implementation arc table row #8, the natural-continuation next session is P-27 Build #7 (deploy session — `/deploy` orchestration). **(a.60) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-27 implementation #7 (Deploy session — pre-deploy `/scoreboard` GREEN → Rule 9 director-Yes gate → ff-merge → push origin/main → Vercel auto-redeploy → ping-pong sync → fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-22-w2-deploy-N.zip`) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** **DEFERRED items count (Rule 26):** ZERO open `DEFERRED:` items at session end. **CORRECTIONS_LOG-tier observations this session:** ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry — **P-43 CWD-drift reproduction #4** — `npm test` ran from root instead of extension dir at mid-scoreboard Check 5; downstream W#3+ consumers don't exist yet). **Three mid-build judgment calls captured in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-21-d entry (per Rule 18 append-only):** (1) API shape — corrected at drift-check time. The launch prompt framed the new "list captured videos WITH signed URLs" endpoint as a Rule 14f forced-picker between (A) `?withUrls=1` query param on the existing GET .../videos route vs. (B) sibling `.../videos/withUrls/` route. Drift check caught that the actual image sibling chose NEITHER — `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/images/route.ts` (line 109) simply returns `CapturedImageWithUrls[]` from the existing GET endpoint; `ListCapturedImagesResponse = CapturedImageWithUrls[]` at `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.ts:513`. The truest "mirror exactly" option is a third one: upgrade the existing GET .../videos route's response shape from `CapturedVideo[]` to `CapturedVideoWithUrls[]` — no param, no sibling route. Director gave go-ahead after the drift-check correction; per Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation exception, the picker was skipped and Claude proceeded with the corrected recommendation. This is exactly what the §B 2026-05-21 (Build #2) entry anticipated: *"the list endpoint can grow `WithUrls` variant later when needed."* Reversible — the new shape is additive and a future split could re-introduce a separate sibling endpoint if needed. (2) Renderer scope — minimal-viable URL detail page section. The design doc §A.2 row #6 remainder names "URL detail page renderer for captured videos — inline `<iframe>` for embeds + inline `<video>` for direct MP4 + click-to-play overlay on thumbnails." Build #5 ships the renderer ONLY — no manual-add modal (like CapturedImagesGallery has via CapturedImageAddModal), no per-row delete dialog, no inline metadata editor. Those mirror later-slice extensions on the image gallery and can ship as polish items if the real-Chrome verification at Build #8 surfaces the need. Click-to-play is provided natively by HTML5 `<video controls>` (browser shows poster + play button + standard transport controls) and by `<iframe>` (YouTube/Vimeo serve their own thumbnail + play affordance) — no custom click-to-play overlay component was needed. Reversible — the add/delete/edit affordances can land in a follow-up polish session. (3) Extension-side type-tightening — match the new wire contract end-to-end. The wire-shape upgrade from `CapturedVideo[]` to `CapturedVideoWithUrls[]` is structurally a superset; the extension's content-script saved-video indicator scan only reads `originalSrcUrl`, so the type tightening was optional at the runtime level. Could have (a) kept extension return types as `CapturedVideo[]` (extension stays narrowly typed for its narrow consumer) OR (b) tightened to `CapturedVideoWithUrls[]` (matches the wire shape end-to-end; future extension code can use the new fields). Picked (b) per Rule 3 ("code is the ultimate source of truth") — the truth is that the wire returns `CapturedVideoWithUrls[]`, and the most honest type signature matches it. (2) EMBED vs. DIRECT_BYTES — single form with internal branching on Save vs. two separate forms — picked single form (form UX is symmetric — same saved-URL picker + category + composition + embedded-text + tags + Save button; only the preview area + network calls on Save differ; one entry-point for the user; branch invisible). Director's drift-check approval at session start (responded "go") confirmed this default-to-recommendation pick. (3) Embed-platform name field collision — initial draft had `platform: string` on the embed branch which collided with the existing `platform: Platform` (W#2 site platform name = amazon/ebay/etc.) at the top level of the form's props; renamed embed-branch field to `embedPlatform: string` so both branches share `platform: Platform` at the top level cleanly; caught mid-build via TypeScript discriminated-union narrowing failure; fixed before any tests ran. **Pre-end-of-session scoreboard (all GREEN):** root tsc clean (unchanged) / extension tsc clean (unchanged) / `npm run build` **57 routes** (unchanged baseline; Build #3 is extension-only — no new API routes) / src/lib node:test **589/589** (unchanged) / extension `npm test` **469/469** (+41 over baseline 428 = 20 helper + 21 validator) / Playwright **91/91** (unchanged). **§4 Step 1c forced-picker NOT FIRED as a separate decision** — per design doc §A.2 implementation arc table rows #5+#6, the natural-continuation next session is P-27 Build #4 (popup paste form + saved-video indicator). **(a.57) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-27 implementation #4 (Build session — popup paste form + inline-add for popup + saved-video indicator) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** **DEFERRED items count (Rule 26):** ZERO open `DEFERRED:` items at session end. **CORRECTIONS_LOG-tier observations this session:** TWO NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entries — (i) P-44 reproduction #N — `npm run zip` (= `wxt zip`) parent-process hang surfaced once more; the `.output/competition-scraping-extension-0.1.0-chrome.zip` was actually produced (195 KB, visible via `ls -la`), but the parent `npm run zip` shell process never exited. shell state shared in a way the harness manages; one `cd` landed inside the extension dir first, then the second parallel Bash's relative `cd` errored trying to go into `extensions/competition-scraping/extensions/competition-scraping`; same collision caused a parallel `find src/lib -name '*.test.ts'` to silently look at the EXTENSION's `src/lib` and return 428 cases instead of the expected 577 — looked correct because 428 happens to match the extension test count baseline coincidentally). Lesson: when running multiple Bash calls in parallel that need specific CWDs, use absolute paths instead of relative `cd`. Suggested follow-up: update `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` to use absolute paths throughout so the skill template itself doesn't carry the relative-cd footgun. **Group A modified this session:** this ROADMAP (header + P-27 polish-backlog entry annotated with "✅ Build #1 complete 2026-05-20-c" + new DEFERRED sub-item for Supabase Global File Size Limit raise + (a.54) flipped to ✅ DONE-at-code-level in this header narrative + new (a.55) RECOMMENDED-NEXT opened in this header narrative); NEW `src/lib/ssrf-guard.test.ts` (~565 LOC, 37 security-class node:test cases covering all IPv4 RFC 1918 / loopback / link-local / CGNAT / TEST-NET / multicast / broadcast / reserved + IPv6 loopback / link-local / unique-local / multicast / unspecified / documentation + IPv4-mapped-IPv6 v4-defer + NAT64 v4-defer + bracketed-v6 + scheme rejection + metadata-hostname pre-resolve blocklist + DNS-rebind catch via injected lookupFn); NEW `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/images/fetch-by-url/route.ts` (~280 LOC) — POST imageUrl → SSRF-guarded fetch → content-type validation against `ACCEPTED_IMAGE_MIME_TYPES` → 5 MB size cap → 10s timeout → 3xx-redirect refusal → server-side direct upload to Supabase Storage via NEW `uploadBytesAsServer` helper → returns `FetchImageByUrlResponse` (capturedImageId + storagePath + mimeType + fileSize + previewUrl signed-URL-for-client-thumbnail) so the modal can preview before the user finalizes; NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/CapturedImageAddModal.tsx` (~640 LOC) — three input modalities (drag-drop zone with dragover highlight + clipboard-paste listener active while modal open + URL-of-image text field with client-side scheme check); image preview thumbnail; optional metadata fields (Image Category via VocabularyPicker, Composition, Embedded Text, Tags); submit converges drag-drop + paste paths through Phase 1 requestUpload + Phase 2 signed-URL PUT + Phase 3 finalize while the URL path skips Phase 1+2 (bytes already uploaded server-side) and goes straight to finalize; mirrors `UrlAddModal` / `CapturedTextAddModal` shape exactly (Escape / Cancel / X / backdrop dismiss / submit-in-flight lock / `crypto.randomUUID()` clientId / POSTs `source: 'manual'`); NEW `tests/playwright/p29-manual-add-captured-image-modal.spec.ts` (~290 LOC, 17 UI-mechanical test cases all `test.skip()` pending P-30 React-bundle rig). **Modified files:** `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.ts` adds `FetchImageByUrlRequest` + `FetchImageByUrlResponse` interfaces; `src/lib/competition-storage.ts` adds `uploadBytesAsServer` helper for server-side direct upload bypassing the signed-URL handshake (admin client uses upsert:true; idempotent); `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/images/finalize/route.ts` wires `body.source` validation via `isSource` + persists `createData.source ?? 'extension'` (the runtime persistence Slice #1's DTO field needed — Slice #3 completes the wire-in); `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/url/[urlId]/components/UrlDetailContent.tsx` adds `refreshImages` callback (refetches images list after manual-add since finalize response lacks signed URLs) + extends `CapturedImagesGallery` to accept `projectId`/`urlId`/`onImageAdded` + renders "+ Manually add captured image" button at right end of section h2 row + mounts modal. **SSRF design pick — Option A (from session-start drift check) — manual DNS resolution + connect by IP + override Host header + TLS SNI**: chosen for security-class code per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (most thorough and reliable); implemented WITHOUT any new npm dependency (no undici needed — Node's built-in `node:https` / `node:http` `request()` accepts `host:<ip>` + `headers.host=<hostname>` + `servername:<hostname>` + a stub `lookup` that always returns the validated IP, closing the DNS-rebind window entirely). **Verification scoreboard — all GREEN:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; **Previously updated:** 2026-05-14 (**W#2 polish session #18 — P-23 Amazon main-image right-click context-menu fix SHIPPED at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** Ninety-fifth Claude Code session — `session_2026-05-14_w2-polish-session-18-p23-amazon-right-click-context-menu-SHIP`. Closes (a.27) RECOMMENDED-NEXT. **Bug:** on real Amazon product-listing pages, right-clicking the main product image did NOT show the "Add to PLOS — Image" menu because Amazon wraps the `<img>` in zoom/overlay elements that intercept the `contextmenu` event before Chrome recognizes the target as `contexts: ['image']`. Workaround pre-fix: click image → Amazon larger-viewer pane → right-click there → menu fires + works. Affects only Amazon; severity MEDIUM. **Fix shape picked — refined Option (A) per launch-prompt recommendation:** widen `chrome.contextMenus` `contexts` from `['image']` to `['all']` in `background.ts` so the menu fires on any right-click target; new content-script helper `find-underlying-image.ts` walks up from the right-click target (depth ≤ 10) AND scans each ancestor's immediate descendants (sibling-img case the Amazon overlay pattern requires) for an `<img>` with non-empty `currentSrc`/`src`; orchestrator attaches a capture-phase `contextmenu` listener at the TOP of `runOrchestrator` BEFORE any awaits (caught + fixed a listener-attach race during Playwright spec-development this session) that always updates `lastRightClickImageSrc`; when `open-image-capture-form` arrives with empty `srcUrl` from background, handler falls back to the cache; both-empty → silent bail (replaces the old background-side guard). Walmart/eBay/Etsy unchanged: Chrome's image-context detection still fires for direct-`<img>` targets under `contexts: ['all']`, populating `info.srcUrl` natively; the cache is never consulted on those platforms. **UX cost:** "Add to PLOS — Image" now appears on right-click of any element (slight noisiness; bail-silently semantics keep it functionally correct). **Pre-ship verification scoreboard — all GREEN:** ext tsc clean; ext `npm test` **334/334** (was 323; +10 new `findUnderlyingImage` unit tests + 1 from refactor); root Playwright extension project **31/31** (was 29; +2 new specs: positive overlay-shield → falls-back-to-cache; negative plain-text → silent bail); ext build clean in 1.42s; content.js 62,437 → 63,038 bytes (+601 bytes within "few hundred bytes" target). **Files changed:** modified `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/background.ts` (widen contexts + drop early bail) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (top-of-function contextmenu listener + cache-fallback in open-image-capture-form handler + detach in all early returns + main cleanup); NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/find-underlying-image.ts` (~95 lines, MAX_ANCESTOR_DEPTH=10, isUsableImageElement + readImageSrc helpers); NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/find-underlying-image.test.ts` (10 unit tests; hand-built Element stubs because the node --test runner has no DOM); NEW `tests/playwright/extension/p23-amazon-overlay-image.spec.ts` (positive + negative case); NEW `tests/playwright/extension/amazon-overlay-image-product-page.html` (overlay-wrapped `<img>` fixture). **Real-Amazon browser verification DEFERRED to W#2 → main deploy session #13** per standard ship-then-deploy pattern (the load-bearing logic — empty-srcUrl content-script fallback path — is covered by the Playwright overlay fixture; the deploy session also confirms the widened-menu UX in real browser). **Director picked recommendation (A) per launch-prompt — recommended option honored, no scope deviation.** **1 INFORMATIONAL CORRECTIONS_LOG entry this session:** Playwright listener-attach race caught + fixed before commit (top-of-runOrchestrator vs. inside-async-flow placement; cleanly recovered via 1 source-code re-architecture; pattern worth recording for future content-script tests that exercise capture-phase event listeners). **Group A modified:** this ROADMAP.md (header + W#2 row Last Session 2026-05-14 prepended + (a.27) flipped ✅ DONE + new (a.28) RECOMMENDED-NEXT W#2 → main deploy session #13 + polish backlog P-23 status flipped to ✅ SHIPPED-AT-CODE-LEVEL); CHAT_REGISTRY.md; CORRECTIONS_LOG.md (header + 1 NEW INFORMATIONAL entry); DOCUMENT_MANIFEST.md; NEXT_SESSION.md (rewritten for (a.28) deploy session #13). **Group B modified:** COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md (new "Polish session #18 — P-23 SHIPPED at code level" section); COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md (§B in-flight refinement entry 2026-05-14). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** session stayed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` end-to-end; pull-rebase clean at session start (workflow-2 and origin/main at same commit post-deploy-#12 ff-merge — `git log` both directions empty); schema-change-in-flight stays "No"; W#1 row untouched. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 6 session tasks tracked + all closed; zero `DEFERRED:` items. **End-of-session commits:** ONE code + test + doc-batch commit (single commit per launch prompt's framing — code/tests/docs together for a code-level ship); push deferred to next session's deploy per Rule 9 deploy gate (workflow-2 push is not deploy-equivalent — only `main` push redeploys vklf.com).)

**Previously updated:** 2026-05-14 (**W#2 polish session #17 — P-20 highlight-flashing on real Amazon FIXED at code level (fingerprint short-circuit) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** Ninety-third Claude Code session — `session_2026-05-14_w2-p20-design-and-ship-fingerprint-short-circuit`. Closes (a.25) RECOMMENDED-NEXT — P-20 design + ship in the same session per director's scope pick. **Evidence-gathering pre-step (Rule 27 forced-picker Option A — manual DevTools trace):** director ran NEW `docs/p-20-trace-script.js` on a real Amazon PDP in Chrome Incognito (extension disabled). Results: **34 would-be `refresh()` rescans in 30s = 1.13/sec** under the orchestrator's 250ms trailing-edge throttle; 234 MutationRecord batches; 181 nodes added (6.0/sec); 1144 text chars added/sec; net node-count growth essentially flat (+1 over 30s — Amazon cycles content in/out continuously). Evidence ruled out shapes (a) longer-debounge (1.13/sec rate means 5s+ debounce needed to mask flashing, breaks +Add button responsiveness) + (c) per-platform DOM scoping (Amazon main area itself mutates; brittle to redesigns) + (d) IntersectionObserver (wrong-problem rewrite). **Shape (b) "remember-and-compare fingerprint" picked** (recommended per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`): addresses root cause (most external mutations cycle the same words back; fingerprint of pending highlight work stays constant; refresh short-circuits before strip-and-reapply); platform-agnostic; composes with existing P-14 mute-MO infrastructure. **Elegant property from reusing `shouldSkipSubtree` in the fingerprint walk:** the walk skips existing `<mark>` elements so the fingerprint reflects PENDING highlight work (unhighlighted matches only); steady state = `"0:5381"` and stays stable until new matchable text appears or existing marks get destroyed by external mutation — both correctly trigger re-apply. **Implementation (~80 LOC + tests):** 2 new exports in `highlight-terms.ts` (`hashFingerprintMatches` pure helper + `computeMatchableFingerprint` DOM-walking); `refresh()` short-circuits pre-mute when fingerprint unchanged + recomputes + stores post-apply fingerprint INSIDE mute window on uncancelled completion; `chrome.storage.onChanged` invalidates fingerprint before refresh (so term-list edits like color changes always re-apply). **Verification:** ext tsc clean; ext `npm test` **323/323 GREEN** (+9 new unit tests on `hashFingerprintMatches` — determinism, count-prefix shape, order-sensitivity, position-sensitivity, long-string collision resistance, large-N stability); Playwright extension project **29/29 GREEN** (was 25 = +4 new `P-20 EXTERNAL-MUTATION` specs across all 4 platforms — each injects 100ms `setInterval` adding/removing non-matchable DOM nodes and asserts `<mark>` mutation count stays at zero over 2.0s); **Previously updated:** May 12, 2026-h (**W#2 polish session #16 — Amazon P-15 FAIL controlled-reproduce on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` PASSED; session #9's observed FAIL retroactively attributed to test-setup ASIN mismatch.** Eighty-seventh Claude Code session. Launch-prompt task was the (a.20) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot from deploy session #9's follow-up doc batch: controlled-reproduce the Amazon P-15 FAIL with a known-good test setup (saved-row ASIN visible on the slug-variant page) to identify whether session #9's FAIL was a test-setup mismatch or a real code bug. **Setup confirmed with director:** deploy-#9 build loaded (`plos-extension-2026-05-12-g-w2-deploy-9.zip` running on test browser); saved Amazon row picked (ASIN `B0CTTF514L` from the 9 canonical-form `/dp/{ASIN}` rows confirmed by 2026-05-12-g DB inspection); DevTools accessible. **Walkthrough sequence:** (Phase 1) Director navigated canonical `https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0CTTF514L` → green "Already saved" overlay appeared in top-right; no PLOS-specific console errors (the visible errors were all Amazon ad-tracking pixels — `amazon-adsystem`, `googlesyndication`, `doubleclick` — blocked by ad-blocker, not PLOS). Amazon's internal redirect added `?th=1` (variant parameter) to the canonical URL; orchestrator's canonicalize handled correctly. (Phase 2) Director navigated constructed slug-variant `https://www.amazon.com/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1` → green overlay still appeared (orchestrator's `maybeShowDetailOverlay` canonicalize path works on slug-variants); `location.href` confirmation surfaced a **DevTools-context-selector pitfall** — director's first `location.href` evaluation returned `m.media-amazon.com/.../sash/ZjX5dSxC9UqUC39.html` (an iframe URL, not the top frame) because Amazon embeds many image/ad iframes and the Console's "JavaScript context" selector quietly pointed at one; redirected director to read the Chrome address bar instead → confirmed actual URL `https://www.amazon.com/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1?th=1`. (Phase 3) Director selected text on the slug-variant page → right-click → "Add to PLOS — Captured Text" → **URL dropdown PRE-SELECTED the saved row automatically** (option (a) per the forced-picker we'd prepared for the result). Post-right-click console showed only Amazon's `NoriLogger DSPClientStrategy` ad-telemetry warnings (noise, not PLOS-related). **Verdict: PASS.** P-15 fix works on real Amazon for slug-variant URLs with ASIN-match-controlled test setup. **Implication for session #9's observed FAIL:** the most likely cause is test-setup ASIN mismatch — hypothesis #1 from the 2026-05-12-g `dc3a314` commit body's plausible-alternatives list — director's session #9 navigation organically reached a slug-variant URL whose ASIN didn't match any of the 9 saved rows. The DB-inspection finding (all 9 canonical) ruled out P-21's asymmetric-canonicalize hypothesis; today's controlled repro with verified ASIN match confirms the remaining code path works. **No code change today.** **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** session stayed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` end-to-end; pull-rebase at session start was a clean fast-forward that absorbed main's 2 doc-only commits (`c1a47ae` + `dc3a314` from deploy session #9 + follow-up DB-inspection) into W#2 per CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-10-c entry #1's operational lesson (after a deploy session, the next W#2 session pulls main first); W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership; schema-change-in-flight stays "No"; no cross-workflow doc edits. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 8 session tasks tracked + completed; zero `DEFERRED:` items at any point. **One INFORMATIONAL CORRECTIONS_LOG entry this batch:** ASIN-match verification-spec discipline lesson — when verifying ASIN-match-dependent behavior on real Amazon, the test setup MUST explicitly construct a known-good slug-variant URL whose `/dp/{ASIN}` matches a saved row's stored ASIN (e.g. today's `/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1` constructed URL); session #9's verification let the director navigate organically, creating ambiguity between "code bug" and "test-setup mismatch". Future Amazon-FAIL-style verifications should include the controlled-repro pattern by default for any ASIN-match-dependent symptom. **Cross-references:** ROADMAP W#2 row Last Session prepended + (a.20) flipped to ✅ DONE PASS + W#2 polish backlog P-15 entry annotated with today's PASS-VERIFY footnote (status stays ✅ SHIPPED AT DEPLOY LEVEL — the Amazon-FAIL caveat now clarified as test-setup mismatch, not code bug) + new (a.21) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot for W#2 polish backlog continuation OR Waypoint #2 Extension build session 5; COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md new "P-15 PASS-VERIFY on real Amazon (controlled repro 2026-05-12-h)" section; CORRECTIONS_LOG.md header + 1 new INFORMATIONAL entry; CHAT_REGISTRY.md new top row + header; DOCUMENT_MANIFEST.md header + per-doc flags + this-session summary. **No code commits this session; one doc-batch commit on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` + push to origin/W#2 (absorbs `c1a47ae` + `dc3a314` from main onto origin/W#2 + today's doc batch in the same push).**)
