Launch-prompt task was the (a.17) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot from session #13's handoff: ship the P-14 fix on top of the session #12 + #13 regression spec — the fix shape (extend `startLiveHighlighting(projectId, options?)` with optional `muteMutationObserver?` callback; orchestrator forward-declares `let observer: MutationObserver | null = null;`, defines `muteMutationObserver` closure (`observer?.disconnect()` → await work → `observer?.takeRecords() + observer?.observe(document.body, {childList: true, subtree: true})`), passes it into `startLiveHighlighting` BEFORE constructing the actual MO) had been captured during session #12's code-reading and was named verbatim in the session #13 launch-prompt. Drift check produced + director picked Option A (ship P-14 fix) via Rule 14f forced-picker at session start — the most-thorough-and-reliable pick because the regression net was already in place and the fix lands on top of a deterministic verification surface. **What shipped this session:** code commit `45c9a15` touching three files +140/-89 — `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts` (+80/-25; new `StartLiveHighlightingOptions` interface; `refresh()` body wrapped in `muteMutationObserver` with no-op default for standalone use), `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (+34/-8; forward-declared `let observer` + `muteMutationObserver` closure defined BEFORE `await startLiveHighlighting(...)` so the closure binds late + `observer = new MutationObserver(...)` reassigns the same variable rather than re-declaring with `const` + cleanup uses optional chaining), `tests/playwright/extension/highlight-flashing.spec.ts` (+26/-63; removed all 3 source-level `test.fail(...)` calls covering 12 tests across 4 platforms × 3 sub-spec kinds; updated file-header + per-test comments to reflect post-fix state). **Verification scoreboard:** Playwright extension 17/17 ✓ ALL genuine green-pass (previously was 5 ✓ + 12 ✘ test.fail-expected; now all natural GREEN including 12 REGRESSION specs flipped + P-10 SPA-NAVIGATION still passing = zero collateral damage to P-10's MO-based URL-change detection); node-test 261/261 pass for the extension's `*.test.ts` glob (no regressions from the highlight-terms.ts + orchestrator.ts edits); ext tsc clean; root tsc clean; no src/ touched so root build + eslint unchanged from `72bb397` baseline. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** session stayed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` end-to-end; pull-rebase clean at session start; W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership; schema-change-in-flight stays "No"; no cross-workflow doc edits. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 3 session tasks tracked + completed; zero `DEFERRED:` items at any point. **Two slips self-caught mid-session, captured in CORRECTIONS_LOG as INFORMATIONAL** (process-improvement insights, not director-flagged): (i) Playwright-stale-build pitfall — first test run after my edits showed the 12 regression specs still ✘ because Playwright loads the extension from `extensions/competition-scraping/.output/chrome-mv3/` (pre-built directory), not from source — rebuilding via `npx wxt build` is mandatory between code edits and `npm run test:e2e:ext`; (ii) variable-shadowing in my first orchestrator edit — `let observer: MutationObserver | null = null;` at function scope COLLIDED with the existing `const observer = new MutationObserver(...)` at the same scope; esbuild accepted the duplicate but the late-binding closure read the outer null, masking the fix. Caught after the post-rebuild test run reproduced the same failure pattern; fixed by reassigning to the outer `let observer` instead of re-declaring with `const`. Both lessons informational for future extension-context Playwright sessions. **Cross-references:** W#2 row Last Session prepended + (a.17) flipped ✅ SHIPPED + new (a.18) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot + P-14 polish backlog list-item flipped ✅ SHIPPED at code level; COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md new "P-14 FIX SHIPPED" section appended above session #13's hardening section; CHAT_REGISTRY.md new top row + header; CORRECTIONS_LOG.md 2 new INFORMATIONAL entries (stale-build pitfall + variable-shadowing self-catch); DOCUMENT_MANIFEST.md timestamps; commit `45c9a15` (code); doc-batch commit hash TBD; push to W#2 branch is a separate end-of-session decision and does NOT deploy vklf.com regardless — only `main` push deploys.)

First Rule 14f forced-picker at session start named the four polish candidates + Session 5 + escape hatch (5 options total); director picked P-14 (the option I recommended — highest severity MEDIUM-HIGH + pre-existing since 2026-05-08-d P-5 ship + blocker to clean S4-B verification per `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` 2026-05-12 entry). Second Rule 27 forced-picker for verification approach offered (A) Playwright extension-context regression test, (B) Director manual walkthrough, (C) Hybrid, (D) escape hatch; my recommendation was (B) per Rule 27's documented scope exception for extension-context flows; director picked (A) for permanent regression coverage. SCOPE Read-It-Back (Rule 14a + Rule 11) surfaced the multi-session implication of (A): harness setup is its own session; regression spec authoring is the next; actual fix-and-verify is a third. Director picked "today = harness setup only; P-14 fix is later" via a third forced-picker. **What shipped this session:** new `tests/playwright/extension/` directory with three files — (a) `fixtures.ts` — Playwright test fixture extending `base.extend` with `chromium.launchPersistentContext('', { channel: 'chromium', args: ['--disable-extensions-except=<extensionDist>', '--load-extension=<extensionDist>'] })` + `serviceWorker` Worker-handle fixture (extracted via `context.serviceWorkers()` + `context.waitForEvent('serviceworker')` fallback) + `extensionId` fixture (regex-extracted from `chrome-extension://<id>/` URL pattern). Headless mode: uses Chromium's "new headless mode" via `channel: 'chromium'` (Playwright 1.60+ feature) — supports loading unpacked extensions without a display, which is the only viable path on a Codespace without a real display. (b) `product-page.html` — mock Amazon product page with predictable highlightable text ("cat scratching post for indoor cats" + multiple paragraphs + relative-link `<a>` elements). (c) `highlight-flashing.spec.ts` — TWO tests: SMOKE (chrome.storage seeding via serviceWorker.evaluate + route interception of `**://*.amazon.com/**` to serve product-page.html + navigate to fake `/dp/B0FAKE1234` URL + wait for `data-plos-cs-active=1` body attribute + assert mark count > 2 after initial highlight pass) + P-14 REGRESSION (observes `<mark>` element addition/removal count over a 1.5-second observation window after 500ms of initial-paint settling; expected mutationCount post-fix = 0; current pre-fix = many because the orchestrator's MutationObserver self-feedback loop strips + re-applies marks every ~250ms. `test.fail(true, '…')` annotation keeps the suite green pre-fix; when fix lands, Playwright reports "Expected to fail, but passed" as a FAILURE, signaling the next session to remove the annotation). **Playwright config extended:** new `extension` project (testDir: `./tests/playwright/extension`, no `use` block since `launchPersistentContext` bypasses default browser fixture); existing `chromium` project gets `testIgnore: 'extension/**'` to keep separation clean. **npm scripts added:** `test:e2e` now scopes to `--project=chromium` (preserves P-17 invocation); `test:e2e:ext` runs the extension project only; `test:e2e:all` runs both. **README extended:** new "extension" project description + cross-reference to the build prerequisite + the wxt-build pipe-blocking workaround per CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-10-f. **Sanity-check round-trip (P-17 discipline applied):** (i) with current code (P-14 bug present) → regression test's assertion FAILS as expected; `test.fail` flips to expected-pass; suite green; the harness's regression-detection capability is PROVEN to catch the live bug class. (ii) Temporarily commented out `observer.observe(document.body, ...)` in `orchestrator.ts:318` (the line that wires the MO — eliminates the loop trivially); rebuilt extension via `wxt build`; re-ran regression test; observed regression's assertion now PASSES (mutationCount = 0); `test.fail` flips to "Expected to fail, but passed" → suite RED — the exact future-fix-detection signal we want. (iii) Restored the line; rebuilt; suite green again. Round-trip confirms harness detects BOTH directions of the P-14 bug class. **Mid-session slips captured to CORRECTIONS_LOG (this batch):** (1) `import.meta.url` ESM-only syntax in `fixtures.ts` first attempt forced Playwright's TS loader to flip CJS→ESM output but kept a `require()` in its own wrapper — runtime error `ReferenceError: require is not defined in ES module scope`. Fix: substitute `process.cwd()`-relative path resolution (the test runner is always launched from repo root). Lesson: when authoring TS test files for Playwright 1.60's TS loader in a non-`"type": "module"` package, avoid ESM-only syntax like `import.meta.url` — use `process.cwd()` + relative path helpers instead. (2) A `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...` Bash invocation effectively reset the Bash-session's snapshot cwd to the extension dir; subsequent "npm run build" invocations from what I thought was repo root were actually running the EXTENSION's `wxt build` (which has a `build` script named the same way). Surfaced after ~4 minutes of head-scratching when the "next build" output never matched Next.js shape. Fix: explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub &&` prefix per the Bash tool's "use absolute paths, avoid cd" guidance. **Doc-drift correction (also this batch):** ROADMAP P-17 entry (2026-05-14) claimed root eslint baseline of "246e/3093w"; today's git-stash A/B comparison shows actual current baseline is 122e/1559w. Either eslint config + node_modules state has shifted since 2026-05-14 OR the P-17 entry's number was wrong as-of-2026-05-14. Either way the P-17 entry corrected this commit (header note about pre-2026-05-12 unverifiable but today's number authoritative). **Verification scoreboard:** ext tsc clean; ext tests 261/261 pass (baseline parity — no ext source changed this session); root tsc clean; root eslint 122e/1559w (exact pre-session parity, verified via `git stash` A/B comparison; zero added by this session's work because tests/** is already in `eslint.config.mjs` globalIgnores from P-17); root build clean (51 routes — exact baseline parity); Playwright chromium project 2/2 pass (P-17 unaffected); Playwright extension project 2/2 pass (smoke ✓ + regression test.fail-protected). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** session stayed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` end-to-end; this session's update appends the harness-landed note. **Doc updates this commit:** ROADMAP.md (this header + W#2 row Last Session prepended + (a.16) NEW author-P-14-regression-spec next-session + (a.17) NEW ship-P-14-fix-after-spec + P-14 polish backlog list-item annotated "harness shipped 2026-05-12-c; **Previously updated:** May 15, 2026 (**W#2 P-12 SHIPPED at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — extension 401-retry / silent-refresh analog to P-1.** Eightieth Claude Code session. Director's launch prompt named (a.14) explicitly; this session executed it. `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts` `authedFetch` refactored to a factory `makeAuthedFetch({ supabase, fetchFn })` mirroring P-1's `makeAuthFetch` in `src/lib/authFetch.ts`. On 401, the wrapper calls `supabase.auth.refreshSession()` once + retries with the new access token; on refresh failure, returns the original 401 unchanged so the caller's existing `PlosApiError(401)` error path is preserved. Production export wraps `fetch` in an arrow `(u, i) => fetch(u, i)` to preserve the window receiver — applying the P-17 lesson from the 2026-05-12 hotfix `08f10e5`. Pre-fetch no-token check still throws `PlosApiError(401, "Not signed in")`; `mapFetchTransportError` wrapping applied to BOTH the first attempt and the retry (TypeError → `PlosApiError(0, "Network unreachable")` semantics survive across the silent-refresh path). **Tests added:** `api-client.test.ts` extended with 9 new tests mirroring `src/lib/authFetch.test.ts`'s 7-test pattern plus 2 extension-specific cases (TypeError on first attempt; TypeError on retry after successful refresh). Test count 252 → 261. **Verification scoreboard:** 261/261 ext tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean; `wxt build` clean (6 artifacts at 676K — baseline parity). **Branch state at session start:** W#2 was 4 commits behind `origin/main` (P-17 + Rule 27 + platform-UI fix-pair + 2026-05-12-b deploy doc-batch); director picked merge over rebase via Rule 14f forced-picker; `git merge origin/main` FAST-FORWARDED cleanly (zero conflicts) because main's 2026-05-12-b deploy session had already absorbed W#2's prior 2026-05-12 doc-batch commit `23375bd`. **Previously updated:** May 12, 2026 (**W#2 verification session + vklf.com production hotfix.** Seventy-sixth Claude Code session. Launch-prompt task was the deferred verification queue (P3B-1..P3B-11 cross-device sign-in test deferred twice; P1V-1..P1V-3 silent token refresh tests; S4-A + S4-B + S4-C Module 2 text-capture walkthroughs). **HIGH-severity production hotfix shipped to main mid-session:** `src/lib/authFetch.ts:82` had a bare-`fetch` reference (`fetchFn: fetch`) introduced 2026-05-10-f with the P-1 silent token refresh; browsers detach `fetch` when invoked via a detached reference and throw `TypeError: Failed to execute 'fetch' on 'Window': Illegal invocation`. The bug broke every authenticated page load on vklf.com but was invisible during deploy sessions #4 + #5 because both ended within the 1-hour access-token freshness window (the silent refresh path that surfaces the bug was never exercised). Director hit the bug at S4-A-4 today. Fix: wrap `fetch` in arrow `(u, i) => fetch(u, i)` to preserve the browser's window receiver — commit `08f10e5` on main (7-line diff: 3-line comment + 4-line formatting). 7/7 existing `authFetch` tests still pass; tsc clean; build clean (51 routes — baseline parity); Vercel auto-redeploy `state: success` per `gh api deployments/4650327672/statuses`; director verified vklf.com loads cleanly post-deploy. **Test-coverage gap captured as P-17** — all 7 P-1 unit tests inject a fake `fetchFn`; none exercise the production export's bare-reference wiring. **Verification queue resumed after hotfix:** S4-A 12/12 PASS; S4-B 12/12 PASS with two pre-existing-bug workarounds (highlight flashing + URL pre-fill canonicalization captured as polish items); S4-C-1 PASS + S4-C-2 SKIPPED OPTIONAL; P1V-2 PASS (today's hotfix verified end-to-end — 3-request sequence 401 → refresh 200 → retry 200 observed exactly as designed); P1V-3 PASS (failure path forces auto-signout + login redirect; doc-drift on "red error" wording captured); P3B-1..P3B-10 PASS (canonical cross-device proof on laptop 2 with fresh-install chrome.storage.local); P3B-11 N/A. **Six DEFERRED polish items captured this session per Rule 14e + Rule 26 — destinations all in W#2 polish backlog this same doc-batch:** P-12 extension 401-retry / silent-refresh analog to P-1; P-13 autofocus on "+ Add new…" inline input (popup + overlay); P-14 highlight-terms applicator + MutationObserver self-feedback loop causes flashing (pre-existing 2026-05-08-d P-5 architecture issue; surfaced only via S4-B's selection gesture); P-15 `pickInitialUrl` missing `canonicalProductUrl` step on slug-variant URLs (session-4 bug — overlay fails to pre-select current page's URL when path has Amazon-style product slug); P-16 extension service worker "went to a bad state unexpectedly" surfaced on laptop 2 chrome://extensions (MV3 SW crash; auto-restarted by Chrome; future diagnosis needs SW's own DevTools); P-17 real-fetch integration test for `authFetch.ts` production export (test-coverage gap that allowed today's Illegal invocation to ship). **Two doc drifts captured:** S4-A-2 wording on Save-button-disabled state; P1V-3 wording on failure-path UI. Both noted for next-session VERIFICATION_BACKLOG cleanup. **Multi-workflow per Rule 25:** session pivoted mid-stream from W#2 (verification) to main (hotfix at commit `08f10e5`) and back to W#2 (verification + this end-of-session doc batch). ## NEW W#2 polish items added 2026-05-12 (P-12 through P-26; EXTENDED 2026-05-13 with P-23-AMAZON Amazon main-image right-click context-menu + P-24 saved-image-indicator + P-25 saved-text-haze + EXTENDED 2026-05-14 with P-26 below-fold capture limitation — director-captured UX/symmetry items). **NOTE: there is a numbering collision between two distinct P-23 entries** — main's (a.24-verify) session captured P-23 = "Amazon main-image right-click context-menu does NOT fire on product-listing page"; workflow-2's session-6 follow-up captured P-23 = "Saved-URL dropdown collapses URL when product name is present." Both entries are preserved verbatim below (the first listed below is the Amazon right-click one; the second is the dropdown one). Future polish session to resolve the collision by renumbering one of them.

Six polish items captured during today's verification session + hotfix. Full root-cause + fix details in `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-12 entry and in `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Outcome 2026-05-12 block. Brief listing here for the polish-backlog index:

- **P-12** Extension 401-retry / silent-refresh analog to P-1. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts authedFetch`. **Status:** ✅ SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL 2026-05-15-b on `main` via W#2 → main deploy session #7 (commits `414efe6` code + `7cfe5fc` docs; ff-merge `6525961..7cfe5fc` on main, pushed to origin/main, Vercel auto-redeployed; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-12-c-w2-deploy-7.zip` packaged with the P-12 refactor compiled into `background.js` + `chunks/popup-*.js` — `refreshSession` Supabase API call confirmed present in both bundles via grep). **Code-level ship was 2026-05-15** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` per polish session #11 (commit `414efe6` — refactor to factory `makeAuthedFetch({ supabase, fetchFn })` + 9 new unit tests; 261/261 ext tests pass). **Verification:** passive per (a.15) entry text — natural come-back >1 hour later on extension popup will surface whether the silent refresh works on a real expired access token; test-time coverage + architectural symmetry with deployed-and-verified P-1 web-side carries the deploy-time confidence. **Severity:** MEDIUM. **Why:** Extension popup's API client had no 401-retry pre-P-12; transient 401s surfaced as "broken popup" state until the popup was re-opened. P-1 shipped this pattern for vklf.com web (`src/lib/authFetch.ts`) on 2026-05-10-f; the extension's API path now has the same wrapper. **Fix shipped:** factory `makeAuthedFetch({ supabase, fetchFn })` returns the actual `authedFetch`; on 401, calls `supabase.auth.refreshSession()` once and retries with the new token; if refresh fails, returns original 401 unchanged. Production export wraps `fetch` in arrow per P-17 lesson.

- **P-13** Autofocus on "+ Add new…" inline category input (popup only — overlay surfaces already had `.focus()` calls; original capture mislisted overlay in "Where"). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:262` ONLY. The 3 content-script overlays were CORRECTED at session start 2026-05-19-c via Rule 3 (code wins over doc): `text-capture-form.ts:305` already had `newCategoryInput.focus()`, `image-capture-form.ts:368` same, `url-add-form.ts:244` uses `setTimeout(() => newInput.focus(), 0)` defer pattern. See CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c §Entry "ROADMAP P-13 'Where' drift" for full root-cause. **Status:** ✅ **DONE 2026-05-19-c** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` then deployed to main via deploy session #21 (commit `e217eb9`; Vercel auto-redeploy no-op since web bundle unchanged — fix lives in extension code only; user sees fix after sideloading deploy-21 zip). **Severity:** LOW. **Why:** Director-requested at S4-A-5 — when user picks "+ Add new…" from category dropdown, the inline name-input that appears should auto-receive focus so user can immediately start typing. Currently requires an extra click. **Fix shipped:** 1 LOC — `autoFocus` prop on the React `<input>` element at `CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:262` (conditionally rendered when `selectedCategory === ADD_NEW_CATEGORY_VALUE`; React focuses the element on mount which is exactly when the inline input appears). **Test coverage:** Option A (no new tests) per Rule 27 forced-picker at session start — declarative React `autoFocus` has no edge cases worth a regression test; director sideload manual verification at deploy time was sufficient. **Verification:** director sideloaded `plos-extension-2026-05-18-w2-deploy-21.zip` + opened popup + picked content category → "+ Add new…" → confirmed cursor lands in inline input automatically (no extra click needed). PASS first try.

- **P-14** Highlight-terms applicator + MutationObserver self-feedback loop causes flashing on all platforms. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` MutationObserver setup ~line 297 + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts refresh()`. **Status:** ✅ SHIPPED AT DEPLOY LEVEL 2026-05-12-f via W#2 → main deploy session #8 — merged onto `main` in commit `2fc6d15` (ff-only after rebase); pushed origin/main; Vercel auto-redeploy green; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-12-f-w2-deploy-8.zip` at repo root (181,920 bytes); director browser-verified on Walmart product page (canonical S4-B reproduction case from polish session #11) — highlights stable, no flashing, text selection survives. Previously ✅ SHIPPED AT CODE LEVEL 2026-05-12-e in commit `45c9a15` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #14 — (a.17) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot closed). The fix mutes the orchestrator MO around each highlight refresh via an optional `muteMutationObserver` callback plumbed through `startLiveHighlighting`. The 12 `test.fail`-annotated regression specs from session #13 commit `ba11027` (cross-platform across amazon/ebay/etsy/walmart × 3 sub-spec kinds — REGRESSION count + REGRESSION identity + SELECTION-STABILITY) flipped to genuine GREEN; annotations removed in the same commit. Playwright extension 17/17 ✓ genuine green-pass post-fix, including P-10 SPA-NAVIGATION guard (no collateral damage to P-10's MO-based URL-change detection). Deploy to vklf.com ✅ DONE 2026-05-12-f via W#2 → main deploy session #8 (commit `2fc6d15` on `main`).

Prior history: Playwright extension-context regression harness landed 2026-05-12-c on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (new `tests/playwright/extension/` + `test:e2e:ext` npm script). Regression spec coverage HARDENED 2026-05-12-d in commit `ba11027` (cross-platform across amazon/ebay/etsy/walmart + REGRESSION (count) tightened from 1.5s→2.0s window + NEW REGRESSION (identity) sub-spec + NEW SELECTION-STABILITY sub-spec + NEW P-10 SPA-NAVIGATION regression guard; 17/17 tests pass with 12 `test.fail`-annotated specs as expected-fail-as-pass — see (a.16) ✅ AUTHORED below). **Fix shape captured 2026-05-12-c during code-reading:** extend `startLiveHighlighting(projectId, options?)` to accept an optional `muteMutationObserver?: <T>(work) => Promise<T>` callback; wrap `refresh()` body in `muteMutationObserver` when provided (no-op default keeps existing tests + standalone use intact). Orchestrator declares `let observer: MutationObserver | null = null;` early, defines `muteMutationObserver` using a late-bound closure (`observer?.disconnect()` → await work → `observer.takeRecords() + observer.observe(document.body, { childList: true, subtree: true })`), passes it into `startLiveHighlighting` BEFORE creating the `observer`. Covers all three refresh paths for free: initial requestIdleCallback pass + MO-callback-driven refresh + chrome.storage.onChanged storage-driven refresh. **Severity:** MEDIUM-HIGH. **PRE-EXISTING since 2026-05-08-d P-5 ship** — only became visible-as-blocker today because S4-B's selection gesture surfaces it. **Why:** Orchestrator's MO observes `document.body` with `childList: true, subtree: true` on every mutation; debounced callback (RESCAN_DEBOUNCE_MS=250) calls `highlighter.refresh()` which strips all `<mark>` elements then re-applies them — both DOM mutations trigger MO again. Self-feedback loop. Host-page activity (lazy-loaded images, ads, hover effects) feeds the loop independently. Visible flashing at ~250ms cadence; selection on highlighted text destroyed every cycle. **Fix shape:** disconnect+reconnect MO around each refresh pass OR filter mutations whose target is a `<mark>` element OR set a "we're mutating" flag the MO callback short-circuits on. Must regression-test P-10 SPA navigation detection still works.

- **P-15** `pickInitialUrl` missing `platformModule.canonicalProductUrl(...)` step on slug-variant URLs. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/text-capture-form.ts:459` call site (the `captured-text-validation.ts pickInitialUrl` function itself was correct in isolation pre-P-15; the bug was the caller skipping canonicalization). **Status:** ✅ SHIPPED AT DEPLOY LEVEL 2026-05-12-g on `main` via W#2 → main deploy session #9 (merge commit `f6b1a98` on main; ff-only after rebase-then-ff per cheat-sheet (b)). Browser-verified PASS on Walmart/ebay/etsy at deploy-#9 (2026-05-12-g). **Initially flagged FAIL on real Amazon at deploy-#9 verification (2026-05-12-g)** — see P-21 below: hypothesis was today's P-15 fix canonicalizes pageUrl but NOT row.url; non-canonical saved Amazon rows (slug-variant, `gp/product/{ASIN}`, trailing slash) fail comparison. **⚠️ Resolved 2026-05-12-h:** controlled-reproduce with verified-ASIN-match test setup PASSED on real Amazon (W#2 polish session #16) — session #9's FAIL was a **test-setup ASIN mismatch, NOT a code bug**. 2026-05-12-g DB inspection had confirmed all 9 saved Amazon rows are canonical `/dp/{ASIN}` form (zero slug-variants), so the asymmetric-canonicalize hypothesis that originally motivated this FAIL note never applied to actual stored data. P-15 fix verified working on real Amazon under ASIN-match-controlled inputs (slug-variant `https://www.amazon.com/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1` → URL dropdown pre-selected the matching saved row). P-21 stays open as a future-defensive improvement covering user-pasted slug-variant URLs at the URL-add form, but no longer positioned as session-9-FAIL-fix. Original P-15 fix shipped 2026-05-12-f code-level in commit `74c7165` (W#2 polish session #15 — director picked Option A via Rule 14f forced-picker as the most-thorough-and-reliable polish-session pick post-P-14-fix). **Severity:** MEDIUM (user-visible UX miss; pre-fix workaround was one extra dropdown click). **Session-4 bug** (surfaced 2026-05-12 during S4-B verification queue post-Illegal-invocation hotfix). **Why:** When user right-clicks on a saved competitor page, overlay form's URL picker failed to auto-pre-select that page's saved URL because Amazon/etc. serve the same product under multiple paths (with and without product-name slug). The "already saved" banner path in `orchestrator.ts:280-282` already passed `location.href` through `platformModule.canonicalProductUrl(...)` BEFORE normalizing; the pre-fill path skipped that step. `normalizeUrlForRecognition` only strips `?…` (query), not path-level noise like `/Product-Name-Slug/` or `/ref=sr_1_3`, so the slug-variant pageUrl never matched its saved-as-`/dp/{ASIN}` row. **Fix shipped:** extended `pickInitialUrl` with an optional `canonicalize?: (href: string) => string | null` 3rd parameter that runs BEFORE normalization on the pageUrl (strictly additive — existing 2-arg callers preserved; null return falls back to raw pageUrl for non-product pages / non-product callers); at `text-capture-form.ts:459`, look up the active platform module via `getModuleByPlatform(props.platform)` and pass its `canonicalProductUrl` as the 3rd arg. **Tests:** 5 new cases in `captured-text-validation.test.ts` under a new `pickInitialUrl — canonicalize step (P-15)` describe block — bug-class repro (slug-variant matches via canonicalize), canonicalize-returns-null fallback, composition with query-stripping, backward-compat (omitting canonicalize preserves legacy behavior), defensive (canonicalize-always-returns-null still works). Ext `npm test` 266/266 pass (was 261; +5 new). Mirrors the working `orchestrator.ts:280-282` pattern.

- **P-16** Extension service worker "went to a bad state unexpectedly" — surfaced on laptop 2 during P3B walkthrough. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/background.ts` + NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/sw-error-logging.ts`. **Status:** ✅ **DONE 2026-05-19-d** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` then deployed to main via deploy session #25 (commit `07416d3`; Vercel auto-redeploy no-op since web bundle unchanged — fix lives in extension code only). **Severity:** MEDIUM. **Why (preserved):** Chrome's chrome://extensions on laptop 2 showed the extension's MV3 service worker had crashed at some point ("Service worker went to a bad state unexpectedly. Context is unknown. Stack Trace: :0 (anonymous function)"). Degenerate stack trace — Chrome had no meaningful source info because nothing caught the underlying error, so the SW lifecycle reported "bad state" with no actionable trace. Chrome auto-restarts the SW on next event so user-visible impact was intermittent. Suspected causes: (1) Supabase auto-refresh-token loop firing during P3B-9's WiFi-off period and throwing an unhandled promise rejection (MV3 SWs treat unhandled rejections more strictly than persistent backgrounds); (2) chrome.runtime.onMessage async paths reaching an uncaught error. **Code reality at session start (drift caught per Rule 3):** the onMessage handler at `background.ts:191-211` was ALREADY wrapped in a `.then/.catch` chain that produces a structured `{ ok: false, error }` response on rejection — so the "wrap async onMessage in try/catch with structured sendResponse" half of the original fix shape was already in place. The actually-load-bearing gap was the absence of any SW global error/unhandledrejection listener — failures Supabase or any other top-level promise produced silently bubbled out with no diagnostic trace. **Fix shipped:** NEW pure helper `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/sw-error-logging.ts` (~95 LOC) — `buildGlobalErrorPayload(err, context)` returns a structured `{ timestamp, context, name, message, stack }` record from any input shape (Error instance / non-Error string / null/undefined / plain object with name+message+stack / object without message). `logGlobalError(err, context, logger = defaultLogger)` wraps the builder + emits via the injected logger (production default: `console.error('[plos-cs-sw]', payload)`); injected-logger seam lets node:test assert payload shapes without touching console. Wiring in `background.ts`: two `self.addEventListener(...)` calls at the TOP of `defineBackground(() => { ... })` (BEFORE any other listener, so they're alive for the SW's full lifecycle including the auto-refresh loop that begins on supabase-module import); listeners do NOT `preventDefault` or re-throw — they just leave a SW DevTools trace. Also extended `errorToEnvelope()` to `logGlobalError(err, 'sw-handled-error')` before returning the envelope, so handled errors also leave a structured trace independent of the global-rejection listener. **Test coverage:** +6 new node:test cases in NEW `sw-error-logging.test.ts` (extension `npm test` 352 → 358); covers Error instance / non-Error string / null+undefined / object-with-name-message-stack / object-without-message (falls back to JSON.stringify with `[unserializable object]` safe-fallback if stringify throws) / `logGlobalError` invokes injected logger with built payload. **Director real-Chrome wiring test (Hybrid Option C via Rule 27 forced-picker at session start):** sideloaded fresh extension build (`plos-extension-2026-05-19-w2-p16-verification.zip`) on real Chrome → opened SW DevTools via chrome://extensions → "Inspect views: service worker" → console executed `Promise.reject(new Error('manual P-16 wiring test'))` → observed `[plos-cs-sw] { timestamp: '...', context: 'sw-unhandledrejection', name: 'Error', message: 'manual P-16 wiring test', stack: '...' }` line in console as expected. Wiring confirmed alive. Director's WiFi-off click-around produced no `[plos-cs-sw]` traces — benign outcome (no unhandled rejections fired during those specific clicks; Supabase swallowed errors internally OR the popup didn't make failing calls). The diagnostic instrumentation is in place for the next time the SW does crash; future SW DevTools captures will show a real stack instead of the prior degenerate `:0 (anonymous function)`. **Pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboards all GREEN at exact baselines (with extension test count bumped by +6):** root tsc clean / ext tsc clean / `npm run build` clean — 53 routes / src/lib node:test 527/527 / extension `npm test` **358/358** (was 352; +6 new sw-error-logging cases) / Playwright 75/75 (44 chromium + 31 extension). **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** ADDITIVE — pure defensive diagnostic instrumentation; zero schema / zero API / zero shared-types / zero downstream consumers; no behavior change in any non-error path. **Cross-references:** commit `07416d3` (the P-16 ship); deploy session #25 main push; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` "Deploy session #25" section for the deploy mechanics narrative + director wiring-test transcript.

- **P-17** Real-fetch integration test for `authFetch.ts` production export. **Where:** `src/lib/authFetch.ts` production export (line 81-88) + new `tests/playwright/` harness. **Status:** ✅ SHIPPED at code level 2026-05-14 (Seventy-ninth Claude Code session, on `main`). **Severity:** MEDIUM (process polish, not user-facing). **Why:** Test-coverage gap that allowed the 2026-05-12 `Illegal invocation` regression to ship. All 7 existing unit tests in `authFetch.test.ts` inject a fake `fetchFn` via `makeAuthFetch({ fetchFn: <fake> })`; none exercise the production export's `fetchFn: fetch` bare-reference wiring (which is the line that had the bug). Node's fetch is more lenient than browser fetch, so even an integration test in node wouldn't catch this. **Fix shape (locked at session start via Rule 14f forced-picker; director picked Option A):** Playwright suite in real Chromium. Catches the regression CLASS, not just the exact 2026-05-12 bug shape, because the real Chromium engine enforces the receiver-binding rule at the C++ level. **What was actually shipped:** `tests/playwright/authFetch-regression.spec.ts` with 2 tests (200 path + 401-refresh-retry path), both asserting `threwIllegalInvocation: false`. Supporting infra: tiny stub HTTP server, esbuild-bundled `src/lib/authFetch.ts` with `@supabase/supabase-js` aliased to a local fake (so the production-export wiring runs end-to-end without a real Supabase), Playwright config, npm scripts `test:e2e` + `test:e2e:install`. Sanity-checked in-session: reverting line 84-88 to bare `fetch` makes both Playwright tests fail LOUDLY with the expected error. Cross-references: commit `08f10e5` (the original hotfix); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-12 entry (the Lessons block); README §"Running the Playwright regression tests" (system-libs install workaround for fresh Codespaces).

- **P-18** Devcontainer postCreateCommand for Chromium system libraries. **Where:** new `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` + `.devcontainer/install-playwright-deps.sh` at the repo root `.devcontainer/` directory (created fresh this session — no existing `.devcontainer/` config to merge with). **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-20** — verification PASS captured this session by running `npm run test:e2e:all` in the rebuilt Codespace with ZERO manual lib install → **91/91 Playwright PASS in 1.8 min**. Conclusive proof the postCreateCommand fired during the Codespace rebuild + installed all Chromium libs from the canonical README lib list. The rebuild happened between 2026-05-21 (when the postCreateCommand was shipped + deployed) and 2026-05-20 (today); director performed the rebuild offline per the deferred-verification handoff captured in 2026-05-21's NEXT_SESSION.md. Verification completed but ALSO surfaced a HIGH-severity slip class — the rebuild silently wiped Claude's persistent operational memory directory; see CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-20 §Entry for the full slip narrative + the 4-layer protective architecture shipped this session (Rule 29 + 2 hooks + 2 scripts + .codespace-backup/) under P-41 below. Closes (a.51) + (a.52) RECOMMENDED-NEXT fully. **ORIGINAL DEPLOY ENTRY (preserved 2026-05-21):** SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL 2026-05-21 — W#2 → main deploy session #30; build commit `49c6403` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (2 new files +58 LOC config); ff-merged clean onto main (`bc60ee1..49c6403`); fresh zip `plos-extension-2026-05-21-w2-deploy-30.zip` (191,561 bytes — byte-identical to deploy-29's + deploy-28's zips, third consecutive byte-identical bundle since no extension source has changed since the P-23 ship on 2026-05-19-g). Pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboards both GREEN at exact 2026-05-20 baselines (identical pre + post confirms the config-only nature of the change). **Director real-Chrome verification SKIPPED + DEFERRED to next session per the Codespace-rebuild coupling discovery** — rebuilding the Codespace tears down the running container and kills Claude's terminal mid-session, so the only path that proves the postCreateCommand wiring actually fires (director's earlier Rule 27 Option A pick = fresh-Codespace-rebuild walkthrough) is structurally impossible to run in-session. Director picked "Wrap up this session now — verify after on your own time (recommended)" at the mid-session Rule 14f follow-up picker. Verification will happen between sessions: director triggers "Codespaces: Rebuild Container" via command palette → ~5-10 min rebuild → fresh terminal → `npm run test:e2e:all` → records PASS/FAIL → next Claude session collects the result as (a.52) RECOMMENDED-NEXT's first task. **Fix shape (refined per pre-coding Rule 14f forced-picker — director picked Option B = separate `.devcontainer/install-playwright-deps.sh` script invoked from `postCreateCommand` over Option A inline one-liner; recommended per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` — cleaner separation; ~30-40 LOC bash; idempotent + commented + re-runnable as standalone recovery tool from any Codespace):** `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` minimum-viable config (3 effective lines — name + postCreateCommand only; deliberately omits the `image` field so Codespaces uses its current default universal:linux image, preserving today's behavior exactly without risk of pinning to a stale image version); `.devcontainer/install-playwright-deps.sh` 54 LOC commented bash mode 100755 idempotent: disable yarn.list / apt-get install full canonical lib set / restore yarn.list; always restores yarn.list via EXIT trap even on failure; safe to re-run by hand from any Codespace as recovery tool. **Full canonical lib set installed by the script (matches README's list at lines 50-74; more libs than the launch-prompt teaser):** `libnss3 libatk1.0-0t64 libatk-bridge2.0-0t64 libcups2t64 libxkbcommon0 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxrandr2 libgbm1 libpango-1.0-0 libcairo2 libasound2t64 libatspi2.0-0t64 libxfixes3 libxshmfence1 libxss1 libxtst6 libnspr4 libdrm2 libwayland-client0 libwayland-egl1 libwayland-cursor0 libgdk-pixbuf-2.0-0 libnotify4 libdbus-1-3 libexpat1`. **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit (pre-classified before code):** Additive (safe). Config-only. No source code, no schema, no API, no test additions. Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact. Only affects fresh Codespace builds; existing Codespaces unaffected. **Pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboard targets met (identical pre + post):** tsc / ext tsc / `npm run build` 53 routes / src/lib node:test 536/536 / extension `npm test` 428/428 / Playwright 91/91 in 2.0 min pre-deploy + 2.1 min post-merge — ALL UNCHANGED vs. 2026-05-20 baseline. Closes (a.51) RECOMMENDED-NEXT (PARTIAL — shipped + deployed but verification pending); (a.52) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-18 verification re-entry — collect PASS/FAIL from director's Codespace-rebuild test, then close P-18 fully + fire §4 Step 1c forced-picker for the actual NEXT polish item (likely P-26 or P-27 design-only). **§4 Step 1c forced-picker for NEXT-after-P-18 explicitly NOT fired this session** — rationale: cleaner sequencing because the PASS-vs-FAIL outcome determines whether the next session's task is pick-next-polish (on PASS) or diagnose-and-fix (on FAIL); firing the picker now would presume PASS. **ORIGINAL CAPTURE (preserved 2026-05-14):** **Severity:** LOW (developer ergonomics only — Playwright tests have a documented manual workaround that resolves in ~2 minutes). **Why:** P-17's Playwright suite requires GTK/X11 system libs (libatk, libxfixes, libgbm, libnss3, libnspr4 etc.) that the default Codespaces Ubuntu 24.04 image doesn't ship. Codespaces system-package state does NOT persist across rebuilds, so every fresh Codespace hits `error while loading shared libraries: libatk-1.0.so.0` on first `npm run test:e2e`. Playwright's recommended `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium` doesn't work here because the Codespace's `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list` has an unverifiable GPG signature that blocks `apt update`. **Original-capture fix shape:** add a `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` with `postCreateCommand` (or `onCreateCommand`) that runs the manual workaround from `README.md` §"Running the Playwright regression tests" — temporarily disable yarn.list, install the lib set, restore yarn.list. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** searched docs (none), repo root (no `.devcontainer/` directory exists today). Cross-cutting platform-side polish (not strictly W#2) — captured under W#2 polish backlog for now alongside P-17 follow-on; future session can re-categorize as platform-wide if needed. Cross-references: CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-14 INFORMATIONAL entry (full diagnosis); README §"Running the Playwright regression tests".

- **P-19** Green status overlay auto-dismiss triggers one-time selection collapse (cross-platform). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/already-saved-overlay.ts` `destroy()` (the `setTimeout(() => handle.destroy(), AUTO_DISMISS_MS)` site + the close-button click handler — both flow through the same `destroy()` function) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` `maybeShowDetailOverlay()` overlay call-site. **Status:** ✅ DONE 2026-05-19-b via W#2 → main deploy session #20 (commit `7e111a8`, deployed to vklf.com — Vercel web bundle unchanged since fix lives in extension code only; user sees fix after sideloading a fresh extension build). **Fix shipped (Option a):** added optional `muteMutationObserver` wrapper prop to new `ShowAlreadySavedOverlayOptions` interface; `destroy()` wraps `banner.remove()` inside the wrapper; orchestrator passes existing closure-scope `muteMutationObserver` at the show-overlay call site (same wrapper already passed to `startLiveHighlighting` per P-14). Both dismiss paths inherit the mute discipline. **Test coverage:** +4 new node:test cases in `already-saved-overlay.test.ts` (was 348 → 352 ext tests pass) — explicit `destroy()` invokes wrapper / `hideAlreadySavedOverlay()` invokes wrapper / replacement-destroy invokes prior overlay's wrapper / default no-op when option omitted. Per Rule 27 default — node:test cases ARE the regression coverage; no new Playwright spec needed since the fix is mechanical mute-wiring already exercised at the production call site. **Sanity:** post-fix scoreboard all GREEN (tsc / ext tsc / `npm run build` 53 routes / src/lib node:test 527/527 / extension `npm test` 352/352 / Playwright 75/75). **Severity:** LOW-MEDIUM (one-time per page load; user selects again and it works; ALL 4 platforms affected). **Captured 2026-05-12-g** during W#2 → main deploy session #9 browser verification (director's verbatim observation: *"in the platforms where the highlighted words don't keep flickering, the selected text is unselected one time if the text is selected soon after the page loads. I think this happens when the green overlay goes away on its own."*). **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "green overlay", "overlay dismiss", "onload banner", "status banner" across COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md, COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md, ROADMAP.md returned zero hits — genuinely new. **Why:** The green status overlay's auto-removal IS a DOM mutation; orchestrator's MO observes `document.body` with `childList: true, subtree: true`; sees the overlay's element removal → 250ms debounce → `highlighter.refresh()` → strip-and-reapply → any active text selection on a stripped `<mark>` is cleared. Same mechanism as P-14 (the strip-and-reapply), different trigger (overlay-dismiss vs. self-feedback loop). The P-14 mute fix wraps the refresh body but doesn't prevent the MO trigger when the overlay disappears. **Fix shape:** ~5-10 LOC. Options: (a) detach the green overlay element via path that doesn't fire the body MO — e.g., move to a Shadow DOM root, OR detach-while-muted using the existing `muteMutationObserver` helper (cleanest — re-uses P-14 infrastructure); (b) skip refresh if no anchor or text-node region changed in the mutation batch (filter on mutation.addedNodes/removedNodes types — more invasive). Recommend (a) — symmetric with P-14's mute discipline.

- **P-23** Amazon main-image right-click context-menu does not fire on Amazon's product-listing page (NEW 2026-05-14 — captured during W#2 → main deploy-#10 cross-platform smoke verification on `main`). **Where:** Amazon platform-module at `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/platform-modules/amazon.ts` + context-menu wiring at `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/background.ts:82-104` using `chrome.contextMenus.create({ contexts: ['image'] })`. **Status:** ✅ **SHIPPED-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-14** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #18). **Fix shape — refined Option (A):** `background.ts` widens `contexts: ['image']` → `contexts: ['all']` for the image context-menu entry so the menu fires on any right-click target, not just elements Chrome recognizes as images; new content-script helper `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/find-underlying-image.ts` walks up from the right-click target (depth ≤ 10) and scans each ancestor's immediate descendants for an `<img>` with a non-empty `currentSrc`/`src`; the orchestrator attaches a capture-phase `contextmenu` listener at the TOP of `runOrchestrator` (BEFORE any awaits, so the cache is wired by the time `data-plos-cs-active=1` is observable — a listener-attach race caught during Playwright spec-development this session) that always updates `lastRightClickImageSrc` (set to the discovered src OR null on every right-click); when `open-image-capture-form` arrives with empty `srcUrl`, the handler falls back to the cache; if both are empty, the handler bails silently (replaces the prior background-side `if (!srcUrl) return;` guard with the content-script-side guard). Walmart/eBay/Etsy unaffected: direct-`<img>` right-clicks continue to populate `info.srcUrl` natively (Chrome's image-context detection still runs as part of `contexts: ['all']`) so the cache is never consulted on those platforms — zero behavior change. UX cost on all 4 platforms: "Add to PLOS — Image" now appears in the right-click menu on any element, not just images; bail-silently semantics keep this functionally correct, just slightly noisier. **Test coverage shipped this session:** +10 unit tests on the `findUnderlyingImage` helper (ext `npm test` 334/334 GREEN, was 323); +2 Playwright extension-context specs in NEW `tests/playwright/extension/p23-amazon-overlay-image.spec.ts` (positive: contextmenu on Amazon-style overlay-shield div → empty-srcUrl message → form opens with underlying `<img>`'s src; negative: contextmenu on plain text → empty-srcUrl message → form does NOT open / silent bail) with NEW `tests/playwright/extension/amazon-overlay-image-product-page.html` fixture mirroring Amazon's `.imgTagWrapper > img + #overlay-shield` zoom-overlay pattern (root Playwright extension project 31/31 GREEN, was 29). **Pre-ship verification scoreboard — all GREEN:** ext tsc clean; ext `npm test` 334/334 in 3.6s; root Playwright extension project 31/31 in 1.3 min; ext build clean in 1.42s; content.js 62,437 → 63,038 bytes (+601 bytes within the "few hundred bytes" target). **Real-Amazon verification DEFERRED to W#2 → main deploy session #13** per standard ship-then-deploy pattern (Rule 27 scope-exception logic mirrors P-20 deploy-session #12: real-Amazon-DOM-ness is part of the bug class but Playwright's overlay-wrapped fixture exercises the load-bearing logic — the empty-srcUrl content-script fallback path — at the regression-coverage level; deploy session also confirms browser-level UX of the widened "Add to PLOS — Image" menu now appearing on non-image right-clicks). Closes (a.27) RECOMMENDED-NEXT; new (a.28) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 → main deploy session #13 to bring P-23 to vklf.com. **ORIGINAL CAPTURE (preserved 2026-05-14):** **Severity:** MEDIUM — workflow degradation only; works via workaround (click main product image → Amazon opens it in a larger viewer pane → right-click that opened image → context-menu fires + full upload chain works). Affects ONLY Amazon — Walmart/eBay/Etsy all work on direct right-click on the main listing-page image. **Why:** Amazon's main `<img>` element on the product-listing page appears to be wrapped in zoom/overlay elements that intercept the `contextmenu` event before Chrome recognizes the underlying `<img>` as the target for the `contexts: ['image']` predicate used by `chrome.contextMenus.create`. In the larger viewer pane Amazon opens on click, the displayed image IS a clean `<img>` so the menu fires. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed before writing this entry:** grepped `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` polish items P-1..P-22 + `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md` §B refinements + extension `background.ts` `contexts: ['image']` wiring; no prior treatment of Amazon main-image right-click context-menu failure found. Related-but-distinct: P-20 covers Amazon flashing/selection-collapse (different DOM concern: text selection + highlight refresh, not right-click); P-14/P-15 are Amazon-related but unrelated to this gesture. **Candidate fixes (for future polish session — not for today):** (a) widen `chrome.contextMenus` `contexts` to `['all']` + detect element-type in the click handler (route to image-capture flow only when `info.mediaType === 'image'` or when `info.srcUrl` is set); (b) inject content-script right-click listener that walks DOM up from `event.target` to find an underlying `<img>` element and triggers the form open directly via `chrome.runtime.sendMessage`; (c) reuse the §5 floating "+ Add" button pattern from URL capture for the main product image — present a small "+" button on hover over the main image that opens the image-capture form directly (no right-click needed). Option (b) is most surgical; option (a) is the smallest code change but expands the menu surface across all right-clicks; option (c) is the most UX-consistent with the URL-capture pattern. Decision deferred to the future polish session. **Captured 2026-05-14** during the Amazon smoke (Phase 3.3) of the deploy-#10 cross-platform verification session via the new Hybrid verification approach (server-side DB checks via `scripts/inspect-w2-state.mjs` + director UI clicks). **Cross-references:** ROADMAP W#2 row Last Session 2026-05-14 entry (this session's full session header); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-14 INFORMATIONAL Rule 27 slip entry (the picker that surfaced the Hybrid verification approach which then surfaced this finding); COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md "Deploy session #10 — FULL VERIFY + cross-platform smoke COMPLETE 2026-05-14" section Amazon sub-section; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/platform-modules/amazon.ts` (the platform module that would receive any DOM-walking fix per option b); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/floating-add-button.ts` (the §5 floating-add-button infrastructure that would be reused for option c).

- **P-22** Playwright extension-context regression spec for Module 2 regular-image gesture (NEW 2026-05-13-b — captured at the Rule 27 forced-picker moment in deploy session #10 when director picked Option C Hybrid: quick manual smoke now + Playwright spec deferred to its own session). **Where:** spec at `tests/playwright/extension/image-capture.spec.ts` (NB renamed from the originally-proposed `image-capture-regular-gesture.spec.ts` — short name future-proofs against the region-screenshot gesture eventually sharing the file or having its own sibling); fixture HTML at `tests/playwright/extension/walmart-image-product-page.html`; alongside the existing `tests/playwright/extension/highlight-flashing.spec.ts` from P-14 session 12. **Status:** ✅ **SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL 2026-05-20** — W#2 → main deploy session #29 fully closes the P-22 multi-slice item by extending the existing single-platform amazon happy-path specs (P-23 / P-24 / P-25 added 2026-05-19) to all 4 platforms (amazon + ebay + etsy + walmart). Build commit `f4e90ec` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (3 files +512/-368 — test-files-only); ff-merged clean onto main (`8cdcbe5..f4e90ec`); fresh zip `plos-extension-2026-05-20-w2-deploy-29.zip` (191,561 bytes — byte-identical to deploy-28's zip, confirming the predicted byte-identical-bundle outcome when extension source is unchanged). Pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboards both GREEN: Playwright **91/91** (was 79; **+12** = P-23 +3 + P-24 +3 + P-25 +6 — note P-25 has 2 tests × 4 platforms = 8, was 2; launch prompt expected ~+9 but actual +12 because P-25 has 2 tests, attach + tear-down, both extended). Director real-Chrome verification SKIPPED per Rule 27 scope-exception (pure regression coverage extension; no user-visible behavior change; Playwright suite IS the verification). **Fix shape (deploy session #29 — covers slice 2 of the original P-22 capture):** each spec replaced its single-platform amazon `test()` body with a `PLATFORMS` array (4 entries: amazon + ebay + etsy + walmart with per-platform image CDN host, fixture URL, and hostname) + a `for (const pl of PLATFORMS)` loop wrapping each test, matching the canonical pattern used by `image-capture.spec.ts:124` + `highlight-flashing.spec.ts:97` + `region-screenshot.spec.ts`. The actual per-platform variation exercised: P-24's `SAVED_IMG_SRC` uses the platform's image CDN host (`m.media-amazon.com` vs `i.ebayimg.com` vs `i.etsystatic.com` vs `i5.walmartimages.com`); page-route glob intercepts the platform's host; `selectedPlatform` chrome.storage seed propagates through the orchestrator attach path's hostname-vs-selectedPlatform cross-check. **Drift caught at session-start (Rule 3 — code wins):** the spec authors' own comments in `p24-saved-image-indicator.spec.ts:5-7` + `p25-saved-text-haze.spec.ts:4-7` explicitly argued the content-script logic is platform-independent and per-platform recognition is already exercised by the existing 4-platform `image-capture.spec.ts` + `highlight-flashing.spec.ts` specs. Surfaced to director via Rule 14f scope picker BEFORE coding; director picked Option A (defensive full extension — all 3 specs × all 4 platforms) per the "all polish items before W#2 graduation" standing directive + cheap insurance against future per-platform divergence. **Slice 3 (error-path coverage) + Slice 4 (dedicated pre-select spec)** remain OPEN as future P-22 sessions if director ever prioritizes — current ship satisfies the "before W#2 graduation" cross-platform regression coverage goal; further error-path + pre-select slicing can fold into future P-NN sessions when natural. Closes (a.50) RECOMMENDED-NEXT; (a.51) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 polish P-18 devcontainer Chromium libs on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **HISTORICAL — SLICE 1 SHIPPED 2026-05-14** at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` in commit `d3dae97` — ONE platform (walmart) + ONE test (full happy path: navigate → wait for orchestrator attach → SW dispatches `open-image-capture-form` with retry-loop for the body-marker-vs-listener-registration race → assert form renders → fill all fields → Save → asserts Phase 1 `requestUpload` + Phase 2 PUT to mocked Supabase + Phase 3 `finalize` all fire with correct payload shapes → form closes); 7 Playwright `context.route` mocks (walmart HTML, walmart image-CDN with fake 1×1 JPEG bytes, GET listCompetitorUrls, GET listVocabularyEntries, POST requestUpload, PUT to fake-supabase, POST finalize) plus catch-all + listProjects mocks for orchestrator init; Supabase session seeded in chrome.storage.local at supabase-js default key shape so authedFetch's getSession proceeds. Test passes in 1.1s; full extension suite 18/18 green — zero regression. **Slices 2-4 remain OPEN as future P-22 sessions:** slice 2 = cross-platform parametrization (ebay + etsy + Amazon fixture pages + parametrized test like highlight-flashing.spec.ts's PLATFORMS array); slice 3 = error-path coverage (CDN-not-authorized 404 from image fetch, image-too-large 413, auth-failure 401, Phase 2 PUT 415 MIME mismatch, finalize 500); slice 4 = dedicated pre-select spec (P-15 canonicalize-before-normalize assertion class — currently NOT covered by any spec since the (a.23) happy-path explicitly `selectOption(FAKE_URL_ID)` to isolate Save flow from pre-select mechanics; pre-select is its own regression target with the slug-variant vs. canonical-form distinction). **Severity:** MEDIUM (regression coverage gap — the gesture ships without an automated regression spec; current 289/289 unit tests cover validation + 2-phase upload helpers + form-state, but the right-click context-menu → form-open → 2-phase upload flow is not exercised end-to-end against a browser DOM). **Why:** Module 2 image-capture is now live across 4 retailers; future code changes to background.ts context-menu wiring, api-bridge.ts submit wrapper, image-capture-form.ts rendering, or 2-phase upload contract could break the gesture and the unit suite would not catch regressions that involve real Chrome extension-context behavior (`chrome.contextMenus.onClicked`, content-script ↔ background messaging, image-CDN host_permission boundaries). **Proposed shape:** mirror P-14's extension-context spec shape (`fixtures.ts` + `product-page.html` stub with images representing each retailer's image markup variants + image-CDN response mocks for the 2-phase upload contract). Coverage targets: (a) right-click on `<img>` element triggers "Add to PLOS — Image" context-menu entry; (b) clicking the entry opens the form with image preview thumbnail + saved-URL picker populated correctly + image-category/Composition/Embedded-text/Tags fields rendered; (c) Save triggers `requestImageUpload` → mocked signed-URL Phase 1 → direct PUT to mocked Supabase URL Phase 2 → `finalizeImageUpload` Phase 3 with the expected CapturedImage payload shape; (d) error paths — image fetch fails, signed-URL request fails, PUT fails, finalize fails. **Preconditions for revisiting:** ideally after the natural follow-up next session of completing the manual deploy-#10 verification (Walmart form/save + cross-platform smoke), so the manual smoke surfaces any real-world gotchas the spec should encode. Could also be sequenced after region-screenshot session 6 so the same spec file covers both image-capture gestures (regular + region) — that's a session-sequencing pick the director makes when prioritizing. **Why deferred not shipped today:** director picked hybrid verification approach in deploy session #10 — quick manual smoke now (highest signal on "did this break?") + Playwright spec earns its own session for proper test-authoring. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/image-capture-form.ts` (the form source); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/background.ts` (context-menu wiring + 2-phase uploader); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts` (`requestImageUpload`, `putImageBytesToSignedUrl`, `finalizeImageUpload`, `fetchImageBytes`); `tests/playwright/extension/highlight-flashing.spec.ts` (P-14 reference shape).

- **P-20** P-14 fix doesn't generalize to real `amazon.com` — external-mutation retrigger keeps the strip-and-reapply loop active on highly-dynamic pages. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts refresh()` + the new `computeMatchableFingerprint` helper in the same file. **Status:** ✅ **SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL 2026-05-14** (deploy session #12 brought yesterday's code commit `198b1ad` to vklf.com via ff-merge `5e18e4b..8f11388`; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-14-w2-deploy-12.zip` 187,918 bytes; browser verification on real Amazon SKIPPED per Rule 27 forced-picker — director picked "trust Playwright 29/29" — sufficient regression coverage from the 4 new EXTERNAL-MUTATION specs injecting 10/sec non-matchable DOM churn across all 4 platforms, slightly above real Amazon's measured 6/sec; director-self-check real-world test list available in COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md "Deploy session #12" section for optional independent verification). **Earlier status:** ✅ SHIPPED-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-14 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #17 — design + ship in the same session per director's scope pick at the post-evidence forced-picker). **Browser-verification on real Amazon DEFERRED** to the next W#2 → main deploy session #12 — Playwright simulator catches the bug class via 10/sec injected mutations but real Amazon's continuously-mutating DOM is the ultimate test; standard W#2 ship-then-deploy pattern. **Severity:** HIGH (Amazon is the primary platform per W#2's Phase 1 throughput target; user-visible flashing + selection-collapse persisted post-P-14-deploy). **Captured 2026-05-12-g** during W#2 → main deploy session #9 browser verification (director's verbatim observation: *"In Amazon, the highlighted words keep flashing and selected text in the product listing page does not stay selected."*). **Evidence gathered 2026-05-14 via NEW `docs/p-20-trace-script.js`** — director ran the MutationObserver trace on a real Amazon PDP (`/dp/B0CTTF514L`) in Chrome Incognito for 30 seconds: 234 MutationRecord batches, 181 nodes added (6.0/sec), 1144 text chars added/sec, **34 would-be `refresh()` rescans = 1.13/sec** under the orchestrator's 250ms throttle. **Root cause CONFIRMED:** Amazon's DOM cycles content in/out continuously (carousels, recommendation tiles, lazy reviews); the MO fires on every cycle; the throttle caps the rate but doesn't eliminate; net flash rate = 1.13/sec exactly matching the user's reported cadence. **Fix shipped — shape (b) remember-and-compare fingerprint** (recommended via Rule 14f forced-picker per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`; alternatives (a)/(c)/(d) evaluated + rejected — see `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-14 entry for full design narrative + per-shape verdict table). **Implementation:** new exported pure helper `hashFingerprintMatches` + new exported DOM-walking `computeMatchableFingerprint` (TreeWalker honoring same `shouldSkipSubtree` rules as `applyHighlightsTo`, so the walk skips existing `<mark>` elements → fingerprint reflects PENDING highlight work, not all matches on page); `lastFingerprint` state inside `startLiveHighlighting`; `refresh()` short-circuits pre-mute when fingerprint unchanged, recomputes + stores post-apply fingerprint INSIDE mute window on uncancelled completion; `chrome.storage.onChanged` invalidates fingerprint before refresh so term-list edits always re-apply. Steady state fingerprint = `"0:5381"` (zero matches, init hash). **Test coverage:** +9 unit tests on `hashFingerprintMatches` (323/323 GREEN, was 314); +4 Playwright `P-20 EXTERNAL-MUTATION` specs across all 4 platforms (29/29 GREEN, was 25) — each injects a 100ms `setInterval` adding/removing non-matchable DOM nodes (~10/sec — slightly above real-Amazon's measured 6/sec to harden the assertion) and asserts `<mark>` mutation count stays at zero over 2.0s. **Closes (a.25) RECOMMENDED-NEXT; new (a.26) ✅ DONE 2026-05-14 — W#2 → main deploy session #12 — P-20 fingerprint short-circuit DEPLOYED to vklf.com (ff-merge `5e18e4b..8f11388` to main; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-14-w2-deploy-12.zip` 187,918 bytes at repo root; pre-deploy scoreboard 323/323 ext tests + 29/29 Playwright extension project + ext build clean; browser verification on real Amazon SKIPPED per Rule 27 forced-picker — director picked "trust Playwright 29/29" — P-20 polish backlog entry flips ✅ SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL on Playwright simulator coverage alone; director-self-check real-world test list provided in deploy session #12 doc batch for optional independent verification). (a.27) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 polish session #18 — ship P-23 Amazon main-image right-click context-menu fix (MEDIUM severity Amazon-specific functional issue; well-scoped single-session ship; candidate fixes already enumerated in deploy-#10 doc batch — widen `chrome.contextMenus` contexts to `['all']` + check element-type in handler / inject content-script right-click listener that walks DOM up to find an underlying `<img>` / reuse §5 floating "+ Add" pattern; pick the lowest-risk fix that compiles cleanly and verifies on real Amazon via post-ship deploy session). Branch: `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.**

  - **(a.26) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 → main deploy session #12 — P-20 fingerprint short-circuit DEPLOYED to vklf.com + real-Amazon verification.** Standard cheat-sheet (b) flow: rebase workflow-2-competition-scraping (today's 2 commits: P-20 code + this doc-batch) onto current origin/main HEAD; resolve any doc-batch conflicts manually (parallel-branch divergence pattern from prior sessions applies); ff-merge to main; push origin/main; Vercel auto-redeploy no-op (zero `src/` Next.js changes — extension-only). Package fresh `plos-extension-2026-05-15-w2-deploy-12.zip` (or whatever the date is at deploy time). **Browser verification on real Amazon (Rule 27 scope exception — the bug class is "real-Amazon-DOM-ness is the point"):** sideload the new zip → set Project + Platform = Amazon + at least one highlight term that appears on Amazon PDPs → navigate to any Amazon product detail page → wait 10–15 seconds for the page to settle through its mutation cycles → confirm (1) highlighted words do NOT keep flashing + (2) text selection over highlighted words does NOT collapse. Compare with one of the OTHER three platforms (Walmart/eBay/Etsy) to confirm zero regression on the stable-DOM platforms. If real-Amazon verification PASSES: P-20 flips ✅ SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL and closes; next (a.27) RECOMMENDED-NEXT opens for the next priority polish item (likely P-23 Amazon main-image right-click context-menu or P-21 pickInitialUrl asymmetric canonicalize per existing W#2 polish backlog).

- **P-23** Saved-URL dropdown collapses URL when product name is present — both should show side-by-side (product name LEFT, URL RIGHT). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/text-capture-form.ts` saved-URL `<option>` label construction + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/image-capture-form.ts` saved-URL `<option>` label construction + (if applicable) `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` ProjectPicker-adjacent saved-URL picker label. **Status:** ✅ **SHIPPED-AT-DEPLOY-LEVEL 2026-05-19-g** — W#2 → main deploy session #28; build commit `5cb2419` (6 files +461/-12); ff-merged `c1f1cd7..5cb2419`; fresh zip `plos-extension-2026-05-19-w2-deploy-28.zip` (191,561 bytes); director real-Chrome verification PASS on all 3 forms (popup paste form + right-click "Add to PLOS — Captured Text" + right-click "Add to PLOS — Image"). **Fix shape (refined per pre-coding Rule 14f forced-picker, director picked recommended Option A em-dash separator + Hybrid test coverage):** factor the label logic into a new pure helper `buildSavedUrlOptionLabel(row): string` at `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/saved-url-option-label.ts` shared by all three caller sites; helper returns `${productName.trim()} — ${url-truncated-at-60}` when productName non-empty (em-dash separator inside `<option>` text — works in native Chrome `<select>` rendering without CSS tricks; truncation budget tightened from 80 → 60 chars when productName also present so the option text stays scannable), and falls back to the pre-P-23 URL-only path (80/77-char truncation unchanged) when productName is null/undefined/empty/whitespace. **Drift caught at session-start (Rule 3 — code wins):** launch prompt named `url-add-form.ts` as second fix site but that file has no saved-URL `<select>` (its `opt.textContent = entry.value` lines are inside the content-category vocab picker — P-36 ship); actual sites with the collapse pattern were three: `text-capture-form.ts:474-476` (named in prompt; line 474 not 450), `image-capture-form.ts:534-536` (NOT named — identical parallel sibling), `CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:255-258` (NOT named — same pattern as JSX). Director picked all-3-sites scope via Rule 14f at session start. **Test coverage (Hybrid per Rule 27):** +12 node:test cases on the pure helper (productName present/absent/null/undefined/whitespace × truncation boundaries at 60/61/80/81 chars + pre-P-23 URL-only regression test); +1 Playwright extension-context spec at `tests/playwright/extension/p23-saved-url-dropdown-label.spec.ts` asserting the rendered `<option>` text for two seeded saved URLs (one with productName, one without) in the text-capture form. **Pre-deploy scoreboard targets met:** tsc / ext tsc clean / `npm run build` 53 routes / src/lib node:test 536/536 / extension `npm test` 428/428 (was 416; +12) / Playwright 79/79 (was 78; +1). Closes (a.49) RECOMMENDED-NEXT; (a.50) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 polish P-22 Playwright cross-platform slices 2-4 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **ORIGINAL CAPTURE (preserved 2026-05-13):** **Severity:** LOW-MEDIUM (UX clarity — current behavior loses the URL identification when product name has been entered; users may not realize which URL they're attaching captures to). **Captured 2026-05-13** by director after region-screenshot session 6 ship. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "url.*picker|saved.url.*dropdown|product.*name.*url|productName.*url|pickInitialUrl" across `docs/ROADMAP.md` + `docs/COMPETITION_SCRAPING_*.md` returned zero hits about combining product name + URL in dropdown labels — genuinely new. The existing `pickInitialUrl` work (P-15, P-21) covers URL canonicalization for matching; the option-label construction is a separate concern. **Why:** Current `<option>` text shows `productName` when present and falls back to `url` only when productName is missing. Director wants both visible always so the user can confirm which URL+product they're targeting. **Fix shape:** ~5-10 LOC per call-site (3 sites) — change `option.textContent = row.productName || row.url` to a two-column shape like `option.textContent = ${row.productName ?? '(no name)'} — ${row.url}` OR use HTML `<option>` with explicit padding/styling. Plus 2-4 new test cases in `captured-text-validation.test.ts` / `captured-image-validation.test.ts` if the formatter is extracted to a helper. Real-browser verification at next deploy session.

- **P-24** Saved-image indicator on the page — no way for the user to tell which images on the current page have already been saved. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (scan/match logic for image elements parallel to the existing anchor-element saved-URL scan at `attachAlreadySavedIcon`) + new helper module similar to `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/already-saved-icon.ts` but for `<img>` elements + API extension to list saved CapturedImages by `competitorUrlId` + canonical-URL-match (server-side OR client-side derived from already-loaded CompetitorUrl rows). **Status:** ✅ DONE 2026-05-19-e — SHIPPED + DEPLOYED + VERIFIED on vklf.com via W#2 → main deploy session #26 (commit `6e7ffa5`). Final fix shape diverged from original capture: per Rule 3 code-vs-doc drift caught at session start — the original premise that `CapturedImage` already had an `original_src_url` field was wrong (no such column in schema; no field on the TypeScript interface; no value flowing through the save path). Director picked Option 1 in the §start-of-session-drift-check picker: add new nullable `originalSrcUrl` column to `CapturedImage`, persist forward on new captures, accept that pre-existing rows show no indicator until re-captured. ~1067 LOC across schema + shared-types + 2 API routes + extension api-client + api-bridge + messaging type-guard + background.ts srcUrl threading + new `already-saved-image-icon.ts` helper + orchestrator extension (urlId Map + scanImages + scroll/resize listeners) + 10 new node:test cases + 1 new Playwright extension-context spec slice. Scoreboards GREEN at +4 src/lib / +10 ext / +1 Playwright over baseline. **Severity:** MEDIUM (UX gap — user can save the same image multiple times without realizing; symmetric with the URL "already saved" green badge + green checkmark icon shipped sessions 1+3). **Captured 2026-05-13** by director after region-screenshot session 6 ship. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "image.saved|saved.image|already.saved.*image" across all W#2 + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_* docs + read of `already-saved-icon.ts` + `already-saved-overlay.ts` confirms: existing `already-saved-icon.ts` (the green ✓ checkmark) is URL-anchor-specific (`data-plos-cs-icon-for` attribute attached to `<a>` elements); existing `already-saved-overlay.ts` (the green badge) is URL-detail-page-specific. Neither covers images — genuinely new feature. **Why:** Symmetric UX with URL recognition. Users naturally expect "already saved" indicators across all capture types. Also: prevents accidental duplicate uploads + saves storage. **Fix shape:** (a) new helper module `already-saved-image-icon.ts` (~80 LOC mirror of `already-saved-icon.ts`); (b) orchestrator scans `<img>` elements with `data-plos-cs-image-handled` flag (mirror of `ATTR_LINK_HANDLED`); (c) for each image, look up its src against the list of saved CapturedImage rows for the current CompetitorUrl; (d) match logic considers both exact-src match AND saved-image's `original_src_url` field (sessions store both); (e) on match, attach a small green ✓ icon overlay positioned at image's top-right corner (z-index above page content); (f) tear down on URL-change / cleanup paths analogous to URL icon teardown. Likely needs new `listCapturedImagesByUrl` API endpoint + bridge helper. Estimated ~300-500 LOC + new Playwright spec slice. Server-side: confirm `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/images` GET endpoint exists or add it.

new `video-category` vocabulary type (parallel to `content-category` + `image-category`); new Supabase Storage bucket for video bytes (NEW — separate from the existing 5MB-capped image bucket per `STACK_DECISIONS.md:144` constraint);
