Current state: app-layer enforcement only per §A.11; defense-in-depth bucket-level cap is missing until director performs the dashboard step. Next stage = **Build #2** (API routes scaffolding under `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/videos/` + extension/shared-types extensions for the route request/response shapes — `RequestVideoUploadRequest` / `RequestVideoUploadResponse` / `FinalizeVideoUploadRequest` / `FinalizeVideoUploadResponse` / `ListCapturedVideosResponse` — per design doc §A.2 implementation arc table row #3). **All 7 open design questions captured at original P-27 capture time RESOLVED via Rule 14f forced-pickers in design session — all 11 picker outcomes logged in design doc §A.0 audit trail:** (1) Doc location pick (Rule 14f at session start) = Option A — new top-level `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` (vs. §B append in `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md` vs. both). (2) Q3 v1 scope pick = Option B — Symmetric v1 (ship full parity with text/image; defer only the obvious extensions). (3) Q6+Q7 per-platform `<video>` detection pick = Option A — platform-agnostic DOM-walking detector + 10-entry video-embed-hostname allowlist + iterate on per-platform quirks as future polish items (resolved original Q5 cross-platform `<video>` detection question). (4) Q8 schema pick = Option A — new `CapturedVideo` table + required `sourceType` enum discriminator + nullable storage/byte-derived fields (resolved original Q3 schema additions question). (5) Q9 vocab UX pick = Option C — inline "+ Add new category" affordance in capture form + 0 seeded entries (director clarification question prompted a reframing of the options BEFORE the pick). (6) Q10a bucket size cap pick = 100 MB per-file (recommended; vs. 50 / 500) — resolved original Q1 Supabase bucket strategy size-cap question. (7) Q10b thumbnail extraction pick = Option A — client-side `<canvas>` frame-grab (vs. server-side FFmpeg / source-poster-only) — resolved original Q2 thumbnail extraction question. (8) Q11 embed save URL validation pick = Option A — URL-pattern regex only, no server-side fetch — resolved original Q4 YouTube/Vimeo handling question. (9) Q12 size cap enforcement pick = Option A — two-layer client + server (tamper-resistant). (10) Q13 thumbnail failure fallback pick = Option A — NULL `thumbnailStoragePath` + generic ▶️ icon placeholder; save never blocked. (11) Q14 test coverage pick = Option A — Hybrid per Rule 27 (node:test on pure helpers + Playwright extension-context spec on amazon happy path; cross-platform Playwright deferred to P-22-style follow-up). **Three Living Questions (Rule 7) answers + DATA_CATALOG §7 reciprocal output declaration both captured per design doc §A.15 + §A.16 — folded into `docs/DATA_CATALOG.md` §7.2.2 W#2 row this session.** **§A.18 deferred symmetry-restore item was SUPERSEDED at end-of-session by the Rule 24 catch:** the inline-add UX already exists for text + image + URL surfaces per P-13 (text-capture-form.ts:305 + image-capture-form.ts:368 + url-add-form.ts:244 + CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:262) — captured as ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-20-b; no cross-cutting symmetry-restore polish item needed; task #10 closed as SUPERSEDED. **Severity:** MEDIUM-HIGH — substantive new functional surface; director-requested expansion item NOT a typical small polish item. **Pre-graduation gating:** YES — per director's standing directive *"All these things should ship before Workflow #2 is deemed complete"* (captured 2026-05-19-d-2 + reinforced today via §4 Step 1c picker option A), P-27 joins P-22 + P-18 + P-26 as the 4 remaining items that must ship before W#2 graduation. **Captured 2026-05-19-g** by director after P-23 ship session via post-handoff addendum directive. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (zero prior treatment found):** grepped `\bvideo[s]?\b` + `capturedVideo` + `video[-_]categor` across ROADMAP + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_STACK_DECISIONS + PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE + PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS + DATA_CATALOG + prisma/schema.prisma + src/lib/shared-types — zero hits on "video capture as a W#2 feature." Two relevant CONSTRAINTS surfaced and must be addressed in the design session: (a) `STACK_DECISIONS.md:144` — the existing 5MB image-upload cap was chosen *specifically* to prevent video uploads (verbatim: *"5 MB cap covers the heavy tail without permitting accidental video uploads or oversized PNGs"*) — this constraint needs a deliberate revisit; the design session will pick the new video-bucket size cap (candidates: 100 MB / 500 MB / per-project budget); (b) `PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS.md:427` — tech-debt note already flagged the need for a workflow-deliverable-storage bucket strategy: *"Workflow-deliverable storage (videos, design files, etc.) likely needs a dedicated bucket strategy — possibly private + signed URLs (already on the tech-debt list)."* — P-27's bucket design satisfies + closes (or scopes) that tech-debt item. **Why:** Director's standing intent for W#2 is capture parity across all three competitor-asset media types (text + image + video) so the platform's downstream workflows can compose competitor-analysis output spanning all three. Without P-27, W#2's outputs are text+image-only, and any future downstream workflow that needs video references for competitor-analysis prompts (likely W#3 transformation prompts; W#5 conversion funnel narratives may also reference video proof points) would either have to consume videos from outside PLOS or block on a future expansion item. Director picked the most-thorough path on all three pre-capture scope questions: **(Q1 Source) URL reference + uploaded video bytes — BOTH stored** (vs. URL-only lighter option); **(Q2 Gestures) Full symmetry with text/image — all 3 paths** (right-click on `<video>` + right-click on embed + popup paste form); **(Q3 Graduation timing) Pre-graduation polish item — same bucket as P-22/P-18/P-26**. **Fix shape (high-level — full design interview lives in a future dedicated design session before any code starts):** (a) new `CapturedVideo` table — fields parallel to `CapturedImage` (id + clientId + competitorUrlId + videoCategory + storagePath + storageBucket + originalSrcUrl + composition + embeddedText + tags + sourceType + fileSize + mimeType + duration_seconds (NEW relative to CapturedImage) + width + height + sortOrder + source + addedBy + addedAt + updatedAt + thumbnailUrl + fullSizeUrl); (b) new shared types + API routes + extension UI mirroring P-23/P-24/P-25's existing patterns; (c) new Supabase Storage bucket (private + signed URLs per `PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS.md:427`) with a deliberate size cap chosen in design session; (d) thumbnail extraction (TBD client-side `<video>`+`<canvas>` frame-grab vs. server-side FFmpeg vs. relying on source URL's poster attribute / YouTube auto-thumb API); (e) saved-video on-page indicator overlay (green ✓ icon mirroring P-24's saved-image icon); (f) URL detail page renderer (inline embedded `<iframe>` for YouTube/Vimeo source URLs + inline `<video>` for direct MP4/WebM source URLs + click-to-play overlay on thumbnails); (g) cross-platform Playwright spec coverage (single-platform amazon happy path first per P-23/P-24/P-25 pattern; cross-platform extension to ebay + etsy + walmart deferred to a P-22-style follow-up). **Test coverage approach (Rule 27 Hybrid likely — to be confirmed in design session):** pure-helper node:test cases on any extracted helpers (e.g., a video-URL-normalizer parallel to `saved-url-option-label.ts`) + Playwright extension-context spec on the right-click happy path single-platform amazon first. **Open design questions for the dedicated design session (no code starts until these are resolved):** (1) New Supabase bucket strategy — private vs. public; signed URLs; size cap; MIME-type allowlist (`video/mp4` + `video/webm` + `video/quicktime`?). (2) Thumbnail extraction — server-side FFmpeg (heavy; new Vercel function) vs. client-side canvas frame-grab (lighter; relies on extension capturing thumb pre-upload) vs. external source-URL poster / YouTube auto-thumb API. (3) Schema additions — new `CapturedVideo` table (cleaner; chosen) vs. polymorphic `CapturedAsset` with `mediaType` discriminator (DRY-er but less type-safe; rejected for symmetry with existing models). (4) YouTube/Vimeo handling — store URL only for embedded platforms (likely; ToS + technical constraints argue against attempting to download bytes from YouTube) vs. attempt download. (5) Cross-platform `<video>` detection — empirical investigation needed in design session for which of the 7 supported platforms host inline `<video>` elements vs. embeds vs. neither. (6) Three Living Questions (Rule 7) answers to capture in DATA_CATALOG: (i) Upstream data needed = Project + Platform + CompetitorUrl + new video-category vocabulary; (ii) Read-only by downstream W#3+; (iii) N/A. (7) DATA_CATALOG §7 Cross-Tool Data Flow Map — new "captured videos" output entry to W#2's row, per Rule 18 reciprocal output declaration; downstream consumers initially "TBD." **Estimated scope:** ~6-12 sessions broken approximately into: Session 1 dedicated design interview + DESIGN §B entry + schema design (no code); Session 2 schema migration via `npx prisma db push` + new bucket setup + API routes scaffolding; Sessions 3-5 extension content-script (right-click `<video>` form + embed fallback form + popup paste form + saved-video indicator overlay + URL detail page renderer); Session 6 single-platform amazon Playwright spec; optional Sessions 7+ thumbnail extraction polish + size-cap tuning + YouTube-embed special handling + cross-platform Playwright extension via P-22 pattern. W#2 graduation estimate now extends from ~3-6 more polish sessions (pre-P-27 capture) → ~9-18 more sessions (post-P-27 capture). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** would flip to "Yes" when this work starts (new `CapturedVideo` table + new vocabulary-type enum value for `video-category`). **Cross-references at capture time:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/image-capture-form.ts` (sibling pattern to mirror for right-click video form); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/find-underlying-image.ts` (sibling DOM-walking helper to mirror for the embed-fallback path); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` (sibling pattern to mirror for popup paste video form); `prisma/schema.prisma` `CapturedImage` model (sibling table to mirror for `CapturedVideo`); `STACK_DECISIONS.md:144` (5MB image cap reasoning that needs revisit for new video bucket); `PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS.md:427` (already-flagged tech-debt note about workflow-deliverable storage strategy that P-27 will close/scope); `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (director's standing preference for most-thorough/reliable path; informed the source-storage + gesture + graduation-timing picks today); COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN §B 2026-05-19-g-2 entry (the mid-build directive Read-It-Back capturing today's scope-add directive verbatim).

- **P-26** Below-fold capture limitation — current region-screenshot design captures only the visible viewport; tall content requires "two halves" workaround (NEW 2026-05-14 — captured during W#2 → main deploy session #11 verification on `main` after director's mid-walkthrough question about why the selection rectangle can't be dragged below the page fold). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/background.ts` `handleCaptureVisibleTab` handler (the single-viewport captureVisibleTab call) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/region-screenshot-overlay.ts` (the overlay clamp-to-viewport behavior) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/region-screenshot.ts` pure-logic helpers (the crop-bound math that assumes single-viewport input). **Status:** OPEN — captured for future re-evaluation only. **Severity:** LOW (UX gap; documented workaround works fine — two captures + two metadata-tagged rows in URL detail page; current Phase 1 design is the deliberate choice per `STACK_DECISIONS §4`). **Why captured (not "fix this now"):** Director picked "keep current design + capture for future re-evaluation" at the mid-verification scope picker — the trade-offs of full-page scroll-capture make it a non-trivial future build, not a quick polish. **Why the limitation exists:** Chrome's `chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab` API is viewport-only by design — there's no extension API that lets an extension reach below-the-fold pixels in a single shot. **Alternative design candidate ("full-page scroll-capture"):** extension programmatically scrolls the page through N positions, calls captureVisibleTab at each, stitches the N PNGs with a canvas. Tools like Awesome Screenshot do this. **Trade-offs to evaluate at future polish session:** (a) sticky headers — would duplicate in stitched image (top banner appears N× if stitching N viewports); (b) lazy-loaded content — need to wait between scrolls for images/widgets to render — adds timing fragility + per-platform wait-time tuning; (c) layout that changes on scroll — some sites collapse/expand elements on scroll, breaking stitch alignment; (d) infinite-scroll pages — no defined "bottom" — would need a manual stop signal (e.g., user presses Esc when reached end); (e) performance — multiple captureVisibleTab calls per gesture (slower; bigger PNGs); (f) test coverage — current Playwright spec tests single-viewport; multi-scroll would need a separate harness; (g) Amazon-specific risks — Amazon's continuously-mutating DOM (the same problem behind P-20) means stitch alignment + sticky-header dedup could be especially fragile. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "scroll.*capture|full.*page.*screenshot|stitching|below.fold|two.halves" across all W#2 + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_* docs + STACK_DECISIONS §4 confirmed: the "two halves" hint copy in the overlay banner IS the existing workaround documentation (currently captured at session 6's overlay-banner copy); no prior treatment of a full-page scroll-capture polish item — genuinely new. **Fix shape:** future polish session at the time the design picks one of the approaches; ~600-1000 LOC including the scroll-and-capture orchestrator + canvas stitch + sticky-header detection + per-platform wait-time config + new Playwright spec harness. **Cross-references:** `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_STACK_DECISIONS.md §4` (the original "two halves" Phase 1 design); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` "Deploy session #11" section (today's mid-verification director question + Claude's plain-language explanation + the trade-off table); CHAT_REGISTRY 2026-05-14 deploy-#11 entry. **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** N/A — no schema change needed (CapturedImage table already accommodates the resulting full-page PNG via existing fileSize / mimeType / storagePath columns; the only schema-touchable concern would be if we wanted to track "this image was stitched from N viewports" metadata, which could be a new optional `metadata` JSON column or stay client-side).

- **P-25** Captured text remains highlighted with a light haze after save — no in-page indicator that text was captured. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/text-capture-form.ts` save success path + new content-script helper `saved-text-highlight.ts` + DOM marker (`<span class="plos-cs-saved-text-haze">…</span>` or CSS Custom Highlight API via `CSS.highlights`) + persistence across page rescans (the highlight must re-apply when the user navigates back to the page since saved text rows fetch fresh from server). **Status:** ✅ DONE 2026-05-19-f (W#2 → main deploy session #27 — commit `e7c82da` ff-merged onto `main` cleanly `1dce84b..e7c82da`; ship: additive nullable `selector String?` column on CapturedText via Rule-8-gated `npx prisma db push` in 940ms; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/captured-text-selector.ts` 460 LOC pure-logic helper for XPath + flattened-offset encode/decode of DOM Ranges; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/saved-text-highlight.ts` 214 LOC content-script helper using CSS Custom Highlight API per Rule 14f forced-picker pick of Option A [non-DOM-modifying haze sidesteps the P-14 MutationObserver feedback-loop concern that drove `muteMutationObserver` for highlight-terms.ts]; orchestrator contextmenu capture-phase listener extended to snapshot `window.getSelection().getRangeAt(0)` alongside the existing lastRightClickImageSrc snapshot — the Range is lost across the message round-trip to background and back, so it must be captured synchronously BEFORE the menu opens; selector wired through `openTextCaptureForm` props → `createCapturedText` body → server persists; orchestrator's `maybeShowDetailOverlay` extended to fetch `listCapturedTexts(projectId, urlId)` in parallel with `listCapturedImages` and call `attachSavedTextHaze(range, savedTextId)` for each row with non-null selector that re-locates in current DOM; light-yellow background + faint dotted underline CSS via `::highlight(plos-cs-saved-text)` pseudo-element in `styles.ts`. Test coverage per Rule 27 Option A Hybrid forced-picker pick: src/lib node:test 531 → 536 (+5 selector POST handler cases); extension `npm test` 368 → 416 (+48 — 36 captured-text-selector pure-logic + 12 saved-text-highlight attach/detach with injected fake CSS.highlights registry); Playwright 76 → 78 (+2 new P-25 extension-context specs covering haze attach on matching saved URL + haze tear-down on navigation away). Director real-Chrome verification PASS on first try: sideloaded `plos-extension-2026-05-19-w2-deploy-27.zip` (191,413 bytes); captured fresh text snippet via right-click → "Add to PLOS — Captured Text"; reloaded page; light-yellow haze visible on the matched span (and zero haze on legacy NULL-selector rows, matching the documented v1 limitation pattern same as P-24's null-originalSrcUrl). One small process-observation captured informationally in CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-f: first Playwright run failed because `.output/chrome-mv3/` had a stale build from before today's orchestrator changes; rebuilt extension and re-ran → PASS. Future-session prevention: rebuild extension before running Playwright extension-context specs after editing extension source.). **Severity:** MEDIUM (UX gap — user can re-capture the same text snippet multiple times or wonder which text they already saved; symmetric with P-24 image-saved-indicator concern). **Captured 2026-05-13** by director after region-screenshot session 6 ship. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "text.*highlight.*saved|saved.*highlight|haze|selection.*saved|highlight.*after.*save" across all W#2 + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_* docs returned zero hits — genuinely new. NOTE: the existing `highlight-terms.ts` handles user-defined HIGHLIGHT TERMS (keyword search-and-highlight) which is an UNRELATED feature; do NOT collide the two CSS classes / data-attributes / lifecycles. **Why:** Symmetric UX with URL + image recognition. Director's framing: *"That text should remain highlighted in a light haze to let the user know it is saved."* **Fix shape:** (a) on text-capture save success, the form already has the user's selection Range (or the original text + a serializable selector) — extend save path to PERSIST that selector to PLOS server as part of CapturedText row (new optional `selector` column or use existing free-form `metadata` JSON if it exists); (b) new content-script helper `saved-text-highlight.ts` that fetches saved CapturedText rows for the current URL + tries to re-locate each one's selection in the live DOM (DOM-walker-based; tolerate small mismatches gracefully) + wraps the located range in a `<span class="plos-cs-saved-text-haze">` (or uses CSS Custom Highlight API for cleaner non-DOM-modifying highlight); (c) integrate with orchestrator's recognition cycle (fire after recognition Set is built); (d) cleanup on teardown; (e) CSS for the haze in `styles.ts` (e.g., `background: rgba(255,255,0,0.15); border-bottom: 1px dashed rgba(0,0,0,0.2);`). Considerations: text-selector serialization (CSS Custom Highlight API is cleaner but needs Chrome 105+ which we already require; alternative is XPath or `Range`-based selector with offset); DOM mutation tolerance (saved text may shift if page restructures — best-effort match, no error if not found); collision avoidance with `highlight-terms.ts` (different class, different lifecycle). Estimated ~400-600 LOC + new Playwright spec slice. Schema change required: add nullable `selector` column to `CapturedText` (or use JSON `metadata` if one exists). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** would flip to "Yes" when this work starts.

- **P-21** `pickInitialUrl` + `buildRecognitionSet` asymmetric canonicalize — both functions canonicalize the page-URL side but NOT the row-URL side; non-canonical saved rows fail comparison. **⚠️ NOTE 2026-05-12-g follow-up DB inspection:** queried `CompetitorUrl` table for all `platform='amazon'` rows (9 total) — **ALL 9 stored in perfect canonical form `https://www.amazon.com/dp/{ASIN}`; zero slug-variants, zero other forms.** This means P-21's asymmetry hypothesis CANNOT explain today's Amazon P-15 FAIL alone, since both sides of the comparison are already canonical for the actually-stored data. **P-21 is still a real defensive improvement** (covers the case where a future user manually pastes a slug-variant URL into the URL-add form before saving), but it's NO LONGER positioned to fix today's observed Amazon FAIL. Next session should NOT just ship P-21 expecting it to recover the Amazon P-15 verification — it must first **controlled-reproduce the FAIL with a known-good test setup** (saved row's exact ASIN visible on the slug-variant page) to identify the actual proximate cause. Plausible alternatives: (a) test-setup mismatch — director's slug-variant page ASIN may have differed from the saved row's ASIN (most likely given the canonical-data finding); (b) `props.platform` value wrong at right-click time so `getModuleByPlatform` returns null and canonicalize is omitted (unlikely given orchestrator's hostname-vs-platform gate, but verifiable via DevTools); (c) actual code bug not yet identified. Updated (a.20) RECOMMENDED-NEXT accordingly. **⚠️ Further update 2026-05-12-h:** controlled-reproduce executed in W#2 polish session #16 with verified-ASIN-match test setup on real Amazon PASSED — P-15 fix works for ASIN-match-controlled inputs; session #9's FAIL retroactively attributed to test-setup ASIN mismatch (plausible alternative (a) above). P-21 status reframed from "fixes the session-9-FAIL" to "future-defensive improvement covering the user-pasted-slug-variant-URL-into-the-URL-add-form case" — still open and worth shipping eventually, but priority drops to MEDIUM-defensive (was MEDIUM-recovers-P-15). Next session: see (a.21) RECOMMENDED-NEXT slot (W#2 polish backlog continuation OR Waypoint #2 Extension build session 5). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/captured-text-validation.ts pickInitialUrl` (lines 128-147) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/url-normalization.ts buildRecognitionSet` (lines 63-72) + caller-site update in `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts ~line 117` where `buildRecognitionSet(rows)` is currently called without canonicalize. **Status:** OPEN. **Severity:** MEDIUM (recovers today's P-15 fix value on real Amazon; improves orchestrator banner accuracy for slug-variant-saved Amazon rows for free). **Captured 2026-05-12-g** during W#2 → main deploy session #9 verification (Amazon FAIL on P-15) → ~10 min in-session code investigation surfaced the asymmetry. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for "asymmetric", "row.url canonical", "buildRecognitionSet canonicalize" across all W#2 + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_* docs returned zero hits — genuinely new. **Why:** Today's P-15 fix extended `pickInitialUrl` to canonicalize the `pageUrl` (LEFT side of comparison) but the loop body still calls `normalizeUrlForRecognition(row.url)` (RIGHT side) — non-canonical row.url stays non-canonical through normalize (which only strips `?…` query, not path-level noise). Same asymmetry pre-exists in `buildRecognitionSet` which is used by orchestrator's "already saved" banner check at `orchestrator.ts:280-285` — if user's saved Amazon row is in slug-variant form, the banner-recognition fails too. Walmart/ebay/etsy have less URL-form variance per product so the asymmetry rarely surfaces in practice on those platforms. **Fix shape:** ~2 LOC in `pickInitialUrl` (canonicalize row.url before normalizing inside the comparison loop) + ~2 LOC analogous fix in `buildRecognitionSet` (accept optional `canonicalize?: (href: string) => string | null` parameter; canonicalize row.url before set insertion) + ~1 LOC orchestrator call-site update (pass `platformModule.canonicalProductUrl` to `buildRecognitionSet`) + ~4 new test cases (canonicalize-row-url-only, canonicalize-both-sides, canonicalize-returns-null-fallback, regression against non-canonical saved rows). **No longer (a.20) RECOMMENDED-NEXT** as of 2026-05-12-h (controlled-reproduce PASS — see above). Stays open as future-defensive polish; director picks from (a.21) menu when prioritized.

- **P-38** Popup auto-pre-select URL when only 1 saved URL exists (NEW 2026-05-19-c — Rule 11 in-session scope-add during P-13 verification on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; director's natural-use feedback: *"popup did not autoselect the url for 'Attach to which saved URL?'"*). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` (useEffect that loads URLs + resetForm). **Status:** ❌ **REVERTED 2026-05-19-c-2** via `git revert` commit `f0cef37`. Originally shipped 2026-05-19-c via deploy session #22 (commit `a0d5c8a`) but determined to be the WRONG FEATURE during P-39 verification on 2026-05-19-c-2 — director's actual ask was for the popup dropdown to pre-select the saved URL **matching the page currently open**, not generic 1-URL-rule auto-select. Reverted in deploy session #24 alongside P-39 revert + P-40 ship. See CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-3 §Entry "Wrong-feature-shipped-twice slip" for full root-cause + prevention rule. The actually-correct fix shipped as P-40 (active-tab-URL match via `chrome.tabs.query` + `pickInitialUrl` pattern). **Original capture preserved below for historical record (note: commit `a0d5c8a`'s message says "P-27" due to a session-start numbering-collision slip — see CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-2 §Entry; doc-layer canonical number is P-38; reverted regardless):** **NUMBERING NOTE:** commit message `a0d5c8a` says "P-27" due to a session-start numbering-collision slip — at mid-session scope-add moment, the new polish item was labeled "P-27" without first grepping ROADMAP for existing P-NN usage. Existing P-27 (delete captured texts/images, deployed 2026-05-18 in deploy session #17) and P-28 (delete URLs with cascade, also deployed 2026-05-18) were already taken; this new item's canonical number is **P-38**. Git history of `a0d5c8a` is frozen per Rule 8 (never rewrite git history); doc layer carries the canonical P-38 number. See CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-2 §Entry "Polish-item numbering collision" for full root-cause + prevention rule. **Severity:** LOW (UX micro-feature; multi-URL case still requires manual pick — see P-39 for the sticky-preference extension). **Why:** Director's S4-A-style natural-use feedback during P-13 verification — popup's "Attach to which saved URL?" dropdown started empty with "Pick a saved URL…" placeholder, forcing the user to click and pick even when there was only one possible answer. The content-script overlay's right-click flow has had URL pre-selection since P-15 + P-21 (via `pickInitialUrl` + canonicalize), but the popup never got the analog because the popup has no "current page URL" context to drive a smart pick. **Fix shipped (Rule 11 forced-picker — director picked the simple rule from 3 options at mid-session):** ~4 LOC in `CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` — in the useEffect's `.then()` callback after `setUrls(urlRows)`: `const onlyUrl = urlRows[0]; if (urlRows.length === 1 && onlyUrl) setSelectedUrlId(onlyUrl.id);` + analogous swap in resetForm to keep zero-friction after post-save reset. Both sites use local-extraction pattern (`const onlyUrl = urls[0]; setSelectedUrlId(urls.length === 1 && onlyUrl ? onlyUrl.id : '');`) to satisfy the extension's `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` tsconfig. **Test coverage:** Option A (no new tests) per Rule 27 forced-picker continuation — React useEffect / setState transitions aren't observable at node:test level without a DOM; Playwright popup-testing is more setup-heavy than this fix's risk warrants. Director's manual sideload verification at deploy time on a TRUE 1-URL Project+Platform combo is the regression check. **Verification:** initial attempt FAILED on three test cases because director's test combo had 2+ URLs (so P-38 correctly skipped the pre-select per Test B's expected behavior); after director set up a TRUE 1-URL scenario (added one URL on a previously-unused platform via right-click flow), P-38 PASS — URL pre-selected automatically. **Pre-capture search per Rule 24:** grep for popup URL pre-selection patterns in popup entrypoints + ROADMAP + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_* docs returned zero hits — genuinely new W#2 polish item.

- **P-40** Popup auto-pre-select URL matching active tab (page-on-page-match) (NEW 2026-05-19-c-2 — actually-correct fix for director's session-start natural-use feedback, after P-38 + P-39 attempts both proved to be wrong-feature). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` useEffect (Promise.all extended from 2-tuple → 3-tuple by adding `chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true })`). **Status:** ✅ **DONE 2026-05-19-c-2** on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` then deployed to main via deploy session #24 (commit `182da37`; Vercel auto-redeploy no-op since web bundle unchanged — fix lives in extension code only). **Severity:** MEDIUM (closes the actual real-world UX friction director surfaced from session start — popup dropdown finally pre-selects the URL matching the page currently open). **Why:** Same UX pattern the right-click content-script overlay has used since P-15 + P-21 — when the user is on a page that's already saved to PLOS, the URL dropdown should automatically pre-select that page's saved row instead of forcing manual pick. Director's session-start feedback during P-13 verification (*"popup did not autoselect the url for 'Attach to which saved URL?'"*) meant THIS specific behavior; I misread it twice (P-38 + P-39, both reverted) before getting it right with P-40. **Fix shipped (15 LOC reusing existing helpers — zero new files, zero new tests since reused helpers are already covered):** (1) imports add `pickInitialUrl` from `captured-text-validation.ts` (already imported) + `getModuleByPlatform` from `platform-modules/registry.ts`; (2) useEffect Promise.all 3-tuple adds `chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true })`; (3) after `setUrls(urlRows)`, look up active tab's URL via `tabs[0]?.url`, get platform module via `getModuleByPlatform(props.platform)`, call `pickInitialUrl(activeTabUrl, urlRows, canonicalize)` with the same `(href) => platformModule.canonicalProductUrl(href)` shape as content-script overlay's `text-capture-form.ts:466`; (4) if matched, `setSelectedUrlId(matched.id)`; if no match, selectedUrlId stays at default '' (placeholder shown). **Test coverage:** Option A (no new tests) per Rule 27 forced-picker — `pickInitialUrl` is already covered by 6 cases in `captured-text-validation.test.ts` (P-15 + P-21 added the canonicalize coverage); the popup-side `chrome.tabs.query` + `getModuleByPlatform` wire-up is mechanical single-call integration that director's sideload manual verification on the REAL workflow at deploy time is sufficient regression coverage for. **Verification:** director sideloaded deploy-24 zip + opened popup on a saved competitor product page → confirmed that page's saved URL pre-selected in dropdown automatically. Three test cases all PASSED (saved page → pre-select; non-saved page → placeholder; slug-variant Amazon URL → still matches via canonicalProductUrl per P-15). **Edge cases handled by reusing pickInitialUrl:** active tab URL undefined (chrome.tabs permission edge case) → no match → placeholder; active tab URL doesn't match any saved row → no match → placeholder; active tab URL is on a different domain than props.platform → pickInitialUrl returns null since saved rows would be on the current platform's domain; slug-variant Amazon URL → `canonicalProductUrl` strips slug + normalizes to `/dp/{ASIN}` and matches saved canonical row. **Permission check:** `activeTab` manifest permission already in place; grants temporary URL access on popup-open user gesture. **Cross-references:** P-15 (`pickInitialUrl` canonicalize step extension) + P-21 (`buildRecognitionSet` symmetric canonicalize) — same helpers reused; CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-3 §Entry (the wrong-feature-shipped-twice slip that delayed P-40 by ~2 hours); commit `182da37` (the P-40 ship); deploy session #24 main push.

- **P-42** (NEW 2026-05-20-b; ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-f** via build commit `2e3270d` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`) `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook silently did NOT auto-fire for memory writes — Layer 1 (Mechanical) of P-41's 4-layer architecture had a gap. **Where:** `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (the hook script — fix shipped this session, see below) + `.claude/settings.json` (PostToolUse wiring — was wired correctly all along; root cause was inside the script) + `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` (the SessionStart canary — only checks SOURCE memory dir health; this session adds a NEW Layer-3b canary at `.claude/hooks/check-memory-mirror-staleness.sh` for SOURCE-vs-MIRROR divergence). **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-f** — closed via build commit `2e3270d` (4 files changed +178/-4). **Empirical root-cause confirmation (the headline finding):** added temporary diagnostic instrumentation to `backup-memory-dir.sh` that wrote raw PostToolUse stdin to `/tmp/plos-hook-debug.log`, triggered a disposable memory-file Write (`_diagnostic_test_p42.md`), captured the actual JSON stdin shape, and confirmed Claude Code's PostToolUse hook stdin uses the field name `.tool_name` — NOT `.tool`. Both `backup-memory-dir.sh` + the sibling `track-edited-docs.sh` were using `jq -r '.tool // empty'`, which always resolved to `""`, so the Edit/Write gate inside each hook always exited without doing work. All three P-42 reproductions across 2026-05-20-b, 2026-05-20-c, and 2026-05-21 stemmed from this single field-name mismatch. **Fixes shipped this session:** (a) `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` jq selector `.tool` → `.tool_name` + hook-contract comment updated to document the empirically-confirmed stdin shape; (b) `.claude/hooks/track-edited-docs.sh` identical fix — captured per Rule 24 as scope-adjacent to P-42 since the same bug class surfaced during diagnosis (the sibling hook's `.claude/session-modified-docs.log` had been 0-byte since May 20 because of the same root cause); (c) NEW `.claude/hooks/check-memory-mirror-staleness.sh` SessionStart canary (~175 LOC) for **Layer-3b defense-in-depth** — compares source memory dir against `.codespace-backup/memory/` mirror on every session start + emits 🚨 alert via `additionalContext` if file count / presence / per-file size diverges (tested in 3 scenarios — healthy / source-only file / size mismatch — all expected outputs); (d) `.claude/settings.json` wires the new canary into the SessionStart hooks array alongside existing `inject-next-session-pointer.sh` + `check-memory-dir-status.sh`. Integration test post-fix (Write of disposable `_integration_test_p42.md` → backup mirror picked up the file → canary then reports empty additionalContext) confirmed both layers work end-to-end before commit; diagnostic instrumentation + test files cleaned up post-test. **Layer architecture after this ship:** Layer 1 MECHANICAL = `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook (NOW WORKING); Layer 2 PROCEDURAL = Rule 29 + `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh`; Layer 3 DETECTION = `check-memory-dir-status.sh` SessionStart canary for SOURCE-MISSING / SOURCE-EMPTY / SOURCE-PARTIAL-LOSS; **NEW Layer 3b DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH = `check-memory-mirror-staleness.sh`** for SOURCE-vs-MIRROR DIVERGENCE; Layer 4 RECOVERY = `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh`. **Reusable Pattern for "how to debug a silent PostToolUse hook":** capture raw hook stdin to a known path (`/tmp/<name>-debug.log`) via `tee` + run a controlled tool invocation + inspect the JSON shape — the pattern surfaced the root cause in one diagnostic step. Worth surfacing as the default first approach when any future hook isn't firing despite being wired in settings.json. **CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-reference:** new §Entry 2026-05-22-f closing the three prior P-42 §Entries (2026-05-20-b initial capture + 2026-05-20-c reproduction #2 + 2026-05-21 reproduction #3 paragraph inside the 2026-05-21 Build #8 §Entry); cumulatively strengthens Rule 29's 4-layer architecture by closing the Layer-1 gap + adding Layer-3b. **ORIGINAL CAPTURE narrative preserved below for traceability:** **Severity:** **HIGH** (the gap defeats Layer 1 of the 4-layer architecture P-41 shipped specifically to prevent memory loss; if this gap persists + a Codespaces rebuild happens, memory loss recurs despite the protective architecture being "in place" — exactly the failure mode P-41 was meant to prevent). **Captured 2026-05-20-b** during P-27 design session end-of-session — Claude wrote 2 NEW memory files (`feedback_destructive_ops_confirmation.md` + `feedback_remaining_roadmap_summary.md`) via Write tool + edited `MEMORY.md` via Edit tool; the doc-batch agent's verification flagged that `.codespace-backup/memory/` had 10 files vs. source's 12 + backup MEMORY.md was 2472 bytes vs. source's 2945 bytes (the index update also didn't mirror). Manually mirrored via 3 `cp -p` commands at end-of-session — source + backup now in sync at 12/12. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (zero prior treatment found):** grepped `mirror.staleness|backup.memory.dir|hook.fire|hook.didn|PostToolUse.*fire` across ROADMAP + CORRECTIONS_LOG + HANDOFF_PROTOCOL — only hits are today's CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-20-b documenting this slip; no prior treatment as a forward polish item. **Why this matters operationally:** P-41 shipped the 4-layer architecture (Mechanical / Procedural / Detection / Recovery) explicitly so memory loss couldn't recur silently. Layer 1 (Mechanical = this hook) is the most-frequently-relied-on layer (fires automatically on every memory write; no Claude memory required). If it doesn't fire, the other 3 layers might still save us (Procedural Rule 29 fires before container ops; Detection canary catches missing-memory; Recovery script restores from backup) — but only if the backup is current. A stale backup makes Detection fire late (after loss) + makes Recovery restore an OLD snapshot. The 4-layer architecture's safety is multiplicative; one broken layer doesn't break it entirely but materially degrades the guarantee. **Possible root causes (unconfirmed — needs investigation):** (a) PostToolUse hook matcher mis-configured in `.claude/settings.json` — fires for specific tools/paths only; the Write/Edit calls this session may not have matched; (b) `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` env var unset at hook-fire time → `BACKUP_DIR` resolves wrong → silent fail at `[ -d "$BACKUP_DIR" ]` guard; (c) the hook fires for a tool-matcher other than Edit/Write — Claude's internal memory-write may use a different tool; (d) `jq` missing on this Codespace (unlikely but worth checking — graceful-degradation exit means no error surfaces); (e) stdout/stderr from the hook is swallowed by Claude Code so silent-failure is invisible. **Fix shape (estimated ~1 session, LOW LOC, HIGH operational importance):** (1) Add a debug-log line to `backup-memory-dir.sh` (e.g., `echo "[backup-memory-dir.sh] fired tool=$TOOL file=$FILE_PATH" >> /tmp/plos-hook-debug.log`) to make the hook fire/no-fire observable; (2) Trigger a controlled test memory write in a fresh session + inspect debug log + diagnose; (3) Fix the root cause based on diagnosis — fix matcher / fix env-var passthrough / change tool wiring / install jq if missing / etc.; (4) **AND/OR** add a defense-in-depth: a NEW mirror-staleness canary (new `.claude/hooks/check-mirror-staleness.sh` SessionStart hook that compares source memory dir against `.codespace-backup/memory/` via file count + per-file md5/mtime; alerts on divergence; auto-syncs if drift is minimal + safe; logs to CORRECTIONS_LOG if drift is substantial). The canary is cheaper insurance than fully diagnosing the PostToolUse hook gap; both together is the most-thorough-and-reliable path per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`. **Cross-references:** P-41 (the 4-layer architecture this is Layer-1-gap of); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-20-b §Entry "backup-memory-dir.sh PostToolUse hook did NOT auto-fire" (the slip narrative + manual-mirror remediation + proposed fixes); `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (the hook itself); `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` (the existing SessionStart canary that doesn't catch mirror staleness); `.claude/settings.json` (the hook wiring to audit); `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` (the mirror destination); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 29 (the procedural layer this composes with). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** N/A — hook/settings work only; no schema, no API, no shared-types. **2026-05-20-c reproduction:** gap reproduced cleanly this session — wrote NEW memory file `feedback_handoff_carryovers_to_roadmap.md` + edited `MEMORY.md` via Edit; backup mirror at `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` did NOT receive either file automatically; manually mirrored via 2 `cp` commands at session-close. Same gap as 2026-05-20-b; investigation still pending. **2026-05-21 reproduction #3:** gap reproduced AGAIN — wrote NEW feedback-memory file `feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md` + edited `MEMORY.md` via Edit; backup mirror was MISSING the new file entirely + the updated `MEMORY.md` was STALE in the backup (3201 bytes from yesterday vs. source 3515 bytes from today). Manually mirrored both via `cp` before the end-of-session commit. **Three reproductions across three consecutive memory-write sessions makes P-42 the highest-priority operational-tooling polish item open** — every reproduction confirms the Layer-1 (Mechanical) gap is reliably reproducible + that director's memory is unsafe across any future Codespaces rebuild until P-42 ships. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED to ship before any future big session.

- **P-43** (NEW 2026-05-20-c; ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-g** via build commit `4afea35` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`) `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` template polish — convert relative-`cd` calls to absolute paths so parallel-Bash invocations don't race on shared CWD state, **and scope-expanded per same-session commentary to ALL `.claude/commands/` templates (scoreboard.md + deploy.md + ship-polish-item.md)**. **Where:** `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` + `.claude/commands/ship-polish-item.md` — three slash-command templates that previously used `cd extensions/competition-scraping && ...` (relative `cd`) and relative `$(find src/lib ...)` substitutions. **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-g** — closed via build commit `4afea35` (3 files changed +20/-20). **Headline fix:** every relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping && ...` invocation in all three templates rewritten as absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...`; every `$(find src/lib -name '*.test.ts')` rewritten as `$(find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib -name '*.test.ts')`; baselines refreshed across all three templates from stale 2026-05-19-g values (53 routes / 536 src/lib / 428 ext / 79 Playwright) to current 2026-05-22-g values (57 / 590 / 558 / 94). **Scope expansion beyond the original narrow scoreboard.md-only fix shape:** the entry's same-session commentary across reproductions #2 + #3 + #4 explicitly recommended extending the fix to ALL `.claude/commands/` templates (verbatim: *"The fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template — ALL parallel-Bash patterns in any skill or session need absolute paths"*); three files + baseline refresh is the most-thorough fix per Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation — partial scope (only scoreboard.md) would have left deploy.md + ship-polish-item.md with the same CWD-leak class waiting to bite the next deploy or polish session. **Most-thorough verification (Task #9):** re-grep across `.claude/commands/` returns ZERO remaining relative-cd or relative `find src/lib` patterns; Checks 1-5 all GREEN at exact baselines after fix (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / extension `npm test` **558/558** / src/lib node:test **590/590** / `npm run build` **57 routes** EXIT=0); re-ran fixed Checks 1, 3, 4 from `/tmp` (an arbitrary non-repo-root cwd — the precise scenario all four prior P-43 reproductions hit) — ALL GREEN at exact baselines proving the absolute-path discipline survives cwd drift; Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention. **Reusable Pattern memorialized in CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-22-g:** (a) any `cd` in a slash-command template MUST be absolute, not relative; (b) any `$(find ...)` substitution should also use an absolute starting directory as belt-and-suspenders against Claude shortening or dropping the explicit cd prefix; (c) baselines in slash-command templates should be refreshed every time scoreboard counts drift; (d) verification = re-run the template's commands from an arbitrary non-repo-root cwd (e.g. `/tmp`) to prove absolute-path discipline survives cwd drift. **CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-reference:** new §Entry 2026-05-22-g closing the four prior P-43 §Entries (2026-05-20-c original capture + 2026-05-21 reproduction #2 + 2026-05-21-d reproduction #3 paragraph + 2026-05-22 reproduction #4 paragraph) + embeds two informational sub-observations (the backgrounded-`npm run build` 0-byte-output reproduction recurrence, and Claude's own test-slip when writing the verification — momentarily dropping the absolute cd prefix from Check 3 — itself a perfect demonstration of the pre-fix bug class). **ORIGINAL CAPTURE narrative preserved below for traceability:** **Severity:** LOW-MEDIUM elevated toward MEDIUM after the 2026-05-21 reproduction #2 (each reproduction strengthens the case to ship soon). The 2026-05-20-c original slip produced a misleading-but-not-erroring scoreboard count — `find src/lib` returned 428 cases instead of the expected 577 because a parallel shell had landed CWD inside `extensions/competition-scraping`; the count looked plausible because 428 happens to coincidentally match the extension test count baseline. **2026-05-21 reproduction #2:** during /scoreboard execution in Build #2 session, a parallel `npm run build` call ran in the extensions/competition-scraping directory (NOT the root) because an earlier parallel `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npx tsc --noEmit` leaked CWD state; symptom was tail-output showing `wxt build` instead of Next.js compiled-successfully + route table; caught + corrected by re-running with explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build > /tmp/next-build.log 2>&1`. The fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template — ALL parallel-Bash patterns in any skill or session need absolute paths. **2026-05-21-d reproduction #3:** during /scoreboard execution in Build #5 session, Check 4 (`node --test --experimental-strip-types $(find src/lib -name '*.test.ts')`) used a relative `src/lib` path WITHOUT an explicit `cd` back to root, so it inherited the prior bash call's cwd (`/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping`) and ran the EXTENSION test suite (482 tests reported, identical to Check 3's extension count). Caught from the duplicated test count + re-ran Check 1 + Check 4 with absolute paths (`cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && ...` and `find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib -name '*.test.ts'`); re-run reported the correct src/lib count of 589/589. Third reproduction within ~2 weeks confirms the CWD-leak class is reliably reproducible across sessions despite the prevention rule being documented since 2026-05-16. **2026-05-22 reproduction #4:** during /scoreboard execution in Build #6 session, Check 5 (extension `npm test`) ran as `npm test 2>&1 | tail -15` WITHOUT a leading `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping &&`; each Bash invocation gets a fresh shell so the prior parallel call's cd didn't carry over; npm reported "Missing script: test" from root (root's package.json has no `test` script, only `test:e2e:*` Playwright entry points). This reproduction is loud-fail variant (npm hard-errors instead of producing a misleading-but-not-erroring count); easy to catch from the error output. Caught immediately + re-ran with proper absolute-path cd; baseline confirmed 482/482 on re-run. **FOURTH reproduction in ~2 weeks across the P-27 Build arc.** At this reproduction rate, P-43 polish should ship as the very next non-P-27 session. The P-43 polish should ship sooner rather than later. **Captured 2026-05-20-c** during P-27 Build #1 /scoreboard run — Claude fired 4 Bash calls in parallel; the relative `cd` race produced a CWD collision; the misleading 428 count required a careful re-run with absolute paths to surface the actual 577. See CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-20-c §Entry for full slip narrative + the official Bash-tool guidance Claude ignored (*"use absolute paths and avoid `cd`"*). **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (zero prior treatment found):** grepped `scoreboard.md|.claude/commands/scoreboard|parallel.*cd|relative cd` across ROADMAP + CORRECTIONS_LOG + HANDOFF_PROTOCOL + `.claude/commands/` — only hits are today's CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-20-c documenting this slip; no prior ROADMAP item. **Fix shape (estimated ~10-20 min, sub-1-hour polish):** rewrite each `cd extensions/competition-scraping && ...` invocation in `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` as `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...` (absolute path); rewrite the `find src/lib -name '*.test.ts'` line with absolute `find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib -name '*.test.ts'`. **Test coverage:** trivial — re-run /scoreboard in a session and confirm the 6-check sequence still produces the expected counts. **Cross-references:** CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-20-c §Entry "Parallel Bash cd shell-state collision during /scoreboard run"; `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (the file to polish); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 27 (the deploy-verification methodology /scoreboard is the canonical tool for). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** N/A — `.claude/` tooling template polish only; no schema, no API, no shared-types.

- **P-47** (NEW 2026-05-22-i) Shadow DOM refactor of `video-capture-form.ts` to replace the 80-event-listener Issue 2 band-aid shipped in P-45 Build #2 (`ee8c79d`) with proper page-level isolation. **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/video-capture-form.ts` (the form opener — currently mounts a backdrop div + content div into document.body; would mount a `<div>` host element with `attachShadow({mode:'open'})` and render the form inside the shadow root instead) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/styles.ts` (all `.plos-cs-form-*` CSS rules — shadow DOM doesn't inherit page styles, so all of the form's CSS would need to be injected inside the shadow root via a `<style>` tag) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedVideoPasteForm.tsx` + `CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` + `UrlAddForm.tsx` (popup React forms — already isolated since popup is a separate document; this entry is primarily about the content-script form; popup forms could be cross-referenced for whether Shadow DOM is needed there too — likely not since popup is its own document context). **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-24-f** end-to-end on vklf.com — Phase-4 director real-Chrome verification on Amazon walked at 2026-05-24-f bundled Phase-4 verification session; BOTH walkthrough steps PASS first-walk (form opens cleanly + interactions work cleanly with Shadow DOM mount + zero page-level focus interference + Escape closes cleanly). Zero fix-forwards needed for P-47. Pairs with the §B 2026-05-22-i Pattern B band-aid memorialization (P-45 Build #2) → §B 2026-05-24-d build → §B 2026-05-24-e deploy → §B 2026-05-24-f Phase-4 PASS as the complete band-aid → structural-fix lifecycle now verified end-to-end in production. ORIGINAL DEPLOY-PHASE-4-PENDING narrative preserved below for traceability. ✅ DEPLOYED-PHASE-4-PENDING 2026-05-24-e on vklf.com — Session 1 (Shadow DOM mount + CSS migration + band-aid deletion) shipped to production via `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` bundled ff-merge `9205340..d68885a` carrying 5 commits (W5 build `3c981be` + Reviews polish `41172f1` + W5 doc-batch `4d0f771` + P-47 build `d08f673` + P-47 doc-batch `d68885a`) behind ONE Rule 9 gate; Vercel auto-redeploy fired; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-24-w2-deploy-34.zip` 202.98 KB dropped at repo root for director sideload at Phase-4 verification. Pre-deploy + post-merge /scoreboard both 5/5 GREEN at unchanged baselines (root tsc clean / ext tsc clean / 558 ext / 786 src/lib / 62 routes). Phase-4 director real-Chrome verification on Amazon DEFERRED to next session at director directive *"defer to next session (per W4 deploy 2026-05-26 Pattern)"* — 2-step verification walkthrough preserved verbatim in next NEXT_SESSION.md ## Standing carry-overs section (c); pairs with W5 4-step walkthrough section (b) into ONE Phase-4 step at next session since both ship via the same extension form surface. After Phase-4 PASS next session → P-47 flips to ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED + P-47 closes (only Session 1 was needed at code level + Phase-4; Sessions 2-3 from the original estimate MERGED into today's bundled deploy). ORIGINAL CAPTURE narrative preserved below for traceability: ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-24-d — Session 1 (Shadow DOM mount + CSS migration + band-aid deletion) landed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` via build commit `d08f673` (2 files +203/-220, net -17 LOC). Pre-build + post-refactor /scoreboard both 5/5 GREEN at unchanged baselines (root tsc clean / ext tsc clean / 558 ext / 786 src/lib / 62 routes). DEPLOY-PENDING — bundled into next session's W5 + P-47 deploy per (a.87). Phase-4 director real-Chrome verification on Amazon also pending — bundles into the next deploy session's Phase-4 step (covers both W5 URL-form additions + P-47 Shadow DOM mount since both ship via the same extension form surface). Session 2 (Cross-platform verification per the original estimate) MERGED into the next deploy session's Phase-4 since the Shadow DOM mount is structural-only with no cross-platform variance expected. Session 3 (DEPLOY) MERGED into the next bundled W5 + P-47 deploy session. **Fix shape shipped today:** Created host `<div>` with `position:fixed inset:0 z-index:999998` + `attachShadow({mode:'open'})`; injected `<style>FORM_CHROME_CSS</style>` inside the shadow root; appended backdrop inside the shadow root; appended host to `document.body`. `destroy()` removes the host (parent of the shadow root) instead of the backdrop. `focusNewCategoryInput()` reads `shadow.activeElement` instead of `document.activeElement` (since `document.activeElement` returns the host element for any descendant focused inside the shadow). Deleted `applyAggressiveEventIsolation` function (62 LOC including the 20-event-array `stopPropagation` loop + the click-handler force-focus with `queueMicrotask` retry) + its 4 call sites + its 20-line context comment. Escape-to-close `keydown` listener stays on `document` (keydown events from inside the shadow root compose up through the host into the document tree). `styles.ts` extracted new exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant containing `.plos-cs-form-*` + `.plos-cs-chip-*` rules previously inlined in `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS`; `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS` now interpolates `${FORM_CHROME_CSS}` so the other forms (image / text / url-add) that still mount to `document.body` continue to receive these rules via the host-page `<head>` stylesheet without drift between the two consumption sites. **NEW reusable Pattern memorialized today (see CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-24-d):** "Shadow DOM mount as structural replacement for per-listener event-isolation band-aid" — when a per-listener band-aid (N events × M elements = N×M listeners on each component open) successfully isolates a component from page-level interference, the structural replacement is a Shadow DOM mount providing the same isolation at the boundary level with zero per-listener overhead; pairs with P-45 Build #2 §Entry 2026-05-22-i Pattern B (the band-aid memorialized) as the long-term-fix pairing. **LOW informational sub-observation:** CSS extraction into shared exported constant + template-literal interpolation back into composite constant — single source of truth for both global stylesheet consumers (image / text / url-add forms via host-page `<head>`) and Shadow DOM root consumer (video-capture form via shadow `<style>` tag); minor refactor pattern. **Severity:** LOW (the 80-event-listener band-aid worked empirically — verified on Amazon during Phase 4 — and was a single localized addition to `video-capture-form.ts`; no user-visible bug remained today before the refactor; the refactor is structurally cleaner with no expected user-visible delta). **Why captured (not "fix this now"):** Issue 2 ships an aggressive event-isolation band-aid (20 events × 4 inputs = 80 listeners on each form open) that solves the immediate page-level focus-stealing problem on Amazon, but Shadow DOM mount is structurally cleaner — events don't bubble out of a Shadow DOM into the page DOM by default, so the isolation is at the boundary rather than per-listener. The band-aid carries hidden risk for any future video-form feature that needs to PROPAGATE events upward (e.g., a future "click outside form to dismiss" behavior would now have to be wired with capture-phase listeners on the backdrop instead of bubble-phase on document). Shadow DOM also performs better — N inputs × 20 listeners is N×20 function-pointer-and-closure allocations per form open vs. zero added overhead with a shadow root. **Fix shape (estimated ~2-3 sessions):** **Session 1 — Shadow DOM mount + CSS migration.** Convert `video-capture-form.ts` to mount a host element with `attachShadow({mode:'open'})`; move all `.plos-cs-form-*` rules from `styles.ts` into a `<style>` tag injected inside the shadow root at mount time; remove the 80-event-listener band-aid (Shadow DOM's isolation makes it redundant); add a single backdrop-click listener on the host element if "click outside to dismiss" is needed. ~150 LOC delta. Re-run extension `npm test` — all 558 cases should remain GREEN (the test setup doesn't exercise the page-level focus-stealing path; the band-aid was a runtime-only defense). **Session 2 — Cross-platform verification.** Sideload, walk through Amazon + Ebay + Walmart + Etsy to verify the form still receives input (the Shadow DOM mount should be at least as good as the band-aid; if anything works WORSE on any platform, fix-forward in-session or revert). **Session 3 (CONDITIONAL — DEPLOY).** If Sessions 1+2 pass clean, ship via Rule 9 gate + ff-merge → main + Vercel auto-redeploy + ping-pong sync + fresh extension zip + director re-verify on Amazon. **Test coverage approach:** Hybrid per Rule 27 — node:test for any new shadow-root-mounting helpers + Playwright for the form's open/close lifecycle + manual walkthrough for the page-level isolation (the same shape as P-45 Build #2's Phase 4 verify). **Cross-references:** P-45 polish-backlog entry (the parent ship that introduced the band-aid the refactor replaces); P-27 Bug #11 (the bug class the band-aid + this refactor both address); P-46 W#2 Phase 2 design session (the next session per (a.69) — P-47 sequencing happens AFTER P-46's design lands since P-46 may introduce new in-form interactions that change the refactor's scope); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/video-capture-form.ts` line ~XXX (the band-aid 20-event stopPropagation block + the click-handler force-focus with `queueMicrotask` retry — both to be removed in Session 1); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/styles.ts` (all `.plos-cs-form-*` CSS to migrate into the shadow root). HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 9 (the deploy gate that fires once for Session 3's `git push origin main`); Rule 14f (the recommended fix path is Session 1+2+3 sequential per most-thorough/reliable; Default-to-recommendation may apply if director defers; Rule 14f picker may fire if director wants alternatives like "Session 1 only as a non-deployed branch" or "skip Shadow DOM and accept the band-aid as permanent"); Rule 24 (this entry IS the Rule 24 outcome captured at P-45 Build #2 ship). CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-22-i Reusable Pattern B (the band-aid pattern memorialized + the Shadow DOM as the proper long-term fix). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** N/A — content-script + CSS refactor only; no schema, no API, no shared-types.

ORIGINAL CAPTURE narrative preserved below: captured 2026-05-22 in `session_2026-05-22-c_p27-build-9-devtools-diagnosis-and-p45-screen-recording-pivot`. Full design captured in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` new §C (12 locked decisions + 11-step user flow + capture API + permissions + schema impact + save-flow integration + implementation arc + open questions). Build #1 of P-45 is the next RECOMMENDED-NEXT session per (a.63). **Severity:** HIGH-MEDIUM (unblocks Amazon + Ebay captures fundamentally — currently broken on the live site per Build #9's Bug #12 diagnosis; universal across platforms vs the per-platform-quirk fix-forward pattern Builds #2-#8 chased). **Why:** Build #9 diagnosed Bug #12 root cause as MSE-player blob: URLs being unreachable from background SW context (`net::ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND` on every fetch attempt). Build #8's retry helper couldn't have ever cleared this. Three short-term fix directions (snapshot-save / content-script-bytes-fetch / block-with-error) were surfaced + the director's escape-hatch question — *"Is there a way to do a screen recording of the video by first selecting the part of the screen that the recording should focus on and then play the video and then record the screen video and audio and then save that recorded part?"* — surfaced screen recording as a 4th universal direction. Director chose at the sequencing picker to **skip the short-term fix + design screen recording properly NOW** — accepting that Amazon/Ebay saves stay broken on the live site for 2-3 more implementation sessions in exchange for not throwing away short-term work + addressing the entire blob-URL class of bugs at once. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (zero direct prior treatment found; one RELATED infrastructure asset surfaced):** grepped `screen recording` + synonyms (`tab.?capture|desktopcapture|getdisplaymedia|mediarecorder|screencast|screen.?capture|record.?video|video.?record`) across ROADMAP + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN + CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_STACK_DECISIONS + PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE + PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS + DATA_CATALOG + KEYWORD_CLUSTERING (all archive/contract/polish) + CORRECTIONS_LOG + prisma/schema.prisma + src/lib/shared-types/ + extensions/competition-scraping/src/ — zero treatment of "screen recording" as a concept; the related find was **the existing P-17 region-screenshot infrastructure** (`chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab` API in use + `activeTab` permission in manifest + content-script ↔ background bridge proven via `requestVisibleTabCapture()` + region-selection overlay built). That infrastructure cuts P-45 scope by ~50% (recording controller extends region-screenshot from single-frame to multi-frame; overlay is forkable; save flow is reusable). **Fix shape (~2-3 sessions implementation per §C.6 implementation arc table):** **Build #1** — Rule 18 interview-cluster deepening of §C → full §C.0-§C.N structure mirroring §A; record-controller (~150 LOC); region-overlay adaptation (forked from `region-screenshot-overlay.ts`); right-click menu addition; in-progress visual indicator (red dashed border + REC badge); schema migration (add SCREEN_RECORDING enum + `prisma db push` + Rule 9 gate); dev-time happy-path verification on Amazon; node:test coverage for record-controller; Playwright extension-context spec for trigger path. ~1 session. **Build #2** — cross-platform verification (Amazon + Ebay + Walmart + Etsy regression); edge cases (cancel Chrome share dialog + size cap exceeded + tab close mid-recording + codec compatibility + audio-disabled tabs + first-frame thumbnail extraction); director real-Chrome walkthrough; bug capture + fix-forward iteration. ~1 session. **Build #3 (CONDITIONAL)** — UX polish + accessibility; cross-platform Playwright specs; deploy via Rule 9 gate; director re-verification; end-of-arc verification backlog entry. ~1 session if needed. **Capture API choice:** `navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia({ video: true, audio: true })` (web standard; NO new manifest permission required; Chrome's per-recording "Choose what to share" dialog IS the permission UX; large win — no install-time re-permission prompt). **Schema impact:** add `SCREEN_RECORDING` as 3rd value in CapturedVideo.sourceType enum — single delta — `prisma db push` fires once during Build #1 under Rule 9 gate. **Test coverage approach:** Hybrid per Rule 27 + §A.13 (node:test for record-controller state machine + Playwright for trigger path + manual walkthrough for actual screen-recording end-to-end with real Chrome share dialog + DevTools cooperation kept available for codec/runtime issues). **Cross-references:** P-17 (region-screenshot — the existing tab-capture infrastructure P-45 extends; review for the reuse seams + the wxt.config.ts permission model); P-27 (Captured-videos — P-45 closes the MSE-player gap §A doesn't cover); CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md §C (the design itself — 12 locked decisions + 11-step user flow + implementation arc + open questions); CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md §B 2026-05-22 (the session entry that captured the design pivot + Bug #12 diagnosis); CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md §A (the existing capture design P-45 augments without replacing); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 9 (the schema migration gate for adding SCREEN_RECORDING enum); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 14f + Rule 18 (the design-interview pattern Build #1 will follow); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 24 (the pre-capture search performed for this entry); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-22 (no slip entry — design pivot is per-protocol, not a correction). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** stays "No" through end of this session (no schema delta at Build #9); flips to **YES** at P-45 Build #1 when SCREEN_RECORDING enum delta ships via `prisma db push`. Build #2 + #3 stay "No" (post-migration polish + verification). Bug #11 + #9 + #15 from Build #8 verification REMAIN deferred to future sessions — Bug #11 (Add new category input dead) is platform-agnostic + applies to both fast-fetch + recording paths; Bugs #9 + #15 are right-click-on-video gesture edge cases potentially obsoleted by users picking the recording path on Amazon/Ebay.

replace left-side platform navigation with horizontal checkbox bar at top combining platform filters + per-column show/hide controls; add prominent "Comprehensive Competitor Analysis" tab at top; rewrite the URL table with ~12 new columns / per-column `?` tooltips / inline cell editing / resizable column widths persisted / adjustable font size persisted / drag-to-reorder rows persisted / trash icon locked far-right) + `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/DetailedUserGuide.tsx` (shrink + reposition) + `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/url/[urlId]/components/UrlDetailContent.tsx` (remove Sizes / Options box at line 740; add new Type/Description-1/Description-2/Price fields to URL box in same order as table; add new "Captured Reviews" box with star-rating multi-select filter; add per-item "Text Analysis" / "Image Analysis" / "Video Analysis" / "Review Analysis" boxes under EVERY captured item; add per-category "Overall Text Analysis" / "Overall Image Analysis" / "Overall Video Analysis" / "Overall Reviews Analysis" boxes at bottom of each capture box; add "Overall Competitor Analysis" text box at bottom of URL box; remove "added on" column from Captured Text box; show image metadata — category / composition / embedded text / tags — inline next to each image with edit + delete affordances; show video metadata — category / composition / embedded text / tags — inline next to each video with edit + delete affordances; add upload-image / upload-video buttons; add edit-descriptions-and-tags affordances; add delete-review affordance; add edit-video-thumbnail affordance; add Scraping Status toggle at top — Incomplete vs Complete — that mirrors back to the Competition Data table's Status column) + **NEW page:** `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (rich-text editor for admin's holistic competitive analysis with hyperlinks back to specific URL detail pages; edit-mode toggle at top + "Competition Data" back-button at top). **Schema additions (Prisma):** CompetitorUrl gains fields — `type` String? + `description1` String? + `description2` String? + `price` String? + `competitionScore` Int? (1-100) + `scrapingStatus` enum (INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE) + `overallCompetitorAnalysis` Text? + `resultsPageRank` Int? + `numProductReviews` Int? + `numSellerReviews` Int? + new fields TBD by design session for "Results page rank" column semantics. `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/video-capture-form.ts` — add "Select preview thumbnail" button that appears when canvas thumbnail capture fails (per §A.12 fallback path) — clicking it surfaces a region-screenshot overlay over the playing video so user draws a rectangle → that rectangle gets screenshotted via P-17 infrastructure → becomes the thumbnail → save proceeds; new "Add to PLOS — Captured Review" extension right-click capture workflow (entire new gesture — extracts star rating + reviewer + body + date from page DOM where possible OR user-enters). **API surface additions:** CRUD endpoints for all new fields + new `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/reviews` + new `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis` + new `/api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]` + endpoints for vklf.com-side direct uploads (POST text / image / video / review) + endpoints for vklf.com-side edits of descriptions / tags / metadata / reorders / deletes / thumbnail replacement + endpoint for Scraping Status toggle. **Status:** 🟢 **DESIGN COMPLETE 2026-05-23 + WORKSTREAM 1 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-23-c + WORKSTREAM 2 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-23-c + WORKSTREAM 3 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-24 on vklf.com via initial ff-merge `51e68f8..c727da9` (6 commits) + 5 in-session fix-forwards `f8293f1` → `0703174` → `712efa0` → `7358963` → `ac45737` resolving 11 Phase-4 verification issues end-to-end + WORKSTREAM 4 SESSION 1 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-24-b on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` via build commit `283d4d1` (9 files +1258/-32) landing the full §C.4 Session 1 scope — NEW `comprehensive-analysis.ts` DI-seam handler factory (~225 LOC) + 20 new node:test cases bringing src/lib to 764 / W1 501-stub filled in via thin DI-seam shim / NEW page route `comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (~269 LOC) with Edit/Done toggle + back-button + last-edited footer / NEW `AnalysisEditor.tsx` (~151 LOC parallel-component to W2 S1's PerItemAnalysisBox but PUT method + `{ contentJson }` body + variant='full' toolbar) / NEW `AnalysisReadView.tsx` (~76 LOC; Quoted intent: build a comprehensive competitive-intelligence editing surface that combines structured table data (with show/hide / resize / reorder / per-cell-edit) + rich-text holistic analysis + per-item AI-ready analysis text boxes + new Reviews capture + many vklf.com-side direct affordances that currently require the extension. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (zero direct prior treatment found across canonical W#2 docs; some related-infrastructure surfaced):** grepped `Comprehensive Competitor Analysis` + `Competition Data page` + `competition.*data.*redesign` + `review.*capture|captured.*review` + `Competition Score` + `Scraping Status` + `rich.?text.?editor` + `column.*show.?hide` + `drag.?to.?reorder` across `docs/ROADMAP.md` + `docs/COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md` + `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` + `docs/COMPETITION_SCRAPING_STACK_DECISIONS.md` + `docs/PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md` + `docs/CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` — zero direct treatment of any of these as a forward polish item; existing infrastructure that surfaced as relevant: (a) **P-17 region-screenshot overlay** (the rectangle-drag overlay used for the screenshot region tool) is the foundation for the "select preview thumbnail" UX in this scope + can be reused as the rectangle-draw for the thumbnail picker; (b) **P-27 captured-videos schema** has the `composition` + `embeddedText` + `tags` + `category` metadata pattern that needs to extend to vklf.com-side editing in this scope; (c) **P-45 screen-recording overlap** — the "select preview thumbnail" rectangle-draw is structurally identical to P-45's region-selection rectangle-draw; both can share `region-screenshot-overlay.ts` / future `video-region-record-overlay.ts`; (d) **existing `UrlDetailContent.tsx:740` Sizes / Options box** confirmed exists + currently read-only — this scope deletes it (action item for the design session: confirm whether existing prod data needs migration vs. just UI hide). Schema-truth check (Rule 3): `type` / `description1` / `description2` / `price` / `competitionScore` / `scrapingStatus` confirmed NONE exist on CompetitorUrl in `prisma/schema.prisma` — all greenfield. **Fix shape (DESIGN LOCKED 2026-05-23; revised estimate 11-17 sessions across 5 workstreams after Q1 + Q9 scope reductions; precise per-workstream session breakdown in `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §C):** **Workstream 1 — Schema design + migration** (~2-3 sessions): finalize all new CompetitorUrl fields + NEW Prisma models (CapturedReview / ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis / UserTablePreferences) + per-item Analysis text fields on existing models; ship via `prisma db push` under Rule 9 gate; backfill defaults if needed. **Workstream 2 — Web UI Competition Data page redesign** (~3-5 sessions): shrink+reposition info boxes; horizontal checkbox bar; tab to Comprehensive Analysis page; table rewrite with all column controls (show/hide / resize / fontSize / drag-reorder / inline-edit); persistence of all UI preferences. **Workstream 3 — Web UI URL detail page redesign** (~3-5 sessions): remove Sizes/Options; add new field group to URL box; add Captured Reviews box with star filter; per-item Analysis boxes; per-category Overall Analysis boxes; metadata-edit affordances next to images/videos; upload/edit/delete affordances for vklf.com-side; Scraping Status toggle. **Workstream 4 — Comprehensive Competitor Analysis page** (~2-3 sessions): rich-text editor integration (probably TipTap — to be decided at design session), persistence, hyperlink-to-URL-detail-page wiring, edit/view-mode toggle, "Competition Data" back-button. **Workstream 5 — Extension + new Reviews capture workflow** (~3-5 sessions): extension URL save form additions (Type/Desc-1/Desc-2/Price); new "Captured Review" right-click capture gesture (mirrors text-capture pattern) including star-rating + reviewer + body extraction; "select preview thumbnail" button + region-overlay reuse from P-17/P-45; extension Playwright tests; production deploy via Rule 9 gate. **Test coverage approach:** Hybrid per Rule 27 + `CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md §A.13` (Node:test for new helpers + API contract / Playwright for trigger paths + form flows / manual walkthrough for editor + drag-reorder + cross-device persistence / DevTools cooperation kept available for codec/runtime issues in the thumbnail picker). **10 DECISIONS LOCKED 2026-05-23** (full rationale + alternatives in `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §A.1-§A.10): **(Q1)** Per-platform Reviews extraction DEFERRED to future per-platform polish sessions; v1 = schema + URL-detail-page view + vklf.com-side manual entry form (no extension Reviews gesture in v1). **(Q2)** Click-to-edit on every cell (spreadsheet feel; Tab/Enter saves; Escape cancels). **(Q3)** Server-side per-user `UserTablePreferences` Prisma model keyed by (userId, projectId) — cross-device sync. **(Q4)** One Comprehensive Analysis page per Project (synthesizes all platforms + competitors; hyperlinks back to URL detail pages). **(Q5)** TipTap rich-text editor library (used across Comprehensive Analysis page + per-item Analysis text boxes + per-category Overall Analysis boxes + URL-box Overall Competitor Analysis). **(Q6)** Hide Sizes/Options UI; KEEP underlying `CompetitorSize` data + table (no destructive migration; reversible). **(Q7)** Number input only for Competition Score 1-100 (no slider in v1). **(Q8)** Status column = bidirectional mirror of URL detail page's Scraping Status toggle (default-locked via Default-to-recommendation; both reads/writes go through `CompetitorUrl.scrapingStatus` enum). **(Q9)** "Select preview thumbnail" button DROPPED from P-46 entirely; automatic thumbnail capture stays as-is. **(Q10)** Workstream sequencing: Schema → URL detail page → Competition Data table → Comprehensive Analysis page → Extension URL save form additions + vklf.com Reviews entry form (most-value-first; capture-path lands last). **Cross-references:** **P-27 captured-videos** (shared metadata pattern — category / composition / embedded text / tags / clientId-dedup; P-46 extends these to vklf.com-side editing + adds per-item Analysis fields); **P-45 screen recording** (the "select preview thumbnail" rectangle-draw is structurally identical to P-45's region-selection rectangle-draw — both can share `region-screenshot-overlay.ts` / future `video-region-record-overlay.ts`; P-46 design session should happen AFTER P-45 ships per sequencing picker); **P-17 region-screenshot** (the existing overlay infrastructure that P-46's thumbnail-picker + P-45's recording-region both build on); Sizes/Options at line 740 to remove) + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/url-add-form.ts` + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/UrlAddForm.tsx` (extension URL save form to extend with new fields);
