All subsequent P-46 workstream sessions stay "No" assuming schema lands in Workstream 1. **Director's verbatim scope-drop directive captured in session transcript** `session_2026-05-22-c_p27-build-9-devtools-diagnosis-and-p45-screen-recording-pivot` (the message starting "Right now, when the 'Competition Scraping & Deep Analysis' card is clicked..." — long message ~3KB covering all 7 scope areas + the 10+ additional notes). **Design doc CREATED 2026-05-23:** `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` (parallels `CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` shape — §A frozen interview answers (10 Q+A) + §B append-only refinements log (empty at end of interview) + §C per-workstream implementation outlines (5 subsections one per workstream: Schema / URL detail page / Competition Data table / Comprehensive Analysis page / Extension URL save form + vklf.com Reviews entry).

- **P-44** (NEW 2026-05-20-c; ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-h** via build commit `57ed383` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`) `wxt build` + `wxt zip` parent-process hang — recurring across multiple sessions (2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g + 2026-05-21 + 2026-05-20-c). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/` build path via `npm run build` → `wxt build` AND `npm run zip` → `wxt zip` (Rule 24 scope-expansion to include `zip` — same root cause; same symptom; both invoked by the deploy pipeline). **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-h** — closed via build commit `57ed383` (5 files changed +61/-11). **Empirical root cause confirmed:** wxt 0.20.25 → Vite 8.0.11 → Rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 (the new Rust-based bundler that replaced Rollup in Vite 8). The native Rolldown binaries (`@rolldown/binding-linux-x64-gnu` etc.) spawn worker threads that aren't released when the build promise resolves, leaving Node's event loop with active handles. wxt's CLI awaits the build then relies on natural event-loop drain — which never happens. Confirmed by reading wxt's CLI internals (`node_modules/wxt/dist/cli/commands.mjs`, `core/build.mjs`, `core/builders/vite/index.mjs`) — `isRolldownVersion(vite.version)` returns `true` for Vite ≥8; Vite 8.0.11's package.json confirms rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 dependency + native binding presence; wxt 0.20.26 + 0.21.0 changelogs reviewed via GitHub releases — no relevant fix upstream; Vite upstream issue #18224 documents related "Vite can hang the node.js process" pattern. **Fix shipped this session:** NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` (16 LOC programmatic-API wrapper — `import { build } from 'wxt'; await build(); process.exit(0);` with browser-flag passthrough + try/catch for non-zero exit on error) + NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (16 LOC same shape but calls `zip()` instead of `build()`); both force-exit with `process.exit(0)` after the promise resolves, bypassing the broken event-loop drain entirely — safe because all userland work has completed by the time we exit (dist artifacts on disk, success log printed, zip artifact written). `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` 4 scripts rewired through the wrappers: `"build": "node scripts/wxt-build.mjs"` + `"build:firefox": "node scripts/wxt-build.mjs -b firefox"` + `"zip": "node scripts/wxt-zip.mjs"` + `"zip:firefox": "node scripts/wxt-zip.mjs -b firefox"`. `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` KNOWN ISSUE blocks replaced with one-paragraph notes that the wrapper now handles the hang + points readers at `scripts/wxt-build.mjs` if a hang regresses; deploy.md preserves the historical CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-references (2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g) and adds a new 2026-05-22-h pointer. **Rule 24 scope expansion to `wxt zip`:** the ROADMAP entry named the bug narrowly as `wxt build` parent-process hang; the fix expanded to ALSO cover `wxt zip` because `wxt zip` internally calls `internalBuild()` from the same `core/utils/building/internal-build.mjs` (verified by reading `core/zip.mjs`); both commands exhibit the identical hang signature for the identical root cause; both are invoked from `npm run` scripts; fixing build but not zip would have left the deploy session's Step 8 (fresh extension zip) still hanging. Symmetric fix across both wrappers is the most-thorough Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation choice. **Pre-fix reproduction:** `rm -rf .output && timeout 45 npm run build` hung past 45s (exit 124 = timeout); dist visible at +6s; process still alive at +36s. **Post-fix verification (from `extensions/competition-scraping/`):** `npm run build` cold (after `rm -rf .output`) — 4s, exit 0, dist valid; `npm run zip` cold — 3s, exit 0, 202,601-byte zip artifact valid at `.output/competition-scraping-extension-0.1.0-chrome.zip`; `npm run build` warm (no clean) — 4s, exit 0; 3x rapid-fire `npm run build` — 3s each, all exit 0 (rules out flakiness). Pre-end-of-session /scoreboard ran from `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub`: Check 1 root tsc clean / Check 2 extension tsc clean / Check 3 extension `npm test` **558/558** / Check 4 src/lib node:test **590/590** / Check 5 `npm run build` **57 routes** / Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED. All GREEN at exact baselines. **Reusable Pattern memorialized in CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-22-h:** (a) when a Node CLI tool hangs after its primary work completes, suspect lingering worker threads / native bindings holding event-loop handles open; (b) check whether the CLI exports a programmatic API — if yes, wrapping it with `await xyz(); process.exit(0);` bypasses the broken event-loop drain entirely; (c) safe to force-exit AFTER artifact verification (file-on-disk / log emitted) — the hang happens during shutdown, not during the work itself; (d) parallel `npm run` script rewires (`build` + `build:firefox` + `zip` + `zip:firefox`) keep the wrapper symmetric across browsers + production/zip variants. **CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-reference:** new §Entry 2026-05-22-h closing P-44 with the empirical root-cause narrative + reusable Pattern + a sub-observation about a second-reproduction Claude-discipline slip during inline-Bash typing (not a slip-of-P-44 — it's a P-43-bug-class reproduction at the Claude typing layer that template hardening doesn't protect against). **ORIGINAL CAPTURE narrative preserved below for traceability:** recurring across multiple sessions (2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g + 2026-05-21 + 2026-05-20-c). **Where (original):** `extensions/competition-scraping/` build path via `npm run build` → `wxt build`; possibly a wxt + Vite + esbuild interaction; possibly a Node.js version interaction; possibly a Codespaces-environment quirk. **Captured 2026-05-20-c** as the formal ROADMAP entry; multiple prior CORRECTIONS_LOG entries reference the behavior. **Pre-capture Rule 24 search performed (prior treatment found — only as informational CORRECTIONS_LOG references; no forward polish item):** grepped `wxt.zip|wxt build hang|wxt build parent|wxt build dist|wxt build kill` across ROADMAP + CORRECTIONS_LOG + HANDOFF_PROTOCOL — multiple CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entries (2026-05-19-f, 2026-05-19-g) describe the hang behavior + workaround; the `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` skill mentions the workaround in its KNOWN ISSUE section; no prior ROADMAP entry capturing this as a forward investigation item. **Investigation shape (estimated ~1 session for diagnosis; ship time TBD based on root cause):** (1) Reproduce the hang reliably in a clean session; (2) capture `wxt build` debug output via `WXT_LOG=debug npm run build` or similar; (3) check if the hang is wxt-side (post-build cleanup never runs) vs. Vite-side (rollup post-bundle phase) vs. esbuild-side (worker pool not closing); (4) check Node.js version against wxt's supported range; (5) check for known issues at https://github.com/wxt-dev/wxt; (6) ship a fix — either upgrade wxt version / add a flag / wrap the build with a kill-after-dist-visible script. **Cross-references:** CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-f §Entry + 2026-05-19-g §Entry (the slip narratives + workaround); 2026-05-22-h §Entry (the closing entry capturing the empirical root cause + the reusable Pattern); `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` "KNOWN ISSUE" sections (the documented workaround — now replaced with wrapper-notes); `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` (the wxt version + script rewires); `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` + `scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (the NEW programmatic-API wrappers shipped this session); `extensions/competition-scraping/wxt.config.ts` (the wxt config). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** N/A — extension build tooling only; no schema, no API, no shared-types.

- **P-41** (NEW 2026-05-20) Codespaces rebuild memory-loss prevention + 4-layer recovery architecture — **Status:** ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-20** (shipped + verified inline this session — the verification IS the test we ran today). **Where:** new `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 29 "Pre-destructive-container-operation audit" (inserted between Rule 28 and §4) + new `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER.md` Rule 8 augment paragraph naming Codespaces rebuild as a destructive container operation + new `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` (SessionStart canary) + new `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (PostToolUse mirror) + new `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` (pre-rebuild audit) + new `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh` (one-command recovery, idempotent, --force flag) + new `.codespace-backup/` directory (persistent-volume backup architecture with tracked README.md + .gitkeep) + updated `.claude/settings.json` (wires the 2 new hooks alongside existing track-edited-docs.sh + inject-next-session-pointer.sh + check-next-session-doc.sh) + updated `.gitignore` (gitignore `.codespace-backup/*` except README.md and .gitkeep). **Severity:** HIGH (preventable slip class — Claude's persistent operational memory was silently wiped on a recommended walkthrough; without the protective architecture this can recur on any future destructive container operation). **Why:** the 2026-05-21 P-18 verification session recommended a Codespaces "Rebuild Container" walkthrough to verify P-18 WITHOUT auditing what the rebuild would wipe. The rebuild succeeded at P-18 verification (91/91 PASS in 1.8 min with ZERO manual lib install — postCreateCommand confirmed firing on rebuild) but silently wiped Claude's persistent operational memory directory at `/home/codespace/.claude/projects/-workspaces-brand-operations-hub/memory/` (~10 cross-session operational-memory files: `feedback_recommendation_style.md`, `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`, `feedback_trust_director_setup_confirmation.md`, `feedback_playwright_for_repeatable_walkthroughs.md`, `feedback_deferred_items_registry.md`, `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md`, `project_sequential_workflow_operation.md`, `project_scaffold_pivot_to_components_library.md`, `project_resume_script_design_scheduled.md`, plus `MEMORY.md` index) PLUS the Claude Code CLI binary itself (director had to manually reinstall + reauthenticate before this session could start). **NO PLOS or vklf.com work was lost** — all PLOS state lives in git + GitHub remote + Vercel + Supabase, all preserved. The loss was Claude's own internal operational memory + dev tooling — meta, not product. Director caught the slip post-rebuild + requested permanent protective scaffolding. **Fix shape (4-layer architecture — composed defenses):** **MECHANICAL** — PostToolUse backup hook mirrors every memory-dir write into `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` (persistent volume; survives rebuild). No Claude memory required; happens automatically on every Edit + Write whose target is inside the memory dir. **PROCEDURAL** — HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md Rule 29 + the `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` audit script + a Rule 14f forced-picker (A: backup-first recommended / B: proceed without backup / C: cancel / D: escape hatch) that MUST fire before any destructive container operation. **DETECTION** — SessionStart canary hook (`.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh`) alerts via additionalContext if memory ends up empty/partial-loss despite the other layers; runs at startup for every session. **RECOVERY** — `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh` one-command recovery (idempotent; --force flag; refuses to overwrite populated dest without --force; restores files in sequence; reports success + count); falls through to reconstruction-from-HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md if the backup is also missing. **Verification this session (the entry IS the test):** SessionStart canary tested with healthy / missing / partial-loss → emits correct alerts. PostToolUse backup hook tested with a memory-dir file edit (mirrors correctly into `.codespace-backup/memory/<basename>`) + a non-memory-dir file edit (no-op, as designed). Restore script tested in safe-mode (refuses to overwrite) + force-mode (restores all 10 files; diff vs. source = identical). `codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` tested in this Codespace → produces structured 7-section report listing memory dir + global npm packages + ~/.local/bin/ + apt extras + credentials + shell profile + VS Code extensions. **Pre-deploy scoreboard met at exact 2026-05-20 baselines (post-rebuild):** tsc / ext tsc / `npm run build` 53 routes / src/lib node:test 536/536 / extension `npm test` 428/428 / Playwright 91/91 in 1.8 min — ALL UNCHANGED vs. baseline (tooling + docs + procedural rule only; no source change). **Cross-references:** `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 29 (the procedural rule + Pre-Container-Audit checklist + Rule 14f picker shape + recovery playbook); `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER.md` Rule 8 (augment paragraph naming Codespaces rebuild as destructive); `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` (SessionStart canary); `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (PostToolUse mirror); `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` (pre-rebuild audit); `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh` (recovery script); `.codespace-backup/README.md` (backup-architecture documentation); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-20 §Entry (full slip narrative + impact + "what would have caught this" analysis); ROADMAP P-18 entry above (the verification PASS that surfaced the silent-wipe slip class as a side effect).

- **P-48** (NEW 2026-05-24-f; DIAGNOSED 2026-05-25; DEPLOYED-PARTIAL 2026-05-25) Screen recording playback stutters / frame drops on vklf.com — **partially resolved:** Sessions 1-2 shipped the `fix-webm-duration` patch end-to-end (seek bar now shows real total duration; director-verified post-deploy), BUT empirical ffprobe post-Session-2 revealed every SCREEN_RECORDING webm is captured at ~6-7 fps actual rate (not 30 fps); the stutter persists because the source files themselves are low-fps, not because of missing duration metadata; speculative fix-forward #1 (attach hidden video element to DOM) empirically BUSTED; Session 3 (Diagnostic #2) opens with empirical instrumentation before any more structural fixes ship. **Status:** **✅ DEPLOYED-PARTIAL 2026-05-25 (Sessions 1 + 2 done; seek bar fixed; stutter NOT resolved — opens Session 3 Diagnostic #2 per (a.91)).** **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/screen-recording/record-controller.ts` (line 455-458 is where `MediaRecorder` is constructed; line 42 is where `VIDEO_BITS_PER_SECOND_DEFAULT = 2_500_000` is exported; Session 2 added the `fix-webm-duration` injection in `emitStoppedOnce` async; FF-1 added DOM-attachment in `productionCropStreamToRegion`). **WHY (revised post-Session-2 2026-05-25):** Director-observed screen-recording playback stutters on vklf.com originally framed as one symptom; Sessions 1-2 revealed it's actually TWO sub-symptoms with different root causes. **Sub-symptom (a) seek bar broken (✅ RESOLVED Session 2):** Session 1's `ffprobe` + EBML inspection identified missing WebM duration metadata in MediaRecorder-produced container headers as root cause (every file shows `Duration: N/A`; canonical MediaRecorder limitation — EBML header written at recording-START before duration is known, never patched after `stop()`); Session 2's `fix-webm-duration` npm dep + DI-seam wiring in `record-controller.ts` injects the JS-known duration into the EBML Segment Info Duration tag post-stop / pre-`onStopped`; director-verified end-to-end on vklf.com post-deploy (recordings made post-Session-2-deploy now show real total duration like "0:22"). **Sub-symptom (b) playback stutters (✅ NOT RESOLVED — Session 3 next):** Phase-4 verification post-Session-2-deploy revealed playback stutter PERSISTS even with duration metadata now correct. Empirical ffprobe across 4 sample recordings showed every SCREEN_RECORDING webm file (since P-45 shipped 2026-05-22-i) is captured at ~6-7 fps actual playback rate, NOT the intended 30 fps — pre-fix file 1 = 106 frames / 15.616s = **6.79 fps**; pre-fix file 2 = 154 frames / 22.69s = **6.79 fps**; post-fix file = 95 frames / 15.30s = **6.21 fps**. Playback can't be smoother than the source. The actual root cause of the stutter is low-fps source files, NOT missing duration metadata. Sessions 1-2 fixed a real sub-symptom (seek bar) but the original "playback stutters" symptom had multiple contributing causes. **Speculative fix-forward #1 (✅ EMPIRICALLY BUSTED):** Session 2's FF-1 build commit `d8b9507` (1 file +25/-2) attached the hidden `<video>` element in `productionCropStreamToRegion` to `document.body` with 1×1 opacity-0 styling on the theory Chrome throttles detached video elements to ~6-7 fps. Post-FF-1 ffprobe: 155 frames / 18.19s = **8.52 fps** — within ffprobe-measurement noise; theory BUSTED. FF-1 commit STAYS in production (no rollback) per director's Rule 14f forced-picker choice — preserves the failed-theory commit as a calibration data point. **Severity:** **LOW–MEDIUM.** Feature is functional (videos play; seek bar now correct); UX still degraded by the stutter; not blocking any workstream graduation. **Fix scope (revised post-Session-2 2026-05-25):** **Session 1 (Diagnostic) ✅ DONE 2026-05-25** — `ffprobe` + EBML inspection + curl HEAD/Range tests; root cause for sub-symptom (a) seek bar locked as missing WebM duration metadata. **Session 2 (Implementation + FF-1 Busted) ✅ DEPLOYED-PARTIAL 2026-05-25** — build commit `279da34` (4 files +211/-9): `fix-webm-duration ^1.0.6` runtime dep in `package.json` + DI-seam wiring (`RecordControllerDeps.fixWebmDuration` optional) in `record-controller.ts` + `emitStoppedOnce` async with try/catch fallback to unpatched blob on error + 4 new node:test cases in `record-controller.test.ts` (test count 558 → 562 ext); shipped under Rule 9 gate #1; ff-merge `a486af8..279da34` to main; director-verified seek-bar fix end-to-end on vklf.com. Speculative FF-1 build commit `d8b9507` (1 file +25/-2; DOM-attachment theory) shipped under Rule 9 gate #2 + ff-merge `279da34..d8b9507`; empirically BUSTED via post-deploy ffprobe (8.52 fps; within noise). **Session 3 (Diagnostic #2, OPEN per (a.91)):** empirical instrumentation pass to identify the ~6-7 fps source-file bottleneck BEFORE shipping any more structural fixes (per the "ffprobe-first" Pattern from Session 1 reinforced by FF-1 empirical bust). Likely scope: add per-frame instrumentation to extension content-script — log `track.getSettings().frameRate` from getDisplayMedia (source-side actual rate) + count VideoFrames at source vs after canvas-crop pipeline using `MediaStreamTrackProcessor` if available + observe `canvas.captureStream(30)` output rate + observe MediaRecorder ondataavailable timer + tabulate to identify whether the bottleneck is at getDisplayMedia source / video element throttling / canvas.captureStream / MediaRecorder encoder; ship as a feature-flagged or dev-only branch; director records 1-2 NEW recordings with instrumentation; Claude reads logs + identifies empirical bottleneck; locks Session 4 implementation scope. Estimated 30-60 min in-Claude. Schema-change-in-flight: NO. **NOTE:** only NEW recordings made AFTER Session 2's deploy benefit from the seek-bar fix; the 3 pre-fix webm files in DB keep their broken metadata at the file layer. **Cross-reference:** P-45 (screen recording feature — DONE 2026-05-22-i) is the feature this polish item degrades. Pairs with P-23 (capture videos from competitor pages) which uses a different capture pipeline (drag-and-drop) — director confirmed today's lag is specific to P-45 not P-23, so P-23 stays out of scope. CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-25 (Session 1 Diagnostic closing entry — 5 sub-observations including NEW reusable Pattern "ffprobe-first diagnostic reveals hypothesis-bust before any code lands") + CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-25 (Session 2 Implementation + FF-1 Busted closing entry — 5 sub-observations including NEW reusable Pattern "Speculative fix-forward without empirical pre-verification = antipattern"). COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B 2026-05-25 (Session 1 diagnostic findings entry) + §B 2026-05-25 (Session 2 implementation + Phase-4 PARTIAL + FF-1 BUSTED entry). **(a.89) closed ✅ DIAGNOSED 2026-05-25 (Session 1); (a.90) closes ✅ DEPLOYED-PARTIAL 2026-05-25 (Session 2); (a.91) opens RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-48 Session 3 (Diagnostic #2) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** **Build commits this polish item:** `279da34` (Session 2 build) + `d8b9507` (Session 2 FF-1 BUSTED). **Schema-change-in-flight:** NO. UI / capture-config + tiny runtime dep only.

**ORIGINAL CAPTURE PRESERVED FOR TRACEABILITY (2026-05-24-f — hypothesis since FALSIFIED 2026-05-25):** *Screen recording playback stutters / frame drops on vklf.com — investigate + cap capture bitrate (+ delivery-side checks). **Where (busted):** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/screen-recorder.ts` (path was a drift — actual file is `screen-recording/record-controller.ts`). **Why (busted):** Most likely root-cause class is real-time decode budget exceeded due to uncapped MediaRecorder bitrate at capture; secondary candidates are delivery-side range-request gaps + container format. **Session 2 fix path (busted):** capture-side bitrate cap — set `videoBitsPerSecond: 2_500_000` (2.5 Mbps) on MediaRecorder + cap `getDisplayMedia` to `frameRate: { max: 30 }` + `width: { max: 1920 }, height: { max: 1080 }`. ~30 LOC in `screen-recorder.ts`.* Session 1 diagnostic ruled this out empirically — cap was already shipped + actual files were well below the cap. The real root cause is container-metadata, not capture-rate.

- **P-49** (NEW 2026-05-25; CORRECTIVE REBUILD 2026-05-28 after wrong-spec rollback) **Reviews Phase 2 — automated per-platform review collection (Amazon + eBay + Etsy + Walmart) via extension and/or crawler, plus three-level AI-driven review analysis system (per-product → cross-Type → cross-everything competitive landscape).** Long-promised follow-up to the A.1 deferred decision in `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` (2026-05-23): *"The per-platform Reviews-extraction mechanism is **DEFERRED to future per-platform polish sessions**."* Today's director directive (2026-05-25) surfaces the full scope cluster in one shot. **SPEC DOCS (Rule 31 canonical source-of-truth — read §3 of each at session start; auto-loaded by `.claude/hooks/inject-next-session-pointer.sh` when P-49 is referenced in NEXT_SESSION.md per the 2026-05-28-b mechanical-layer extension):** `docs/polish-item-specs/P-49-W5-reviews-phase-2-master-spec.md` (NEW 2026-05-28-b — MASTER SPEC carrying the verbatim FULL director re-paste covering all 3 Reviews Phase 2 pages + cross-cutting joint-discussion decisions; per-page specs derive their §1 from this master) + `docs/polish-item-specs/P-49-W5-S2-S3-competitor-reviews-analysis.md` (NEW 2026-05-28-b — BACKFILL for the already-shipped Competitor Reviews Analysis Table page; covers W5 Sessions 2 + 3 ship state + 12 divergence findings from §1 verbatim + 7 answered clarifying questions + 3-session corrective-fix plan locked Fix A + Fix B + Fix C) + `docs/polish-item-specs/P-49-W5-S4-category-page.md` (Reviews Analysis By Competitor Category Table — Sessions 1-3 of the 5-session corrective rebuild) + `docs/polish-item-specs/P-49-W5-S5-type-page.md` (Reviews Analysis By Competitor Type Table — Sessions 4-5 of the corrective rebuild). The corrective rebuild was triggered by the 2026-05-28 scope-misread incident: build commit `5fa1f53` shipped a wrong-spec single-page "By Category-Type" surface (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-28 pending capture) that was hard-reverted via revert commit `958ccf8` on `main` + `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; director's verbatim correct spec lives in the two spec docs above. NEW Rule 31 (HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md) makes this verbatim-instructions-capture mechanism mandatory for all future polish items with detailed director instructions. **Status:** **✅ CLOSED 2026-06-01 — P-49 W5 Reviews Phase 2 corrective rebuild COMPLETE. The Type page — `Reviews Analysis By Competitor Type Table` (`/competition-scraping/reviews-analysis-by-type`) — shipped in ONE all-in-one session (director chose all-in-one over the Session-4/Session-5 split) ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-06-01 end-to-end on vklf.com via `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` — director Phase 4 PASS (both per-Type AI flows verified, including the non-bulleted button). With it, BOTH the Category page (closed 2026-05-30-d) and the Type page are shipped + verified, so P-49 W5 — the last remaining P-49 unit — is CLOSED. NOTE: this session STARTED 2026-05-31 and the harness rolled to 2026-06-01 during the build; the session is stamped 2026-06-01 (the date-boundary crossing is recorded in CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-06-01). A SEPARATE fabricated/reverted session earlier on 2026-05-31 is recorded ONLY as the CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-05-31 TOP-TIER SLIP and is NOT a normal prior session. Build commit `f23df1b` (the all-in-one Type page build); `main` went `cb00f53 → f23df1b` (clean ff-merge); both `main` and `workflow-2-competition-scraping` at `f23df1b`. The Type page is a LEAN mirror of the now-closed Category page: grouping key `competitionCategory` → `type`, an `(Untyped)` fallback bucket, and the intentional Type↔Category column SWAP (Type = column 1, Category = column 3). It was born with EVERYTHING: the 13-column grouped table + show/hide + click-to-edit + per-review stacked Stars/Reviews Summary + the interactive batch (type banner rows + two-level @dnd-kit drag + hide-with-restore) + the per-page layout memory (NEW additive nullable `UserTablePreferences.typeTableLayout Json?` column; `prisma db push` to prod, director-approved per Rule 8, zero data loss) + BOTH per-Type AI flows (bulleted dedup + non-bulleted prose, both director-verified) + the Source Reviews column + all 4 of the 2026-05-30-d director adjustments. **LEAN architecture (KEY pattern):** rather than duplicating ~450 lines, the build REUSED the grouping-agnostic helpers (`category-analysis-aggregation`, `buildCategorySourceReviewRows`, the per-category prompt parsers) and GENERALIZED the `review-analysis-run-batch` per-category branch to also serve the per-type flows (parameterized by grouping `level` PER_TYPE + the type-worded `PER_TYPE_BULLETED/NONBULLETED` prompts + a `typeKey` group-key alias) — the per-category path stays byte-identical. future build sessions touch `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/` (per-platform review-extraction modules + right-click context-menu wiring + DOM walkers per platform) + possible new crawler infrastructure (TBD per design-session anti-bot decision) + `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/url/[urlId]/components/UrlDetailContent.tsx` (Captured Reviews UI extensions: star-count breakdown counters at top of section, star-filter, server-side drag-to-reorder within-star + across-star, bulk-delete affordance) + `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (likely home for cross-Type + cross-everything AI analysis surfaces; OR new dedicated page per design decision) + new AI analysis trigger API routes under `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/` + likely new Prisma schema (for AI analysis outputs; TBD per design). **WHY:** Reviews are a key competitive-intel signal; existing P-46 W2 Session 4 (shipped 2026-05-28) ships the data shape + manual entry path; today's Phase 2 adds automation across BOTH collection (per-platform extension + optional crawler) AND analysis (LLM-driven summarization at 3 aggregation levels). **Director's verbatim per-platform specs (preserved here for design session ingestion):** **Amazon** — listing page `https://www.amazon.com/.../dp/<ASIN>`; capture `Customers say` AI summary block + Top reviews US + Top reviews other countries from listing page; per-star URL `https://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/<ASIN>/ref=cm_cr_arp_d_viewopt_sr?...&filterByStar=critical&pageNumber=N`; per-star pagination at 10 reviews/page via "View 10 more reviews" button click-loop; capture star + title + body + helpful-count ("x people found this helpful"); sort by helpful-count desc within star; default 200/star scrape cap user-adjustable. **eBay** — listing page `https://www.ebay.com/itm/<ID>`; feedback URL `https://www.ebay.com/fdbk/mweb_profile?fdbkType=FeedbackReceivedAsSeller&item_id=<ID>&username=<SELLER>&...&overall_rating_item=NEUTRAL` (or `=NEGATIVE`) at ~25 reviews/page paginated; capture review body only; map Neutral → 3-star + Negative → 1-star automatically (eBay doesn't have per-star granular feedback); default 200/filter scrape cap. **Etsy** — listing page `https://www.etsy.com/listing/<ID>/...`; `Reviews for this item` section on listing page + `View all reviews for this item` overlay with per-star percentage filter affordances at top-right (hover to see %, click to filter); ~8 reviews/page in overlay paginated; capture review body only; capture 3+2+1 star reviews by default at 200/star user-adjustable. **Walmart** — listing page `https://www.walmart.com/ip/<NAME>/<ID>`; per-star query-param URL `https://www.walmart.com/reviews/product/<ID>?ratings=N` (multi-star via repeated `&ratings=M` params) at 10 reviews/page paginated; capture star + title + full-expanded body (click "View more" expander on long reviews to reveal full text); default 200/star scrape cap user-adjustable. **Critical anti-bot constraint per director:** *"Both methods should ensure that we don't get flagged by amazon for going against their terms or doing something wrong. That means our functionality should be very close to real world human user sitting where admin is."* Extension-method runs in director's logged-in browser session at director's IP (low risk on all 4 platforms; behaviorally indistinguishable from a power user). Crawler-method (headless server-IP) is **high-risk on Amazon specifically** (aggressive anti-bot detection; captchas; IP bans; possible ToS strikes against seller account if Amazon associates scraping with director's account); lower but real risk on Walmart/Etsy; lowest on eBay. Realistic crawler implementation typically requires residential proxy rotation + browser-fingerprint randomization + captcha-solving services + careful rate-limiting — significant ongoing cost + complexity. Decision on crawler scope deferred to design session; default expectation per most-thorough/reliable reasoning is extension-method only with crawler dropped or deeply deferred. **AI-driven review analysis (three levels per director):** (1) **per-product two-sweep summarization** — first sweep produces per-batch summaries of manageable review subsets; second sweep merges batch summaries into a comprehensive per-product summary; user can scope to a specific star-count subset or all reviews together. (2) **cross-Type pooled analysis** — same review-analysis system run across all products sharing a `type` value (the Competition Data column shipped via P-46 W3 2026-05-23-e); produces a comprehensive summary per Type. (3) **cross-everything analysis (competitive landscape)** — same system pooled across all Types within a Project; produces a full competitive-landscape review report. Existing AI infrastructure to potentially mirror: PLOS Auto-Analyze for keyword clustering (`docs/AUTO_ANALYZE_PROMPT_V*.md` × 4 versions, `docs/MODEL_QUALITY_SCORING.md`, `docs/INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md`) — has two-sweep patterns relevant here. **Severity:** **HIGH** (director-prioritized as the next round of additions; foundational to W#2's competitive-intel value proposition; locks W#2 graduation step until Phase 2 closes at the workstream-by-workstream level). **Fix scope (post-design 2026-05-25-b: 4 ACTIVE internal workstreams; W5 AI review analysis Session 1 NEXT per (a.101)).** 4 build clusters (one per platform: Amazon → eBay → Etsy → Walmart, in that director-stated priority per A.2). Each cluster: per-platform module wiring in `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/`, right-click "Scrape reviews for this URL" context-menu entry, DOM walker per platform's specific review-page structure, paginated "load more" automation honoring per-page review counts (Amazon 10/page, eBay 25/page, Etsy 8/page, Walmart 10/page) with conservative 1-3s random delays + captcha-aware abort + rate-limit UI notification per A.15, Supabase write through existing `CapturedReview` model with `source = 'extension-scrape'` (new source enum value vs. current `'manual'` per A.13), user-configurable scrape cap (per-URL via new `CompetitorUrl.reviewScrapeCap` Int default 200 column per A.4 + per-trigger override), Shadow DOM progress indicator on the page per A.3 (reuses P-47 mount pattern), sort by helpful-count where available. Estimated ~2-4 sessions per platform = ~8-16 sessions total. **Amazon Session 1 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-26** shipped via build commit `422436f` (20 files +2069/-7): schema migration (the §A.13/§A.16 package: new `ReviewAnalysis` model + `ReviewAnalysisLevel` enum PER_PRODUCT/PER_TYPE/PER_PROJECT + `CapturedReview.sortRank Int?` + `.helpfulCount Int?` + `.platform String?` + `CompetitorUrl.reviewScrapeCap Int? @default(200)`) via `npx prisma db push` (1.44s; additive only; zero data loss); NEW shared `scrape-pagination.ts` (~310 LOC; A.15 conservative defaults + AbortSignal-cancellable + structured `ScrapeProgress` event stream); NEW `scrape-progress-indicator.ts` (~230 LOC; Shadow DOM-mounted corner indicator reusing P-47 2026-05-24-d mount pattern); NEW `amazon-review-extractor.ts` (~300 LOC; per-row DOM walker against `[data-hook="review"]` selectors + `runAmazonReviewScrape` orchestrator using `fetch()` + `DOMParser` for pagination); right-click context-menu wiring in `background.ts` + `messaging.ts` + `orchestrator.ts`; PLOS-side handler extensions additive to P-46 W2 S4 baseline. 49 new extension node:test cases (`scrape-pagination.test.ts` +20 + `amazon-review-extractor.test.ts` +29). NEW baseline locked: extension `npm test` = **611/611** (+49 from baseline 562); src/lib 786/786 UNCHANGED; routes 62/62 UNCHANGED. **Amazon Session 2 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-27** shipped via build commit `1830074` (5 files +1054/-99): major refactor of `amazon-review-extractor.ts` (+419/-93) — NEW `AMAZON_STAR_FILTERS` const + `AmazonStarFilter` type (one_star..five_star) + NEW `starRatingForFilter` / `buildAmazonStarFilterUrl` / `buildAmazonProductListingUrl` helpers; `runAmazonReviewScrape` refactored from "scrape current view" to ASIN-driven cross-star loop (pages 1..N all fetched via fetch+DOMParser since the cross-star loop visits multiple star filters, none of which are the live document — extension of Session 1's fetch+DOMParser pagination Pattern); `AmazonScrapeContext` shape changed (`cap` → `capPerStar`; NEW `asin` + optional `starsToVisit`); `AmazonScrapeResult` gains `insertedByStar` + `customersSayInserted`; captcha + rate-limit abort the whole scrape per §A.15 anti-escalation; NEW `sortByHelpfulCountDesc` helper (stable; null sorts last; doesn't mutate input) applied per-star before saveReview; NEW `extractCustomersSayFromListing` helper (4 selector fallbacks: `[data-hook="cr-insights-widget"]` canonical + 3 alternates); NEW `scrapeOneStar` + `scrapeCustomersSayBlock` private orchestration helpers; `AmazonScrapeSaveInput.source` widened to discriminated union `'extension-scrape' | 'extension-scrape:customers-say'`. `orchestrator.ts` (+59/-37) — `start-review-scrape` handler refactored to extract ASIN via existing helper + fire `openScrapeTriggerModal` BEFORE dispatching to `runAmazonReviewScrape` + build new context shape (asin + capPerStar from modal output) + saveReview wrapper passes `input.source` through to `createCapturedReview` so both per-reviewer + Customers-say discriminators reach the API; modal cancel returns silently. NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/scrape-trigger-modal.ts` (320 LOC) — Shadow DOM-mounted modal with per-star-cap numeric input pre-filled with saved per-URL `reviewScrapeCap` default (open shadow root on a fixed-positioned host div reuses P-47 mount pattern — SECOND consumer of the P-47 Pattern after Session 1's progress indicator); returns Promise resolving to `{ capPerStar }` on Start OR null on Cancel / Escape / backdrop click; NEW `clampCap` pure helper exported for unit testing (MIN_CAP=1, MAX_CAP=5000; floors decimals; returns 200 for non-finite). Phase 4 director real-Chrome verification surfaced TWO co-occurring bugs (seller auto-detect failing because modern eBay listing pages don't render `/usr/<seller>` links in the visible DOM — seller is embedded in a JSON data island as `"sellerUserName":"<value>"`; AND This Item filter returning All Items rows because the feedback page renders both This Item + All Items tabpanels server-side with the All Items panel carrying the `hidden` HTML attribute — walker was matching rows from both panels) that survived THREE speculative fix-forward commits: **FF#1+#2 bundled `23b6221`** (4 files +183/-30; +4 ext tests) trigger modal extended with optional eBay seller text input (auto-detect prefill; manual paste fallback when listing-page extraction fails) + `buildEbayFeedbackUrl` adds `q=<itemId>` + `filter=feedback_page:RECEIVED_AS_SELLER` (director's working URL params); **FF#3 `c19f187`** (2 files +58/-25; 0 new tests) full `_item`-suffixed param set replaces `_pgn` pagination (`page_id_item=N` + `sort_item=RELEVANCEV2` + `filter_image_item=false` + `filter_video_item=false` + `filter_automated_feedback_item=true` + `filter_topic_item=`) — both BUSTED — still returned All Items. Per the director-picked Rule 14f outcome ("Diagnostic instrumentation (Recommended)") after FF#3 BUSTED, **FF#4 `6675963`** (2 files +116/-0) shipped TEMPORARY diagnostic instrumentation dumping fetched HTML to director's Downloads folder via programmatic `<a download>` click + console.logs structured row-count breakdowns per selector probe; director ran ONE scrape → uploaded 3 HTML files (listing + NEGATIVE feedback page 1 + NEUTRAL feedback page 1) + pasted console output. Analysis of dumped HTML files revealed BOTH root causes simultaneously, producing **FF#5 `23aa851` EMPIRICALLY-VERIFIED FIX — the WIN** (3 files +176/-135; +8 ext tests — 6 sellerFromListingHtml + 2 tabpanel-scoping): NEW `extractSellerFromListingHtml(html)` regex extraction from raw HTML against the `"sellerUserName":"<value>"` JSON data island (legacy DOM-based `extractSellerFromListingDocument` preserved as classic-view fallback) + scope walker to `[role=tabpanel]:not([hidden])` (falls through to whole-doc scope as classic-view safety net) + removes ALL FF#4 diagnostic instrumentation. **Director Phase 4 PASS verdict on FF#5 = "Everything worked perfectly."** RESOLVES the eBay sub-cluster end-to-end on vklf.com. Phase 4 director real-Chrome verification surfaced that the URL-construction approach was BUSTED — Etsy's "View all reviews for this item" overlay loads via AJAX on the same listing URL with NO separate URL for filters or pagination. **Architecture pivot via FF#1 `67aeacd`** (2 files +1058/-705) rewrote `etsy-review-extractor.ts` from URL-construction to live-DOM driver (click View-all-reviews button → wait for overlay → click histogram filter → wait for AJAX content swap → walk rows → click pagination Next → wait → repeat) based on director's paste of the 41 KB overlay outerHTML — the empirical evidence that broke the speculative-FF cycle EARLY this time (3 fix-forwards total vs eBay's 5 + Amazon's 4); empirically-grounded selectors: `.deep-dive-sheet` + `[data-deep-dive-reviews-container="true"]` + `[data-review-region]` + `.wt-text-body` + `a[href*="/people/"]` + `[role="img"][aria-label^="Rating:"]` + `[data-reviews-histogram="true"] button[data-rating-value="N"]` + `nav[aria-label="Pagination of reviews"]`. FF#1 BUSTED silently (scrape ended with 0 captures + no error toast). Per the director-picked Rule 14f outcome ("Diagnostic instrumentation (Recommended)") after FF#1 BUSTED, **FF#2 `a3107b6`** (1 file +163/-2) shipped TEMPORARY diagnostic instrumentation: `[PLOS ETSY DIAGNOSTIC]` console.log at every step + auto-download of overlay HTML to Downloads via programmatic `<a download>` click; reused yesterday's eBay FF#4 Pattern identically. Director ran ONE scrape with DevTools Console open + uploaded the diagnostic console output + the overlay HTML (`plos-etsy-diag-overlay-at-scrape-start.html` 41 KB). Analysis of the dumped HTML file revealed the exact root cause, producing **FF#3 `41b03c5` EMPIRICAL FIX — the WIN** (2 files +73/-190): `findOverlayContainer` fallback selector `[aria-modal="true"][role="dialog"]` matched Etsy's hidden `#customer-photo-overlay-carousel` (review-photo lightbox; pre-rendered in DOM with `aria-hidden="true"` + `wt-display-none` class but present at page load). Fix: restrict to `.deep-dive-sheet` only + reject hidden variants (aria-hidden, wt-display-none, hidden attribute). All FF#2 diagnostic instrumentation removed in the same commit (mirrors yesterday's eBay FF#5 removing FF#4 instrumentation). **Director Phase 4 PASS verdict on FF#3 = "Everything worked perfectly this time"** (identical phrasing to yesterday's eBay FF#5 PASS — same outcome shape twice in a row across different platforms + different root causes; eBay: JSON data-island + tabpanel-scoping bugs; Etsy: over-broad findOverlayContainer fallback selector matching hidden non-deep-dive dialogs). RESOLVES the Etsy sub-cluster end-to-end on vklf.com. **STAYS YES** through end of session and carries forward to next W5 session until next W5 deploy session ff-merges to main + Vercel auto-redeploys; final state YES at 2026-05-27 session end. **Cross-reference for design-session ingestion:** the canonical interview-locked spec lives in `docs/REVIEWS_PHASE_2_DESIGN.md` (frozen-§A 15 decisions + empty append-only §B + §C 4 active workstream outlines); future P-49 build-session §B entries land there per Rule 18, not in this ROADMAP entry.

- **P-51** (NEW 2026-05-28; RE-INSERTED 2026-05-28 after revert commit `958ccf8` wiped the original entry) **Per-Project competitive landscape AI summary on the existing `/comprehensive-analysis` page — output pasted directly into the TipTap editor.** **Status:** **NEW 2026-05-28 — UNSCHEDULED** (AI-flow item; slot per director's 2026-05-28 directive is BETWEEN P-49 and P-50 in next-priority order — runs after the in-flight P-49 W#2 Reviews Phase 2 closes, before P-50 Condition Pathology card). **SPEC DOC (Rule 31 canonical source-of-truth):** `docs/polish-item-specs/P-51-comprehensive-analysis-ai-summary.md` — read §3 at session start for the consolidated spec; §1 carries director's verbatim instructions; §2 carries joint-discussion adjustments. **WHY (director's 2026-05-28 verbatim, abridged — full text in spec doc §1):** *"the setup of the AI summarization for the /comprehensive-analysis page that is already setup. Essentially, just the way we have been taking multiple reviews from urls and summarizing them using AI, we will be taking all the reviews and creating a very comprehensive competitive landscape analysis that will be pasted into the text box editor on the /comprehensive-analysis page that is already setup."* — extends the P-49 W5 AI-summarization arc with the fifth + final aggregation level: cross-everything Per-Project competitive landscape. **Process directive (director's 2026-05-28 verbatim):** detailed Q&A + planning + options + approval are DEFERRED to the start of the P-51 build session itself; the spec doc's §3 is a SKELETON PLACEHOLDER ONLY at this time — do NOT pre-resolve the design at planning time. **Prior treatment + NEW twist:** PER_PROJECT enum slot already provisioned in `prisma/schema.prisma`; original §A.10 design in `docs/REVIEWS_PHASE_2_DESIGN.md` proposed a separate read-only banner; director's 2026-05-28 NEW twist routes the AI output INTO the existing user-editable TipTap document (commingle pattern — fundamentally different from prior P-49 W5 flows). **Severity:** **MEDIUM**. **Estimated effort:** 1-3 sessions depending on Q&A outcomes at session start. **Branch:** `workflow-2-competition-scraping` by default (W#2-scoped). **Schema-change-in-flight at session entry:** NO. **(a.105)+N:** P-51 not on the immediate next-session critical path; the P-49 W5 corrective rebuild (5-session plan per `docs/polish-item-specs/P-49-W5-S4-category-page.md` §2) takes precedence; P-51 slots in after those close.

- **P-50** (NEW 2026-05-25) **Add new 'Condition Pathology' card to PLOS dashboard between 'Competition Scraping & Deep Analysis' and 'Therapeutic Strategy & Product Family Design' cards.** **Status:** **NEW 2026-05-25 — UNSCHEDULED** (small single-session item; can be slotted into any deploy session OR done standalone on `main` between W#2 sessions). **Where:** TWO files (per-Project landing page `src/app/projects/[projectId]/page.tsx` lines 13-29 AND project-list page `src/app/projects/page.tsx` lines 147-163; both contain a 15-entry `WORKFLOW_DEFS` array with a sync-comment header "keep in sync with /plos page"). Insert new card at position 3 in BOTH arrays so they stay in sync. Suggested entry: `{ id: "condition-pathology", icon: "🩺", title: "Condition Pathology", active: false, route: null },` (icon TBD if director has preference; 🩺 = medical/condition theme; alternates: 🦠 / 🧪 / 🩹 — avoid 🧬 since it clashes with Therapeutic Strategy). **Card-only scope per Rule 14f forced-picker 2026-05-25** — director picked "just a card with that text (placeholder; workflow design comes later)" over "full Workflow #3 + renumber all downstream workflows" + over "defer scope decision." Implication: no doc-wide W# renumbering required (Therapeutic Strategy stays W#3 by convention even though it slides to card-position 4); Prisma schema unchanged (already free-form `workflow String`; no enum to update); existing docs that reference workflow counts (`PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §2` "14 PLOS workflows") stay accurate at the WORKFLOW level (Condition Pathology is a placeholder card, not yet a numbered workflow). **WHY:** Director's 2026-05-25 directive *"Between the 'Competition Scraping & Deep Analysis' card and the 'Therapeutic Strategy & Product Family Design' card in PLOS, please add a new card with the text 'Condition Pathology'."* — surfaces upcoming Condition Pathology capability on the dashboard; reversible; preserves architectural flexibility on whether it ever becomes a full workflow. **Severity:** **LOW** (cosmetic UI; placeholder card; ~5 LOC across 2 files; trivially reversible). **Fix scope:** Single small session, ~10 min in-Claude. Branch: `main` (platform-wide infrastructure, not workflow-2-scoped; per `docs/MULTI_WORKFLOW_PROTOCOL.md` §11 branch model). Mechanically: edit both card-array files at position 3 inserting the new entry; /scoreboard pre-build + post-edit at unchanged baselines; Rule 9 deploy gate to push to `main`; Vercel auto-redeploy ~2 minutes; director real-Chrome cross-walk: open vklf.com → any project → confirm Condition Pathology card appears at position 3 + open `/plos` dashboard → confirm same on project list. **Cross-reference:** Captured during today's 2026-05-25 Reviews Phase 2 scope-expansion session alongside P-49 (this entry is the separate small concern; P-49 is the multi-workstream Phase 2 hub). PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §2 narrative may eventually need an addendum if Condition Pathology gets promoted to full workflow status — that's a follow-up decision when the workflow's actual design lands. **(a.92):** P-50 not on the next-session critical path (low priority + standalone; lives on `main` not `workflow-2-competition-scraping`); will be done in some later opportunistic small session OR bundled with a future `main`-branch session. **Schema-change-in-flight:** **NO** (no `prisma db push`; pure UI card-array edit).

- **P-39** Sticky most-recently-used URL preference (popup, multi-URL case) (NEW 2026-05-19-c — director surfaced during P-38 verification when their real-world Project+Platform combos turned out to have 2+ URLs so P-38's simple `length === 1` rule didn't help). **Where:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx` (extend useEffect URL-list-load + add new onChange-handler to write to chrome.storage.local) + new pure-function helper at `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/lib/sticky-url-preselect.ts` (extractable for node:test). **Status:** ❌ **REVERTED 2026-05-19-c-2** via `git revert` commit `b635ae7`. Originally captured 2026-05-19-c as (a.45) RECOMMENDED-NEXT + then SHIPPED same day at commit `2766031` deploy session #23 + verified PASS on synthetic test setup, but determined to be the WRONG FEATURE — director's actual session-start ask was for the popup dropdown to pre-select the saved URL **matching the page currently open**, NOT a sticky-per-session-pick fallback for multi-URL combos. The actually-correct fix shipped as P-40 (active-tab-URL match) in deploy session #24 alongside this revert. See CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-3 §Entry "Wrong-feature-shipped-twice slip" for full root-cause + prevention rule. **Original capture preserved below for historical record (note: this was originally captured under commit `2766031`'s message which says "P-28" due to a session-start numbering-collision slip — see CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-2 §Entry; doc-layer canonical number is P-39; reverted regardless):** **NUMBERING NOTE:** originally labeled "P-28" in mid-session TaskCreate + initial doc-batch references — same numbering-collision slip as P-38 (existing P-28 is delete-URLs, deployed 2026-05-18). Renumbered to **P-39** in this end-of-session doc batch; TaskList sweep per Rule 26 confirmed Task #14 description updated to P-39 before completion. **Severity:** MEDIUM (closes the actual daily-felt UX friction in multi-URL combos — most real-world Project+Platform pairs have 2+ saved URLs so P-38's rule never fires; without P-39, every popup save still requires manual URL picking). **Why:** Director's natural-use feedback 2026-05-19-c during P-38 verification — multi-URL combos still required manual pick despite P-38 ship. Director picked at the in-session scope-vs-defer forced-picker: ship the trivial 1-URL case (P-38) now + capture the broader sticky-preference feature (P-39) as new W#2 polish item for next session with proper design + chrome.storage.local persistence + node:test cases. Per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` separation of scope — P-39 deserves its own session for clean design + tests + verification. **Fix shape (~20-30 LOC + test coverage Option A per Rule 27 forced-picker at session start):** track user's last-selected URL per (project, platform) tuple in `chrome.storage.local` under key shape `plos-cs-popup-url-pref-{projectId}-{platform}`; pre-select stored value if it matches an existing url's id; fall back to P-38's `length === 1` rule if no stored preference matches; fall back to placeholder if neither applies. Write to storage on every URL selection change (`onChange={...setSelectedUrlId...}` handler). resetForm post-save: keep the stored preference unchanged (user's last save was AT that URL, so that's the right thing to pre-select again). Edge case: if stored URL id no longer matches any current url (URL was deleted), fall back to P-38's rule or placeholder. Extract `getStickyPreselectedUrlId(urls, storedPref)` as a pure function in `popup/lib/sticky-url-preselect.ts` for node:test coverage; storage I/O side wired directly in the component (mechanical wire-up; doesn't need test coverage). Estimated +6-10 new node:test cases (stored-pref matches existing url → pre-select; stored-pref doesn't match → fall back to length===1; stored-pref undefined → length===1 fallback; multiple urls + no stored pref → empty string; etc.). **Cross-references:** P-38 above (the 1-URL trivial case this extends); CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-19-c-2 §Entry (numbering collision root-cause); NEXT_SESSION.md (launch prompt + pre-build read list); TaskList #14 (DEFERRED task, completed in this end-of-session doc batch). **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** stays "No" — `chrome.storage.local` is extension-side storage only; no server schema, no API change, no shared-types change.

- **P-52** (NEW 2026-05-31-b) **AI model registry + central model-selection methodology + Opus 4.8 rollout** — platform-wide infrastructure item spanning W#1 Keyword Clustering + W#2 Competition Scraping. **Captured during a read-only code audit on 2026-05-31-b** (director's session-start Issue 2; deferred to its own `main`-track session via a Rule 14f picker Q2=A "own session on main" because it spans W#1 + W#2 and touches the live W#1 tool). **THREE parts:** (a) a central tracking DOC listing every place a user picks an AI model across W#1 + W#2; (b) a METHODOLOGY/process rule (likely a NEW HANDOFF_PROTOCOL rule mirroring Rule 31's spec-capture mechanism) so any newly-added model-choice function is registered in the doc; (c) ADD Opus 4.8 (`claude-opus-4-8`) as an option in every model picker + pricing table. **AUDIT FINDINGS — FIVE model-choice call sites found:** (1) W#1 `AutoAnalyze.tsx` keyword tool — 5 models hardcoded in 3 spots (`AA_PRICING` ~L95-100, default `claude-sonnet-4-6` ~L128, `<select>` options ~L2116-2120), NO central list; (2-4) W#2 `PerReviewSummarizeModal` / `PerCompetitorSummarizeModal` / `GlobalCompetitorSummarizeModal` — each DUPLICATES `SUPPORTED_MODEL_VERSIONS` locally instead of importing; (5) shared seam `src/lib/competition-scraping/review-analysis/client.ts` (`DEFAULT_MODEL_VERSION` + `SUPPORTED_MODEL_VERSIONS` + `isSupportedModelVersion`) + `pricing.ts` (`MODEL_PRICING`) + validated in `review-analysis-run-batch.ts`. The browser extension has NO model selection. **Adding Opus 4.8 cleanly** = edit `client.ts` + `pricing.ts` + the 3 W#2 modals (or better, refactor the modals to IMPORT the central list — part of the methodology) + W#1 `AutoAnalyze`'s 3 spots. **OPEN BLOCKER:** need official Opus 4.8 pricing numbers (input / output / cache-read / cache-write per-MTok) for the pricing tables, else cost estimation falls back to a default. **Rule 24 pre-capture search result:** PRIOR TREATMENT FOUND — `docs/REVIEWS_PHASE_2_DESIGN.md` §A.7 locks the W#2 model policy ("Opus 4.7 default + Opus 4.6 selectable"); `docs/MODEL_QUALITY_SCORING.md` is W#1 stability scoring (NOT model-selection UI). No existing central registry doc — the registry + methodology genuinely do not exist yet, so this entry is new. **Where:** NEW SDK-free central list `src/lib/competition-scraping/review-analysis/models.ts` (`client.ts` re-exports for back-compat) + `pricing.ts`; `src/app/.../competitor-reviews-analysis/components/PerReviewSummarizeModal.tsx` + `PerCompetitorSummarizeModal.tsx` + `GlobalCompetitorSummarizeModal.tsx` (now IMPORT `models.ts` instead of duplicating); W#1 `AutoAnalyze.tsx` (Opus 4.8 added inline to `AA_PRICING` + dropdown); NEW central registry doc `docs/AI_MODEL_REGISTRY.md` + NEW **Rule 32** in `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` + NEW SessionStart hook `.claude/hooks/check-model-registry-drift.sh`; **spec doc** `docs/polish-item-specs/P-52-ai-model-registry.md` (Rule 31 step 3). **Severity:** MEDIUM (improves model-selection maintainability + ships the latest model option to the director across both live tools; the duplicate-list drift across the 3 modals is a latent maintenance hazard — now resolved via the shared `models.ts` import). **Status:** ✅ **DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-29-b (build `5b9784a`)** — all THREE deliverables shipped + deployed + director-verified on vklf.com (Phase 4 verbatim "passed"): (1) NEW `docs/AI_MODEL_REGISTRY.md`; (2) NEW Rule 32 + the SessionStart drift-detect hook; (3) Opus 4.8 (`claude-opus-4-8`) added to `models.ts` + `pricing.ts` (Opus-tier PLACEHOLDER pricing + CONFIRM comment) + W#1 `AutoAnalyze.tsx`, with the 3 W#2 modals refactored to import the central list. Defaults UNCHANGED (W#2 Opus 4.7 / W#1 Sonnet 4.6). NEW reusable PATTERN — "SDK-free constants module lets client components import shared constants without bundling the server SDK". Closed (a.111). **TWO carry-overs remain OPEN** (captured in the spec doc §4): (i) official Opus 4.8 pricing numbers — `pricing.ts` uses Opus-tier placeholder pending official input/output/cache-read/cache-write per-MTok numbers (director may supply offline); (ii) the deferred W#1 `AutoAnalyze.tsx` shared-list migration — Opus 4.8 was added inline this session (safer for the live tool); migrating W#1 to a shared model list is deferred to a future session. **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** NO entry → NO exit (model lists + pricing tables are code constants; zero migration).

- **P-53** (NEW 2026-06-01) **Excel "Export Table" button for the Reviews Analysis By Competitor Category + By Competitor Type pages.** **Captured during the P-49 W5 Type page all-in-one build (2026-06-01) as a Rule 3 / Rule 26 finding:** the master-spec §1 question Q-D listed an Excel "Export Table" download for the Category/Type pages (mirroring the Competitor Reviews Analysis Table page's existing `reviews-table-export.ts` xlsx export), but a code-truth audit established it was NEVER actually built on the as-shipped Category page — so the Type page, built as a faithful mirror of the Category page, also lacks it. It is therefore a separate, unbuilt feature for BOTH the `reviews-analysis-by-category` and `reviews-analysis-by-type` pages (the working Excel export today exists only on the older `competitor-reviews-analysis` page). **Where (when built):** a client-side xlsx export of the currently-visible columns (mirroring `src/lib/competition-scraping/.../reviews-table-export.ts` + the Q7→A "visible columns only" decision from the Competitor Reviews Analysis Table page), wired into both grouped pages (`reviews-analysis-by-category/page.tsx` + `reviews-analysis-by-type/page.tsx`); no new route, no schema change expected (client-side). **Rule 24 pre-capture search result:** PRIOR TREATMENT FOUND — the working pattern lives at `src/lib/competition-scraping/.../reviews-table-export.ts` (shipped 2026-05-29-c for the Competitor Reviews Analysis Table page); the grouped Category/Type pages would mirror it with grouping headers. **Severity:** LOW (a convenience export; the data is fully visible + editable on-page; the two AI flows + the Source Reviews column are the substantive features and all shipped). **Status:** OPEN — captured this session per the Rule 26 deferred-item sweep (Task #6 DEFERRED → this entry); not yet scheduled. **Schema-change-in-flight flag:** expected NO (client-side export).

- **P-54** (NEW 2026-06-01-b) **`/competition-scraping` MAIN-PAGE (Competitor URLs table) enhancements — 9 director requirements (R1–R9), built as a 5-phase plan.** **Captured during a director-directed session-start reprioritization (2026-06-01-b):** the session was scheduled to open the (a.119) `/comprehensive-analysis` AI-summary requirements-gathering, but the director DEFERRED (a.119) (verbatim: "I want to work on something different today and defer this step for the session after the requirements of the features mentioned today are finished") and instead directed a NEW batch of main Competitor URLs table enhancements. The 9 requirements + the resolved decisions D1–D9 + the 5-phase plan + the open questions live in the **SPEC DOC (Rule 31 canonical source-of-truth — read §1 verbatim directive + §3 decisions at session start):** `docs/polish-item-specs/P-54-competition-scraping-main-table-enhancements.md`. **The 9 requirements:** R1 last-column right-edge resize; R2 page-level vertical scroll; R3 remove the redundant blue "Comprehensive Competitor Analysis" button (duplicate of the top nav tab); R4 header pinned while scrolling; R5 drag-reorder columns + share the table layout across the Project; R6 a "Sort By" grouping box (group rows by Platform / Category / Type as banner-row groups, reusing the By-Category/By-Type pattern); R7/R8/R9 dynamic text/image/video category columns with stacked aligned sub-rows + per-item "Your Analysis" editable in-table + refetch-on-return + shown-by-default + locked column pairs. **Where:** main-table surface `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/UrlTable.tsx` + `url-table-columns.ts` + `ColumnResizeHandle.tsx` + `ColumnVisibilityBar.tsx` + parent `CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx`; NEW pure helper `src/lib/competition-scraping/column-order.ts`; NEW Prisma model `ProjectTablePreferences` + NEW handler `project-table-preferences.ts` + NEW route `/competition-scraping/project-table-preferences`. **Rule 24 pre-capture search result:** PRIOR PATTERNS FOUND + REUSED — the grouped By-Category/By-Type pages (`category-table-grouping.ts` / `type-table-grouping.ts`, banner-row + two-level dnd-kit drag) are the precedent for Phases 4–5; the per-user `UserTablePreferences` layout memory + the reviews-table `rowSpan` sub-row alignment are reused; the genuinely-new piece is the shared per-Project `ProjectTablePreferences` store + the horizontal column-header drag. **Severity:** MEDIUM (substantive usability + collaboration improvement to the most-used table in W#2). **Status:** ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-01-d — ALL 5 PHASES (R1–R9) DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED on vklf.com** (Phases 1–3 2026-06-01-b — director PASS on all five deploys; Phase 4 2026-06-01-c — director verbatim "pass"; Phase 5 2026-06-01-d — director verbatim "pass"). Phase 5 was the LAST piece. **Phase 1 (`b134b5d`)** = R1 last-column resize + R3 remove blue button (no schema change). **Phase 2 (`e0661ba` + FF1 `b27841f` + FF2 `1f18308`)** = R2 + R4 whole-page window-scroll both axes + header sticky-to-window (D1 REVISED — director picked whole-page scrollbars over the earlier floating-bar idea), with FF1 shrink-wrap the table wrapper (`width:max-content`) + 48px trailing gap so the page scrolls to the full table width + the last-column resize edge is grabbable, and FF2 grey panel shrink-wraps to full table width + `MAX_COLUMN_WIDTH` 600→4000 + auto-scroll-the-window-during-resize (`document-space width = clientX+scrollX` + RAF `window.scrollBy` near the right edge) so the last column drags as far right as needed (no schema change). **Phase 3 (`7a10ba4`)** = R5 drag-the-column-headers (a ⠿ grip per header; a horizontal `SortableContext` sharing the ONE `DndContext` with the vertical row-reorder list, id-discriminated in `handleDragEnd`) + share the WHOLE main-table layout across the Project (column order + show/hide + widths + row order + font size), backed by a NEW additive `ProjectTablePreferences` model (`projectId @unique`, NEW `columnOrder Json`) + a SEED-ON-READ handler that copies the requesting user's `UserTablePreferences` into the new project row on first GET (so the pre-existing layout is preserved, no reset); the per-user `UserTablePreferences` is untouched. **`prisma db push` applied the additive `ProjectTablePreferences` table to prod (1.27s, zero data loss); Schema-change-in-flight flipped YES→NO at the Phase 3 deploy push.** **FOUR NEW reusable PATTERNS** (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-06-01-b): "Shrink-wrap a horizontally-overflowing table wrapper (`width:max-content`) + a trailing `paddingRight` gap so the PAGE owns the horizontal scroll AND the last-column resize edge is reachable" + "Auto-scroll the window during a resize drag (RAF + document-space width = `clientX+scrollX`)" + "Two sortable axes (vertical rows + horizontal column headers) in ONE dnd-kit DndContext, discriminated by id in `onDragEnd`" + "Seed-on-read migration from a per-user store to a shared per-Project store preserves the user's existing layout". **Phase 4 (`87d8efa`) — ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-06-01-c (director "pass"):** R6 per D2 — a NEW "Sort By" box (beside Platforms / Columns in `ColumnVisibilityBar`) groups the main table's rows by Platform / Category / Type into banner-row groups (mirroring the By-Category / By-Type pages); "None" (flat) stays the default; within a group both per-column click-sort and row-drag work; group banners are draggable (two-level drag); the Phase-3 column reorder stays independent; the empty bucket renders "(Uncategorized)" / "(Untyped)" pinned last. **Q-H resolved WITH the director via a 3-question design picker BEFORE coding** (all 3 Recommended chosen): persist the grouping mode + per-mode banner order SHARED across the Project; sort + drag WITHIN each group; draggable group banners. NEW pure helper `main-table-grouping.ts` (`buildMainGroupedRows` re-buckets ALREADY-SORTED rows — grouping is a re-bucketing LAYER, not a re-sort — + `groupKeyOf` / `groupLabelOf` / `reorderableGroupKeys` / `coerceGroupOrderMap`; +21 node:test); additive `ProjectTablePreferences.groupBy String @default("none")` + `groupOrder Json @default("{}")` shipped to prod via `prisma db push` (1.28s, zero data loss; Schema-change-in-flight flipped YES→NO at the deploy push); `UrlTable.tsx` grouped render REUSES `applyCategoryDrag` / `applyCompetitorDrag` from `category-table-layout` (generic pure string-array ops); the flat path UNCHANGED (Phases 1–3 untouched); NEW `GroupBannerRow` component. **NEW reusable PATTERN** (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-06-01-c): "Grouping as a re-bucketing LAYER over already-sorted rows — pass the display-ordered rows into the grouping helper + preserve within-bucket input order, so the active column-sort / manual row order flows through for free; the two-level-drag re-rank helpers generalize across pages because they are pure string-array ops". Scoreboard: src/lib 1270/1270 (+21), build 71 routes UNCHANGED (reuses the existing `project-table-preferences` endpoint), root+ext tsc clean, ext 910/910. **Phase 5 (`6363300`) — ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-06-01-d (director "pass"); CLOSES P-54:** R7/R8/R9 per D3/D4/D5/D7/D8/D9 — for each captured content/image/video category the main table grows a locked column-PAIR (the captured/embedded text + its glued "[category] Analysis" column); multi-item cells stack as aligned rowSpan sub-rows (D3, reusing the reviews-table pattern); a "Content / Image / Video Categories" group of checkboxes shows/hides each kind (D7, shown when content exists); cells edit IN-table — plain text inline, "Your Analysis" via a click-to-open pop-out editor reusing `PerItemAnalysisBox` (Q-I) — writing back through the EXISTING per-item PATCH routes (`/text/[textId]`, `/images/[imageId]`, `/videos/[videoId]`; D5/D9); the table refetches on tab refocus (D4); pairs move together under column reorder (D8) and align in BOTH the flat AND the Phase-4 grouped render paths. **Q-F + Q-I resolved WITH the director via a 3-question design picker BEFORE coding** (Q-I → pop-out editor [Recommended]; Q-F → auto-append new categories left of "Added On", keep custom order [Recommended]; the 3rd build/deploy-cadence question → director override "build all three at once, single deploy"). NEW pure helper `src/lib/competition-scraping/dynamic-columns.ts` (+ `.test.ts`; +21 node:test); additive optional `MainTableCaptures` / `MainTableCapturedItem` shared-types + `CompetitorUrl.captures?`; opt-in `?withCaptures=1` include on the existing GET `/urls` (lightweight projection); `UrlTable.tsx` physical-column model (fixed + dynamic value/analysis pairs, rowSpan sub-rows, in-cell editing, locked-pair reorder) + `ColumnVisibilityBar.tsx` 3 group checkboxes + `CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx` captures fetch + `handleCapturedSave`. **NO schema change — the prior handoff's predicted "LIKELY schema change" was AVOIDED:** the dynamic column keys ride in the EXISTING `ProjectTablePreferences.columnOrder` / `columnVisibility` / `columnWidths` Json maps (only the value key is orderable; the analysis column is glued at render), so NO new field was needed; Schema-change-in-flight STAYED NO; NO `prisma db push`. **NEW reusable PATTERN** (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry 2026-06-01-d): "Locked column-pairs with NO schema change — store ONLY the value key in the shared `columnOrder`; glue the analysis column at render; the existing `moveColumnKey` drags the pair as one for free" + the opt-in `?withCaptures=1` include kept the route count at 71. Extension rebuilt locally via `npx wxt build` (1.574s, no epoll_wait hang this time after `pkill -f wxt` cleanup) at `638.82 kB` total (exact match for polish session #8 verified baseline); zipped server-side as `plos-extension-2026-05-10-c-p2-p9-p10.zip` (177,116 bytes) with unique filename to bypass any caching of yesterday's `-p3` build. **P-9 verification — ALL 9 STEPS PASSED on vklf.com:** Ebay search + Ebay listing detail (cap-removal verified — Highlight Terms now appear on previously-blocked ~1.5MB / ~1.58MB pages); chrome://extensions Errors panel zero new "exceeds highlight cap" entries (cap entirely removed → no warning to repeat); Walmart search (~636-675KB heavy-SPA) — Highlight Terms now appear + page does NOT freeze (chunked walker yields between batches); zero cap-warning entries even after Walmart's ~20+ MutationObserver re-renders; soft 50-term-count cap warning still fires when terms exceed 50 (separate from removed body-text cap); Amazon + Etsy spot-checks no regression; live-edit on Walmart updates highlights within ~1-2s (chrome.storage.onChanged + last-wins cancellation). **P-10 verification — ALL 10 STEPS PASSED on vklf.com:** direct paste of saved Walmart URL → banner appears within ~1s + auto-dismisses 5s; SPA navigation to saved Walmart product (the previously-flaky path) — banner appears reliably 5/5 times across different products; unsaved-product navigation correctly suppresses banner; unsaved → saved navigation re-fires banner (dedupe correctly tracks last-considered URL); refresh same URL re-fires banner; manual × dismiss + stay-on-URL correctly suppresses re-fire; Amazon + Ebay + Etsy spot-checks confirm no regression. **P-2 BROWSER RE-VERIFY DEFERRED + corrected test sequence captured:** original verification spec from polish session #8 conflated two separate fetch paths — (a) supabase auth's `signInWithPassword` (NOT covered by P-2's `mapFetchTransportError`) and (b) `authedFetch`'s vklf.com API calls (IS covered). The "sign out → WiFi off → sign in" sequence in the original spec hits path (a) — supabase returns "Failed to fetch" verbatim — which is what director observed. **Corrected sequence for next session:** (1) sign in normally with WiFi ON; (2) turn OFF WiFi; (3) close popup, then re-open it; popup's `ProjectPicker` re-fetches via `authedFetch` on mount → P-2 converts `TypeError` to `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable — check your connection.')` → `ProjectPicker` prepends "Couldn't load your projects (0): " → expected red error box reads approximately "Couldn't load your projects (0): Network unreachable — check your connection." DEFERRED task #6 registered per Rule 26. **One mid-session director-side correction:** Walmart highlights + "+" icon both appeared NOT to work after popup-platform switch; root cause = orchestrator reads `selectedPlatform` ONCE on page load (orchestrator.ts:78-81); page-refresh after platform-switch fixed both immediately. This is by-design (avoids mid-session platform-mismatch chaos) but the verification spec didn't call it out — captured as CORRECTIONS_LOG entry. **Mid-session friction captured to CORRECTIONS_LOG (3 entries):** (a) polish session #8 didn't run `git pull origin main` to absorb main's `21d717b` before adding new commits → caused today's divergence (process gap; structural fix — extend MULTI_WORKFLOW_PROTOCOL §4 to require pulling main into feature branches when main has advanced); (b) P-2 verification spec design conflated supabase-auth + authedFetch fetch paths → ran the wrong test sequence today (spec-design gap); (c) verification spec for P-9 + P-10 didn't call out refresh-after-platform-switch requirement → director hit silent gate-check rejection on first Walmart attempt (spec-design gap). **Schema-change-in-flight stays No throughout** (no schema work today; today is rebase + deploy + browser-verify only). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** pull-rebase clean at both checkpoints (session start + before doc-batch commit); W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership; W#2 row updated. **TaskList sweep at end-of-session per Rule 26:** 6 tasks tracked; 1 `DEFERRED:` task remains open (#6 — P-2 browser re-verify with corrected sequence; destination = next deploy session OR dedicated polish session — captured in this ROADMAP P-2 entry below + COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md "Polish session #8" P-2 sub-table). **Doc updates this commit:** ROADMAP (header + Active Tools W#2 row Last Session prepended + W#2 polish backlog P-9 + P-10 ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED + P-2 ✅ DEPLOYED + browser-verify DEFERRED with corrected sequence), CHAT_REGISTRY (new top row + header), DOCUMENT_MANIFEST (header + per-doc flags + this-session summary), `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` (append-only Rule 18 entry covering today's deploy + the divergence reconciliation + P-9/P-10 verify + P-2 deferral), `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` (Polish session #8 section P-9 + P-10 sub-tables marked PASS; P-2 sub-table updated with corrected test sequence + "deferred to next session" note), `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` (3 new entries — divergence process gap + P-2 spec-design gap + platform-switch-refresh spec gap). Doc-batch commit on `main`; push pending Rule 9 approval — doc-only push triggers Vercel rebuild but contains zero user-visible code changes (the user-visible code from `d2e2115` is already deployed earlier in session).)

**Previously updated:** May 10, 2026-b (**W#2 polish session #8 — P-2 + P-9 + P-10 polish bundle SHIPPED at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** Three extension-only fixes in one bundle. **(P-2) `authedFetch` offline error handling** — wrapped `fetch()` in try/catch; new exported `mapFetchTransportError(err)` helper converts native `TypeError("Failed to fetch")` (offline / DNS failure / CORS preflight failure / refused connection) → `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable — check your connection.')`; AbortError + other shapes re-thrown unchanged; new test file `api-client.test.ts` with 6 tests covering all the conversion cases. **(P-10) AlreadySavedOverlay reliability on Walmart heavy-SPA pages** — three orchestrator changes: (1) `maybeShowDetailOverlay()` dedupes by `location.href` via new `lastOverlayUrl` closure variable (set BEFORE recognition match so navigate-away-and-back correctly re-fires); (2) new `scheduleDetailOverlayCheck()` 150ms-debounced cancellable timer ensures only the final URL gets a banner check after pushState bursts; (3) MutationObserver-based SPA URL change detection via new `lastObservedUrl` closure variable — checks `location.href !== lastObservedUrl` inside the existing 250ms-debounced rescan callback and triggers the overlay-check debouncer if changed. **Critical technical wrinkle that drove the design:** Chrome content scripts run in an **isolated world** with their own `window.history`, so patching `pushState` in content-script context does NOT intercept host-page React Router calls (Walmart, etc.) — the MutationObserver is the cross-context-safe signal since SPA navigation always causes immediate DOM mutation. **(P-9) Live-page Highlight Terms 500KB body-text cap REMOVED entirely** — replaced with chunked/incremental highlight pass via `requestIdleCallback`. New exported `processInChunks<T>(items, processItem, options)` generic helper (default chunk size 500 nodes ≈ 10-15ms/chunk; pluggable `yieldFn` for testing; honors `CancellationSignal` between chunks). `applyHighlightsTo` is now async + chunked; `startLiveHighlighting.refresh()` rewritten for last-wins cancellation (each new refresh cancels in-flight pass before starting; latest DOM/term state always becomes eventual visible state). Removing the cap auto-resolves the backlog's recommended (e) dedupe warning + (f) skip-if-unchanged measurement — no cap → no warning to dedupe → no `document.body.textContent.length` re-measure overhead. 9 new tests added to `highlight-terms.test.ts` for `processInChunks` (chunk boundaries, ordering invariant, cancellation mid-pass, cancellation before first item, default chunkSize). **Verification scoreboard:** ext compile clean; ext tests **220/220** pass (was 205/205 — +6 P-2 + +9 P-9 processInChunks); ext build clean (**638.82 KB total**); ext lint clean (npx eslint exit 0; "Pages directory" informational message from inherited Next eslint plugin not a lint error); root tsc clean; root build clean (**50 routes** exact baseline parity — no new routes); root tests **393/393** pass exact baseline parity; root lint **52 problems (13e/39w)** exact baseline parity. **Browser verification: NOT YET — code lives on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` only; not on `main`; vklf.com runs prior code.** Combined with session #7's P-3 narrowed work (commit `16d4351`), the W#2-vs-main deploy gap now covers FOUR polish items (P-2 + P-3 narrowed + P-9 + P-10) plus session #7's schema addition; next deploy session (item (a.4) in Active Tools row) will browser-verify all four together. **Multi-workflow per Rule 25:** pull-rebase clean at session start; W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership; schema-change-in-flight stayed "No" entire session — none of P-2/P-9/P-10 touched `prisma/schema.prisma`. **TaskList sweep at end-of-session per Rule 26:** 5 tasks tracked + completed (start-of-session sequence; P-2 fix; P-10 fix; P-9 fix; verification + doc batch); **zero `DEFERRED:` items at any point.** **Director picked option A "Proceed with all three fixes today P-2 → P-10 → P-9 (Recommended)"** at session start via Rule 14f forced-picker, despite ROADMAP's Active Tools W#2 row Next Session list having `(a.4)` deploy as RECOMMENDED NEXT — Claude surfaced the ordering trade-off transparently in the drift check before the picker fired. **Doc updates this commit:** ROADMAP (header + Active Tools W#2 row Last Session + W#2 polish backlog P-2 + P-9 + P-10 ✅ SHIPPED at code level on W#2 branch + Next Session item (a.5) marked DONE-at-code-level), CHAT_REGISTRY (new top row + header), DOCUMENT_MANIFEST (header + per-doc flags + this-session summary), `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` (append-only Rule 18 entry), `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` (new browser re-verify section for the three fixes — lands at next deploy session). Doc-batch commit on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; push pending Rule 9 approval — push to W#2 branch does NOT deploy vklf.com regardless.)
