S3-25 (300ms hover-debounce + no flicker on quick traversal) ✅. **Two polish items surfaced + captured during walkthrough via Rule 26 + Rule 18 mid-build directive Read-It-Back:** (P-7) URL-add overlay positioning — director directive at S3-12: *"shouldn't open directly on top of the very product listing for which the url is being added"*; (P-8) ✓ already-saved icon punches through URL-add overlay — director observation at S3-16: *"its checkmark shows up on top of the new overlay (which it shouldn't)"* — root cause yesterday's `f4226ca` icon-visibility-boost set z-index to 2147483647 (max int32) which equals the form's z-index. **Mid-session pivot per Rule 14f Option A** — director chose to fix P-4 + P-5 + P-7 + P-8 inline before continuing Ebay/Etsy/Walmart walkthrough (rather than re-encounter the same bugs across 3 more platforms). **Four polish fixes shipped (single code commit):** (P-8 first / smallest) z-index restructure in `styles.ts` — layered tiers (page-overlay 999990 for saved-icon + add-button + dismiss + overlay-banner; modal-backdrop 999998; modal-content 999999) replacing the prior universal max-int 2147483647 (Amazon's chrome max-z-index is ~5000-10000 so 999990 has ~100× headroom). (P-7) URL-add overlay positioning — added optional `triggerRect?: DOMRect | null` to `UrlAddFormProps`; threaded through `FloatingAddButton.onClick(href, triggerRect)` callback contract update + `currentLinkRect` storage at button-show time; new `computeFormPosition()` places form on side of viewport opposite the trigger's horizontal center; falls back to centered when `triggerRect === null` (right-click context-menu path). (P-4) Amazon sponsored-ads SSPA-redirect detection — new `decodeSspaInner(href)` reads `pathname === '/sspa/click'` cases, pulls `searchParams.get('url')` (auto-decoded once), prepends `${protocol}//${host}`, feeds back through ASIN_RE; both `matchesProduct` and `canonicalProductUrl` branch on it; 9 new tests using two real captured sponsored-ad URLs (sp_atf placement → ASIN B0DWJTLNYT; sp_mtf placement → ASIN B0716F3NFG) covering happy path + edge cases. (P-5 / largest / Rule 18 design Read-It-Back done before coding — director approved design before coding started) Live-page Highlight Terms application — new `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts` (~240 LOC) + `.test.ts` (~120 LOC unit tests on regex + colormap pure logic). Reads `highlightTerms:<projectId>` from `chrome.storage.local`; word-boundary case-insensitive regex with longest-first ordering + whitespace tolerance; TreeWalker over text nodes skipping script/style/noscript/textarea/input/iframe/svg/math/code/pre + contenteditable + our own .plos-cs-* UI; `<mark class="plos-cs-highlight">` wrappers with inline background-color + auto-flipped foreground via existing `getContrastTextColor()`; `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener for live popup-edit refresh; MutationObserver hookup via existing orchestrator observer (shares one observer; debounced 250ms); SPA navigation refresh via existing popstate hook; perf safeguards (50-term soft cap with console.warn + truncate; 500KB body-text cap with skip + warn); initial pass via `requestIdleCallback` (graceful on still-painting pages); cleanup wired into orchestrator teardown. **Verification scoreboard:** ext compile clean; ext tests **185/185** pass (159 prior + 9 SSPA + 14 highlight-terms regex/colormap + 3 baseline tweaks); ext lint clean (npx eslint exit 0; the "Pages directory cannot be found" message is the parent Next.js plugin's informational warning, not an error); ext build clean (`.output/chrome-mv3/` 634 kB total — content.js 28.01 kB up from 21.71 kB attempt-#3 baseline due to highlight-terms module); ext zip regenerated (175090 bytes vs 172683 attempt #3, +1.4%). **Browser re-verify of 4 polish fixes is DEFERRED to attempt #5** per Rule 14f Option A wrap-now choice at session-end pause-check (vs Option B push through re-verify or Option C all the way through) — code-complete + toolchain-verified, browser re-verify benefits from fresh attention. **TaskList sweep at end-of-session per Rule 26:** Tasks #18 + #19 (DEFERRED P-7 + P-8) completed when migrated to ROADMAP polish backlog new entries + `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` append-only entries. **Previously updated:** May 8, 2026-c (**W#2 Waypoint #1 verification ATTEMPT #3 — PARTIAL — Amazon S3-1 through S3-11 ✅; S3-12 through S3-36 + S2-3 re-verify deferred to attempt #4.** Director resumed verification on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` in `session_2026-05-08-c_w2-waypoint-1-verification-attempt-3-extension-session-3`. Three real bugs in extension session 3 code surfaced + FIXED INLINE in single code commit `f4226ca`: (1) **content-script CORS blocker** (the original attempt #3 trigger) — content scripts run in host page's origin (amazon.com etc.) which is NOT in vklf.com's CORS allowlist (`chrome-extension://*` only), so `listCompetitorUrls` + `createCompetitorUrl` direct fetches failed preflight with `TypeError: Failed to fetch`. Fix: new `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/api-bridge.ts` routes the 3 PLOS API calls through `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` → background service worker → fetch from extension origin where CORS passes. Bonus: content.js bundle dropped ~219 KB → ~21 KB (supabase no longer pulled in transitively into per-page content script — real perf win on every Amazon/Ebay/Etsy/Walmart page load). (2) **Floating "+" button hover grace timer** — moving cursor from link to floating button fired link-mouseleave → button hidden before cursor reached it; fix: 150ms grace timer + button mouseenter cancels timer. (3) **"Already saved" icon dedupe + visibility** — Amazon product cards have 4+ anchor tags pointing to same product (image, title, review, price links) so 1 saved URL produced 4 ✓ icons; AND default 16px muted-green icon was too subtle to spot at default styling against Amazon's busy chrome. Fix: dedupe in `scanLinks` to 1 icon per unique normalized URL across MutationObserver re-scans + CSS visibility boost (28×28 vibrant emerald `#16a34a`, 3px white border + green halo ring + drop shadow + bolder ✓ glyph + max z-index 2147483647). **Walked-through outcome:** S3-1 reload → revealed CORS blocker; S3-2 + S3-3 permissions spot-check ✅ with 2 doc-text caveats captured (vklf.com → www.vklf.com per attempt #2 fix; Storage permission may not display in Chrome UI even when granted); S3-4 sign in + Amazon platform ✅ via reload reconfigure; S3-5 Console clean ✅ post-CORS-fix; S3-6 Amazon search loads ✅; S3-7 hover → "+" button ✅ post-grace-timer-fix; S3-8 cursor away → 150ms grace hide ✅ with doc-text update; S3-9 click "+" → modal with all 5 expected elements ✅; **S3-10 Save → URL appears in PLOS-side viewer Amazon ✅ — full end-to-end of CORS messaging-proxy fix verified LIVE**; S3-11 ✓ icon on saved link ✅ post-dedupe-and-CSS-fix on 3rd iteration. Director called wrap-up at S3-11 per session-mgmt lucidity preference (3 substantive mid-session pivots already absorbed). **Verification scoreboard:** ext compile clean; ext tests **159/159** pass (146 prior + 13 new api-bridge tests); ext lint clean; ext build clean (`.output/chrome-mv3/` 627 kB total — content.js 21.71 kB vs prior 219 KB pre-fix); ext zip regenerated (172683 bytes vs 221422 attempt #2 — 22% smaller from supabase-out-of-content-script bonus). **Five deferred items captured via Rule 26 + migrated end-of-session into destination docs:** P-3 broadened (per-user-per-project extension state moves to PLOS DB — directive surfaced when reload reset Highlight Terms; "no matter where the user logs in, they can pick up where they left off") → ROADMAP polish backlog P-3 + DESIGN §B; P-4 (Amazon sponsored-ads SSPA-redirect detection) → ROADMAP polish backlog P-4; P-5 (live-page Highlight Terms application — director directive: highlight words on the page user is on) → ROADMAP polish backlog P-5 + DESIGN §B; P-6 ("Sponsored Ad" checkbox in URL-add form + PLOS-side tag — synergy with P-4 auto-detection) → ROADMAP polish backlog P-6 + DESIGN §B; the rest of session-3 walkthrough (S3-12 through S3-36 + deferred S2-3 re-verify) → next-session task. **Multi-workflow per Rule 25:** schema-change-in-flight stays "No" both rows; pull-rebase clean at session start; W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership. MODIFIED `App.tsx` (replaced `SignedInScreen` Verify-Connection placeholder with `SetupScreen` composing the three pickers + active-session banner once both project + platform selected; persisted state hydrates on mount; "Verify connection" button retired since the project picker itself proves the auth round-trip works). MODIFIED `style.css` (extended with field-block layout + select/textarea styling + active-session banner + chip row + swatch popover grid). MODIFIED `api-client.ts` (typed `listProjects()` return as `ExtensionProject[]` with shape-validation filter). MODIFIED `tsconfig.json` (extension-only — added `allowImportingTsExtensions: true` + `noEmit: true` to support .ts extensions in imports that node:test under `--experimental-strip-types` requires). MODIFIED `package.json` (extension-only — added `test` script). **Director-approved scope at session start via Rule 14f `AskUserQuestion`:** Option A "All three, full per-spec" RECOMMENDED picked (4 options surfaced: **A recommended** / B pickers-only-defer-Highlight-Terms / C all three + URL-recognition-badge stub / D escape hatch). Implementation: server-side `/canvas/rebuild` route accepts new optional `keywordUpdates` + `archiveKeywords` body fields; pre-fetches archive source rows via one findMany outside the transaction; appends `removedKeyword.create` + `keyword.deleteMany` + `keyword.updateMany` ops to the existing array `$transaction`; wraps with `{timeout: 30000, maxWait: 5000}`. Client-side `AutoAnalyze.tsx doApplyV3` computes all updates pre-rebuild, merges placement + reconciliation by id, augments rebuild payload; removes the sequential archive POST loop AND both fire-and-forget `onBatchUpdateKeywords` call sites (the actual burst sources at `KeywordWorkspace.tsx:627`'s forEach). Consolidation pass and manual batch retry inherit the fix for free. 327/327 src/lib tests pass; tsc clean; build clean (25 routes); lint at exact baseline parity (16e/40w; zero new). **Live D3 verification stats:** 50 regular batches + 5 consolidations (after batches 12, 22, 32, 42, 52); canvas grew 124 → 194 topics; ~24,000+ keyword updates flowed through the fold-in (every one bypassed the old burst-PATCH path); 135+ keywords archived (mostly celebrity+bursa Turkish-city homographs — high-quality model picks); per-batch wall-clock 12-23s rebuild HTTP (within 30s timeout); zero `FATAL: Max client connections reached` errors; zero post-rebuild refresh failures; 3 of 50 batches needed validation retry (~6%, normal); per-batch cost trajectory $0.32 → $0.45 (mild growth confirms recency-stickiness fix's design intent); total spent ~$22.91. **W#1 PRODUCTION-READINESS GATE prerequisite #5b ✅ DONE.** Plan↔code drift surfaced mid-D3: consolidation prompt still allows ADD_SISTER_LINK + REMOVE_SISTER_LINK (director's plan was "no sister links in Auto-Analyze at all"); 2026-05-03-c recency-stickiness fix only deferred them from regular batches, not consolidation. Drift this session: +3 sister links from consolidation #1 only (#2-#5 emitted 0). Cleanup deferred to next session — small follow-up (~10-20 LOC). P2025 race condition (2026-05-04-d bonus finding) eliminated for free by approach (iii). Run paused at batch 52 (canvas 194) per agreed 50-batch verification target; checkpoint preserved for next-session resume of remaining ~205 batches. Director's $50 Anthropic top-up has ~$27 remaining; another top-up needed before completing D3. Active Tools row updated; W#1 PRODUCTION-READINESS GATE prerequisite #5b → ✅ DONE; Pool-exhaustion HIGH-severity entry marked addressed for Phase 1; new entry for sister-link consolidation drift cleanup; standing instructions list refreshed. On a 401 response the wrapper calls `supabase.auth.refreshSession()` once and retries with the new access token; if refresh also fails (refresh-token expiry or transport error) the original 401 propagates through the caller's existing `PlosApiError(401)` path unchanged. Production export wraps `fetch` in an arrow `(u, i) => fetch(u, i)` to preserve the window receiver — same P-17 lesson the web side captured via the 2026-05-12 hotfix `08f10e5`. **Existing behaviors preserved:** pre-fetch no-token check still throws `PlosApiError(401, 'Not signed in')` so the 76 popup call sites' error paths are byte-identical for the no-session case; `mapFetchTransportError` wrapping applied to BOTH the first attempt AND the retry, so `TypeError → PlosApiError(0)` semantics survive across the silent-refresh path. **Branch state at session start:** W#2 was 4 commits behind `origin/main` (P-17 + Rule 27 + platform-UI fix-pair + 2026-05-12-b deploy doc-batch); director picked merge over rebase via Rule 14f forced-picker; `git merge origin/main` FAST-FORWARDED cleanly (zero conflicts) because main's 2026-05-12-b deploy session had already absorbed W#2's prior 2026-05-12 doc-batch commit `23375bd`. **Lesson learned + applied:** CORRECTIONS_LOG 2026-05-10-c entry #1 pull-main-into-feature-branch-at-session-start protocol worked as designed. **Tests added:** `api-client.test.ts` extended with 9 new tests mirroring `src/lib/authFetch.test.ts`'s 7-test pattern plus 2 extension-specific cases — TypeError on first attempt → `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable')`; TypeError on retry (after successful refresh) → `PlosApiError(0)`. Four hardening axes shipped: (i) cross-platform parametrization via `for (const pl of PLATFORMS)` loop wrapping `test.describe` blocks across amazon / ebay / etsy / walmart (route interception of each host + matching `selectedPlatform` seed because the orchestrator's `getModuleByHostname` cross-check at `orchestrator.ts:105-109` requires both hostname AND selectedPlatform to agree); (ii) tighter REGRESSION (count) threshold — observation window grew 1.5s → 2.0s after 800ms settle (was 500ms), giving ~8 MO cycles of headroom; assertion stays === 0 (the right tightness); (iii) NEW REGRESSION (identity) sub-spec — tags every initial mark with `data-plos-test-id`, asserts all tagged elements survive in the DOM after the window; catches a future partial fix that reduces churn count without eliminating the strip-and-reapply loop; (iv) NEW SELECTION-STABILITY sub-spec — draws a text selection over the first highlighted mark, asserts the serialized selection text survives a 1.0s observation window; this is the user-visible second symptom of P-14 and the blocker to clean S4-B verification per VERIFICATION_BACKLOG 2026-05-12 entry. (a.20) ✅ DONE 2026-05-12-h — W#2 polish session #16 — controlled-reproduce of Amazon P-15 FAIL with **verified-ASIN-match test setup** on real Amazon PASSED. Director walked the controlled-repro on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` with deploy-#9 build (`plos-extension-2026-05-12-g-w2-deploy-9.zip`) running on test browser: saved row ASIN `B0CTTF514L` confirmed visible in popup → navigated canonical `https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0CTTF514L` (Amazon's internal redirect appended `?th=1`) → green "Already saved" overlay appeared (sanity: orchestrator canonicalize-and-recognize path works at canonical+`?th=1` form) → navigated constructed slug-variant `https://www.amazon.com/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1` (resolves to `...?th=1`) → green overlay still appeared (orchestrator's slug-variant canonicalize works) → selected text + right-click "Add to PLOS — Captured Text" → **URL dropdown PRE-SELECTED the matching saved row automatically**, exactly as P-15's fix is designed to. **Verdict: PASS.** P-15 fix works on real Amazon for slug-variant URLs with ASIN-match-controlled test setup. **Implication for session #9's observed FAIL:** the most likely cause is test-setup ASIN mismatch (hypothesis #1 from `dc3a314` commit body's plausible-alternatives list) — director's session #9 navigation organically reached a slug-variant URL whose ASIN didn't match any of the 9 saved canonical rows. DB-inspection finding (all 9 canonical) ruled out P-21's asymmetric-canonicalize hypothesis as the explanation; today's controlled repro confirms the remaining code path works for ASIN-match-controlled inputs. **No code change today.** **Mid-walkthrough finding (not a slip, just a procedural note):** DevTools Console's "JavaScript context" selector can quietly point at an iframe context on Amazon pages (Amazon embeds many image/ad iframes for sash + ad content); director's first `location.href` evaluation returned `m.media-amazon.com/.../sash/ZjX5dSxC9UqUC39.html` (an iframe URL) instead of the top frame's URL; redirected director to read the Chrome address bar instead — confirmed top-frame URL was `https://www.amazon.com/Test-Product-Name/dp/B0CTTF514L/ref=sr_1_1?th=1`. Future Amazon-DevTools walkthroughs should default to "read the address bar" or explicitly switch the Console context to "top" before evaluating `location.href`. **Lesson captured to CORRECTIONS_LOG as INFORMATIONAL:** ASIN-match-dependent verification specs MUST construct a known-good slug-variant URL whose `/dp/{ASIN}` matches a saved row; session #9's organic-navigation pattern created ambiguity between "code bug" and "test-setup mismatch". **P-21 the defensive symmetric-canonicalize stays in W#2 polish backlog** as a future low-priority defensive improvement (covers the case where a future user pastes a slug-variant URL into the URL-add form before saving), but is NOT positioned as session-9-FAIL-fix anymore.

(a.13) 🛡️ P-17 real-fetch integration test for `authFetch.ts` production export — RECOMMENDED-NEXT on `main`. Captured 2026-05-12 from the vklf.com `Illegal invocation` hotfix root-cause analysis: the production export at `src/lib/authFetch.ts:82` passed `fetch` bare as `fetchFn`, which browsers detach from the window receiver and reject with `TypeError: Failed to execute 'fetch' on 'Window': Illegal invocation`. All 7 existing unit tests inject a fake `fetchFn` via the DI seam — the production wiring line itself was never exercised. Node's fetch is more lenient than browser fetch, so a node-context integration test wouldn't catch it either; need a jsdom or Playwright browser-context test. Scope: ~30–60 LOC test that imports the production export, exercises a real (mocked but realistic) authenticated fetch through the production wiring, asserts no `Illegal invocation` thrown. Cross-references: `src/lib/authFetch.ts:81-88` (the fixed production export); `src/lib/authFetch.test.ts` (the 7-test suite that doesn't cover production wiring); commit `08f10e5` (the 2026-05-12 hotfix). **Branch:** `main` (platform infrastructure used by W#1 + W#2 + future workflows). **Pre-session terminal commands:** `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --rebase origin main`. **Launch prompt** *(paste as first message in fresh Claude Code session)*: *"Read docs/CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER.md and follow every rule in it. I think this happens when the green overlay goes away on its own."*). **Root cause** captured 2026-05-12-g + re-verified at this session: orchestrator's MutationObserver observes `document.body` with `childList: true, subtree: true`; the green status overlay's auto-removal IS a DOM mutation; sees the overlay removal → 250ms debounce → `highlighter.refresh()` → strip-and-reapply → any active selection on a stripped `<mark>` is cleared. **Status-cell vocabulary:** 🆕 about-to-start / 🔄 active-dev / 🛠 schema-change-session / ✅ graduated / ⏸ paused / — no-current-work. See `MULTI_WORKFLOW_PROTOCOL.md` §5 for definitions.

---

## The 4-phase model (canonical)

Per `PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS.md §11`, platform development is structured around four phases. This roadmap restructures all roadmap items into these phases.

```
Phase 1 — Admin-solo tooling
  Goal: admin runs 50 Projects/week solo through all 14 workflows
  Gate to Phase 2: all 14 workflow tools built; one end-to-end Project completed

Phase 2 — Multi-user infrastructure
  Goal: platform ready for worker onboarding
  Gate to Phase 3: infrastructure complete; 1 test worker running successfully

Phase 3 — Worker ramp
  Goal: scale from 1 test worker to 50 workers at 500 Projects/week
  Gate to Phase 4: stable 500/week operation

Phase 4 — Scale hardening
  Goal: scale from 500/week to 5,000/week
  Gate: ongoing
```

---

| **Phase M** (DB refactor + API rewrite + UI rework) | ✅ COMPLETE — All 9 checkpoints + 9.5 bug-fix deployed to vklf.com |
| **Phase 1 — Admin-solo tooling** | 🔄 IN PROGRESS — Keyword Clustering (workflow 1) is the first tool; 13 more to build |
| **Phase 2 — Multi-user infrastructure** | ❌ NOT STARTED — architectural sketches exist; no build work |
| **Phase 3 — Worker ramp** | ❌ NOT STARTED |
| **Phase 4 — Scale hardening** | ❌ NOT STARTED |

## ✅ RESOLVED 2026-05-12 — W#1 PRODUCTION-READINESS GATE — D3 RETRY (full-fledged test of the Keyword Clustering tool) — NEW 2026-05-03

**Resolution (NEW 2026-05-12):** ✅ RESOLVED in W#1 Tool Graduation. Gate met at 5 of 6 prerequisites ✅ VERIFIED LIVE; prereq #1 (cold-start banner UI) 🟡 PARTIAL by deliberate director decision per (a.1) — happy-path verified live 2026-05-03-b across 5–10 hard refreshes, banner code path covered by unit tests + code review, natural-flake confirmation engineered close to never-fires by the #2/#3/#5b fixes (zero flakes across 86-batch D3 RESUME at scale 2026-05-05-d). Banner UI verification folds into normal director use as a passive watch — `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_POLISH_BACKLOG.md` §P-1 captures the passive-monitoring framing. W#1 doc structure now per HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 22: `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ARCHIVE.md` (full history) + `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_DATA_CONTRACT.md` v1 (downstream-facing) + `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_POLISH_BACKLOG.md` (deferred work). Future W#1 visits use the Resume Prompt at `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_DATA_CONTRACT.md` §7. Original 2026-05-03 entry below preserved for historical context.

## (Historical) W#1 PRODUCTION-READINESS GATE — D3 RETRY — original 2026-05-03 capture

**Status:** NEW 2026-05-03 — captures the "full-fledged test" milestone explicitly. **D3** is the canonical name (from `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md` §6 Scale Session E) for resuming the paused Bursitis Test 2 project at canvas 120 topics and running it to completion through all 291 batches → ~700+ topics. **Two attempts to date were both PARTIAL** (2026-05-01-b paused at 84 topics; 2026-05-02-e paused at 120 topics). This entry collects the prerequisites + success criteria + post-pass action so the milestone is tracked by every session, not re-derived each time. Captured at the director's request 2026-05-03 after end-of-session realization that the docs name "D3" but never explicitly schedule the retry.

**Why this entry exists:** prior pattern was reactive — partial run → discover bottleneck → fix → partial run again. Without an explicit milestone with prerequisites, the prerequisites drift in priority and D3 retry never gets scheduled. This entry makes D3 the named target so every session sees it at start.

**Prerequisites (in dependency order):**

1. 🟡 **Cold-start render-layer fix verification (PARTIAL — 2026-05-03-b)** — code shipped 2026-05-03 (commit `52db8e3`); live happy-path verified 2026-05-03-b on vklf.com against Bursitis Test 2 (5-10 hard refreshes; workspace populated normally on every attempt; NO banner fired during the test window because no flake fired); banner UI live verification (yellow "Retrying load…" + red "Could not load X — Click here to retry" + click-to-retry button) **DEFERRED** to next natural flake event during D3 retry or another Auto-Analyze run. Director chose Option B at the 2026-05-03-b verification fork (accept partial; alternative was DevTools-induced controlled flake test). **Effectively folded into the D3 run itself** rather than requiring a dedicated session — banner UI passes/fails in flight during D3. The "🟡 CODE FIX SHIPPED 2026-05-03 — Cold-start hard-refresh" entry below stays at 🟡 (not ✅) until banner-UI confirmation lands.
2. 🟡 **Recency-stickiness fix (CODE FIX SHIPPED 2026-05-03-c — pending live verification at next D3 attempt)** — sister-link op deferral to consolidation-only + Q5→B touch-semantics refinement. Two-part fix per `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md` §6 D3 partial validation outcome: (1) V4 prompt drops `ADD_SISTER_LINK` + `REMOVE_SISTER_LINK` from per-batch operation vocabulary + applier gains `regularBatchMode: true` flag (mirrors `consolidationMode: true` pattern; rejects sister-link ops atomically when set) + AutoAnalyze.tsx wires `regularBatchMode: true` at the three regular-batch apply call sites (defense in depth: prompt + applier + tests); (2) `recordTouchesFromOps` stamps only topics whose own structural identity changed (ADD_TOPIC drops parent stamp; MOVE_TOPIC drops newParent stamp; MERGE_TOPICS drops source stamp; SPLIT_TOPIC drops source stamp; DELETE_TOPIC drops deleted-id stamp; MOVE_KEYWORD drops source stamp; sister-link ops drop both endpoint stamps). 15 new tests; 299/299 src/lib pass; tsc + build clean; lint baseline parity. **Live verification deferred to D3 retry itself** — the per-batch input-cost trajectory across the run is the success signal (per-batch input cost stays stable rather than growing monotonically). Director must re-paste the updated V4 prompt into the Auto-Analyze UI before the next D3 attempt. (Item (a) on the W#1 standing instructions; was item (c) before recommendation reordering.)
3. 🟡 **Underlying ~25% pgbouncer/Prisma flake-rate fix (FIRST RATE-FIX SHIPPED 2026-05-04-b — SCOPE REFRAMED 2026-05-04-c)** — measurement-instrumentation pass landed 2026-05-04 (`58fe5e6`); first rate-fix landed 2026-05-04-b (`c34ddc9` + `1e42394`). All three commits now LIVE on vklf.com via the 2026-05-04-b push. **🚨 SCOPE REFRAME 2026-05-04-c:** D3 retry's batch 1 surfaced `FATAL: Max client connections reached` errors — **a different failure mode than the rate-fix targets**. `withRetry` retries transient codes P1001/P1002/P1008/P2034; pool exhaustion has no Prisma code (just the FATAL message embedded in `error.message`); retrying it makes pool pressure worse, not better. The 2026-05-04-b rate-fix is bounded to transient flakes — its natural-data verification window remains open for that scope, but **pool exhaustion is a separate failure mode that requires a separate fix** (see prerequisite #4 below). Outstanding within this prerequisite's scope: live verification of the transient-flake portion via natural `[FLAKE]` data accumulation (1-3 days normal director use OR multi-batch run after #4 ships). **What the rate-fix did:** option (c) "withRetry parity extension" first-pass complete. Commit 1 wraps the silent auth + activity helpers (`lib/auth.ts` `verifyProjectAuth`'s findUnique + `verifyProjectWorkflowAuth`'s upsert; `lib/workflow-status.ts` `markWorkflowActive`'s find + create + update paths + `ensureProjectWorkflow`'s upsert) — the universal silent multiplier from the prior session's audit. Commit 2 wraps the apply-pipeline writes (POST `/canvas/rebuild` including the multi-second `$transaction`; POST/PATCH/DELETE `/canvas/nodes`; PATCH `/canvas`; sister-links + pathways creates/deletes; keywords routes + `/keywords/[keywordId]`; removed-keywords + restore). Combined coverage lifts ~4% → ~80%+ of authenticated apply-pipeline paths. Idempotency tradeoff for unique-constraint-free creates documented; rare retry-after-partial-commit duplicates accepted vs 500s. **What's pending:** live verification — either at next D3 attempt (the most thorough path) or via natural `[FLAKE]` data window accumulating from normal director use over the next 1-3 days (the rate-fix's effectiveness is empirically gradeable from `retried=true` vs `retried=false` ratios in Vercel log counts). **What was deliberately deferred:** option (a) Supabase plan-tier change (**director confirmed 2026-05-04-b end-of-session: NOT currently on Pro**; upgrade-to-Pro recommended; director's upgrade timing TBD — see "🟡 FIRST RATE-FIX SHIPPED 2026-05-04-b" entry below for full plan-tier discussion), option (b) Prisma client lifecycle / `@prisma/adapter-pg` audit, option (d) atomic-rebuild differentialization, option (e) Phase 2 server-side execution architecture (long-term). Item (a) on the W#1 standing instructions.

4. ✅ **Supabase Pro upgrade for pool-capacity headroom — DONE 2026-05-04-b** (director upgraded at end of session_2026-05-04-b; not in 2026-05-04-c — corrected post-original-commit when 2026-05-04-c initially framed this as "❌ NOT STARTED"). **HOWEVER:** tonight's pool exhaustion fired **ON Pro tier**, not Free — meaning Pro's higher default capacity is still insufficient for the apply-pipeline's ~22-parallel-PATCH burst pattern. Pro upgrade was necessary but not sufficient. The actual gate-blocker on the pool dimension is now prerequisite #5 below.

5. 🟡 **Pool-exhaustion mitigation (POOL-TUNE TRIED 2026-05-04-d — INSUFFICIENT)** — director executed the three-step investigation tonight: Pro tier confirmed; compute size revealed as **"Nano"** (Pro plan ≠ bigger compute — Pro just unlocks the configurable knob); pool size at default **15** (Postgres `max_connections` capped at **60** on Nano per dashboard's 80% warning); bumped to **40** (67% of cap, safely below 80% warning threshold). Small-batch test on Bursitis Test 2 batch 2: **wall did not clear**. Same FATAL: Max client connections reached at canvas 124. Vercel log expansion reveals actual burst is **55-60+ parallel PATCHes per batch (NOT the 22 estimated 2026-05-04-c)** — counted ~40 successful + ~18 failed PATCHes in a 3-second window. Pool 40 absorbs ~40 of the burst; pool 48 (max safe under 80% warning) ~10 short; pool 60 (100% of max_connections) at Supabase's instability line. **Pool tune at Nano compute is structurally insufficient.** The pool size 40 stays in place as a secondary buffer (still helping absorb a portion of the burst), but the structural answer is the code-fix in #5b below. (Replaces the 2026-05-04-c "Pool-exhaustion investigation" framing — that investigation completed 2026-05-04-d; the answer is "pool tune alone is not enough; code-fix required.")

5b. ❌ **Parallel-PATCH-burst reduction code-fix (NEW 2026-05-04-d — gate-blocking)** — three approaches per ROADMAP item (d) below: (i) client-side worker queue serializing the 55-60+ parallel PATCHes (~10-20 LOC, lowest risk, ZERO quality impact, ~3-6s slower per batch); (ii) bulk PATCH /keywords endpoint collapse (existing endpoint accepts arrays; needs audit for field coverage; faster, fewer round trips); (iii) atomic batch into canvas-rebuild $transaction (largest refactor; ZERO quality impact; ELIMINATES the P2025 race condition found 2026-05-04-d for free; most thorough). All three approaches preserve clustering quality (model produces same operations regardless of how they're applied). Per director's standing preference for most-thorough-and-reliable, approach (iii) is the likely recommendation pending code review. **Effort: 1-2 sessions design + first-pass implementation.** Live verification at next D3 attempt after code-fix lands. Director chooses approach at next session's start after Claude reads operation-applier.ts + AutoAnalyze.tsx + canvas-rebuild route. **STATUS UPDATE 2026-05-05:** Director chose approach (iii) atomic-batch-into-rebuild-transaction; commit `0caa200` SHIPPED + verified live across 50 batches + 5 consolidations; **STATUS UPDATE 2026-05-05-d:** RE-VERIFIED at scale across 86 additional batches + 8 consolidations during D3 RESUME COMPLETED. ✅ DONE.

6. ✅ **Sister-link consolidation drift cleanup — Option A invisibility (NEW 2026-05-05-b; VERIFIED LIVE AT SCALE 2026-05-05-d).** Code SHIPPED 2026-05-05-b — sister-link operations and the Sister Nodes column made invisible to the consolidation model via three layers: (a) Consolidation V4 prompt sections 1+2 stripped of all sister-link references; (b) new `omitSisterNodesColumn?: boolean` flag on `buildOperationsInputTsv` drops the column from TSV input (consolidation call site passes `true`); (c) applier `consolidationMode` rejects ADD_SISTER_LINK + REMOVE_SISTER_LINK (in addition to ADD_TOPIC + ADD_KEYWORD) as silent backstop. ~115 LOC across 5 files; 9 new tests; 336/336 src/lib pass. Director re-pasted updated Consolidation Initial Prompt + Primer before D3 resume. **VERIFIED LIVE AT SCALE 2026-05-05-d:** D3 RESUME COMPLETED ran 86 batches + 8 consolidations; sister-link count stayed at 82 throughout including two stress-test bulk-archive consolidations (cons #4 archived 31 keywords; cons #8 archived 117 keywords); zero ADD_SISTER_LINK / REMOVE_SISTER_LINK ops emitted by model; zero applier rejection events (canonical substring `is not allowed in consolidation mode (sister links` matched zero times in Vercel logs). Three-layer defense held perfectly at production scale.

**Why this is gate-blocking:** even with all four other prerequisites perfect (cold-start fix, recency-stickiness fix, transient-flake rate-fix, Pro upgrade), the apply pipeline's parallel PATCH burst will continue hitting the pool wall until either Pro's pool config is tuned higher OR the burst pattern itself is reduced code-side. Pool exhaustion is a capacity-vs-demand problem; retries cannot solve it.

**Success criteria (D3 — copied verbatim from `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md` §6):**

- Bursitis run reaches **≥ 600 topics**
- No quality regression (per-batch reconciliation continues clean)
- Per-batch wall-clock matches or improves V3
- Reconciliation continues clean across the whole run
- Consolidation passes fire automatically every 10 batches as designed
- Per-batch input cost stays stable rather than growing monotonically (the input-context wall solved)

**On D3 pass:**

## 🚨 HIGH (2026-05-04-c; updated 2026-05-04-d) — Pool exhaustion under apply-pipeline burst (`FATAL: Max client connections reached`) — fires ON PRO TIER; pool tune INSUFFICIENT; code-fix required

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-04-c. **POOL-TUNE TRIED 2026-05-04-d — INSUFFICIENT.** Director bumped pool size from default 15 to 40 (67% of Nano compute's Postgres max_connections=60 cap; comfortably below Supabase's 80% instability warning). Small-batch test on Bursitis Test 2 batch 2 produced same failure. Pool tune at this compute tier is structurally insufficient; **code-fix is now PRIMARY and gate-blocking** (see "Fix layers" below). Pool size 40 stays in place as secondary buffer.

**🚨 BURST-SIZE CORRECTION 2026-05-04-d:** the 2026-05-04-c estimate of "~22 parallel PATCH /keywords/[id] calls per batch" was WRONG by a factor of ~2.5×. Vercel log expansion in tonight's session revealed **the actual burst is 55-60+ parallel PATCH calls per batch** (counted: ~40 successful PATCH 200s + ~18 PATCH 500s in a 3-second window during Bursitis Test 2 batch 2 with 19 operations applied). The 2026-05-04-c estimate was based on a Vercel view that showed only failures (collapsed/filtered); tonight's expanded view shows the full burst. All prior reasoning that assumed "22-burst" needs recalibration: at 50-Phase-3-workers × 60-burst = **3,000 simultaneous connections at peak** — uncoverable by any compute tier upgrade Supabase offers (largest tier 16XL caps at ~12,000 max client + ~128 default pool size; even with 3000 connections of headroom, the per-pool default would need radical custom tuning).

**The problem:** the apply-pipeline fires **~55-60+ parallel PATCH /keywords/[id]** requests per batch (one per touched keyword) when applying batch results to the canvas. (Was estimated ~22 in 2026-05-04-c entry; corrected 2026-05-04-d after Vercel log expansion.) Each parallel request opens a Prisma connection through Supabase's pgbouncer transaction pooler at port 6543. Supabase Free-tier pool capacity is overwhelmed. Postgres returns `FATAL: Max client connections reached` to every request beyond the limit. The error surfaces as `PrismaClientUnknownRequestError` or `PrismaClientInitializationError` with NO Prisma error code — just the FATAL message embedded in `error.message`. Cascade: ~28 cascading 500s within ~0.1 sec at 8:30:40 PM in the 2026-05-04-c D3 batch 1 (~22 PATCH /keywords/[id] failures + 4 GET /canvas + 2 GET /canvas/nodes failures).

**Why the 2026-05-04-b withRetry rate-fix doesn't help:** withRetry retries Prisma codes P1001/P1002/P1008/P2034. Pool exhaustion has no Prisma code. Even if it WERE matched as transient, retrying immediately worsens pool pressure (each retry attempts another connection while the pool is full). **Retries cannot fix capacity.**

**Fix layers (in order — REVISED 2026-05-04-d after empirical pool-tune test):**

1. ✅ **Supabase Pro upgrade — DONE 2026-05-04-b.** Pro plan unlocks the configurable pool-size knob (Free hides it); but Pro plan default compute is **Nano** (smallest available) — the upgrade did NOT auto-bump compute. Underlying Postgres `max_connections` stays at 60 on Nano regardless of plan tier.

1a. 🟡 **Pool size config knob tuning — TRIED 2026-05-04-d, INSUFFICIENT.** Director bumped pool size from default 15 → 40 (67% of Nano compute's 60-cap; comfortably below Supabase's 80% warning threshold which fires at 48). Small-batch test outcome: pool 40 absorbed ~40 of the 55-60+ burst; ~18+ still failed with FATAL. Pool 48 (max safe under warning) would be ~10 short of the burst peak. Pool 60 (100% of max_connections) hits Supabase's instability warning. **No safe pool size at Nano compute absorbs the actual burst.** The pool size 40 stays in place as a secondary buffer (still helping); the structural answer is layer (2) below.

2. ❌ **Apply-pipeline parallel-PATCH-burst reduction — NOW PRIMARY (gate-blocking 2026-05-04-d).** Three approaches (revised with corrected burst size):
   - **(i) Worker queue (client-side throttle).** Serialize the 55-60+ parallel PATCHes via a small worker queue (e.g., 10 simultaneous max). ~10-20 LOC change in `AutoAnalyze.tsx`. **Quality impact: ZERO** (same operations, same final canvas state — just sequenced over slightly more wall-clock time). Speed impact: each batch's apply phase ~3-6 seconds longer (negligible vs. ~90-second model phase). Risk: lowest. Doesn't fix the P2025 race condition (see separate entry).
   - **(ii) Bulk PATCH /keywords endpoint.** Use the existing `PATCH /keywords` endpoint that accepts arrays. Send all 55-60 updates in one HTTP request instead of separate ones. **Quality impact: ZERO** if the bulk endpoint covers all needed fields (audit needed; may need small extensions). Speed impact: faster (fewer round trips). Risk: medium. May or may not fix P2025 race depending on internal sequencing.
   - **(iii) Atomic batch into canvas-rebuild $transaction.** Fold the keyword updates into the same `$transaction` that already rebuilds the canvas. One transaction; all updates atomic. **Quality impact: ZERO. Reliability impact: IMPROVES — eliminates the P2025 race for free.** Speed impact: potentially faster (one transaction vs. many round trips). Risk: highest — larger refactor; need to keep transaction within timeout bounds. **Most thorough and reliable per director's standing preference; likely recommendation pending code review.**

   Director chooses approach at next session's start after Claude reads `src/lib/operation-applier.ts` + `src/app/projects/[projectId]/keyword-clustering/components/AutoAnalyze.tsx` + the canvas-rebuild route. Effort: 1-2 sessions design + first-pass implementation.

3. **Pre-flight pool-config visibility check (LOW priority addition).** Pre-flight runner could surface the active Supabase compute tier + configured pool size at run start, with a warning if the pool size looks too small for the expected batch's parallel-PATCH count. Nice-to-have once layer (2) ships. ~10-20 LOC.

4. **Compute tier upgrade (LOW priority — symptom treatment, not root cause).** Going Nano → Micro/Small adds CPU/RAM but max_connections stays at 200 on those tiers. Medium ($60/mo) gets max_connections=400; Large ($110/mo) gets 600; XL ($210/mo) gets 700; 2XL ($410/mo) gets 1500. Even 2XL's 1500 won't comfortably absorb Phase 3's 50 × 60 = 3000 burst. **Compute upgrade is treating-the-symptom money; layer (2) is the structural answer.** Could be considered as a Phase 2 bridge if Phase 2 multi-user transition starts before layer (2) ships, but not before then.

**Phase 3 implication (zoom-out per Rule 16):** at 50 concurrent workers (Phase 3 scale target per `PLATFORM_REQUIREMENTS.md §1`), pool exhaustion will be vastly worse than today's solo-director load. Pro upgrade is necessary but not sufficient — the apply-pipeline parallel-burst pattern itself needs revisiting before Phase 3, OR the connection pool needs further tuning beyond Pro's defaults. Architectural concern that propagates to W#2-W#14 because they share the same DB + pool.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-04-C-D3-RETRY-PARTIAL-POOL-EXHAUSTION-FINDING STATE block (full forensic detail); `PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md §10` "🚨 Connection pool exhaustion under apply-pipeline burst" entry (platform-level technical debt registry); `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-05-04-c INFORMATIONAL entry (process lesson on rate-fix scope vs. discovered failure mode).

## 🔍 NEW MEDIUM (2026-05-04-d) — P2025 race condition between canvas-rebuild's archive and parallel keyword PATCHes

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-04-d during pool-tune small-batch test Vercel log analysis.

**The finding:** during Bursitis Test 2 batch 2's apply pipeline at 23:08:06 UTC, the canvas-rebuild `$transaction` archived 2 keywords (Greg Hayes proper-name + Capsella bursa-pastoris botanical Latin — both correct semantic calls). In the same window, the parallel PATCH /keywords/[id] burst was firing. One of the failed PATCHes (keyword `78c696c5`) returned a **DIFFERENT error type** than the rest of the burst: `P2025 "No record was found for an update"` — a Prisma "record doesn't exist" error, NOT pool exhaustion (which has no Prisma code).

**Most likely cause:** race condition where the rebuild archived/relocated the keyword while a parallel PATCH was still in-flight against it. The PATCH read the keyword state pre-archive, computed an update, fired the update — and by the time the update reached Postgres, the keyword had been moved/archived by the rebuild transaction.

**Why MEDIUM, not HIGH:** isolated occurrence (1 of 58 PATCHes); the operation that "lost" was a placement update on an archived keyword, which is by definition a no-op once archived. No data corruption; no quality impact. But the failure surfaces as a 500 to the user, which is bad UX.

**Fix relationship to Pool exhaustion entry:**
- Approach (i) worker queue: doesn't fix this; the race can still happen at the boundary between rebuild-transaction-finishing and the next worker-queue-batch firing.
- Approach (ii) bulk PATCH endpoint: may or may not fix this depending on whether the endpoint sequences the updates relative to the rebuild transaction.
- Approach (iii) atomic-batch-into-rebuild-transaction: **eliminates this race for free** because all updates apply atomically with the archive. Strong reason to favor (iii).

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-04-D STATE block; the "Bonus finding" section.

---

## 🔍 NEW MEDIUM (2026-05-04-d) — Pre-flight check results not visible to director on Resume click

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-04-d after director's repeated correction across multiple sessions.

**The problem:** when director clicks the Resume button in the Auto-Analyze overlay, the 12 pre-flight checks (P1-P12 per 2026-05-02 instrumentation, including the new P11+P12 for consolidation prompt validation) run somewhere — server side, browser console, or silently — but **DO NOT display visibly in the overlay UI to the director**. Director has flagged this in 2026-05-04-d session and earlier sessions. Implication: director cannot independently verify which checks passed/failed before the run fires. If a check fails silently and the run aborts, director has no visibility into why. Even worse: Claude has repeatedly mis-described the click→pre-flight visibility flow because there's no observable surface to verify Claude's claims against.

**Suggested implementation:** a small panel/modal that appears on Resume click showing P1-P12 with ✓/✗ + brief description per check; persists until first batch starts. ~50-100 LOC UI work in `AutoAnalyze.tsx`.

**Priority:** elevated within Phase-1 polish bundle because it (a) affects director's ability to self-verify a pre-run safety check and (b) eliminates a recurring slip pattern from Claude (assuming visibility that doesn't exist). Standalone session candidate or first item in next polish bundle.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-04-D STATE block standing instruction (d); `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-05-04-d entry on recurring pre-flight visibility slip.

The "Auto-Analyze started" banner shows Model + Processing Mode + Scope but also omits Thinking Mode/Budget. Director flagged this 2026-05-05-d when prompted to verify thinking config — had to open settings panel manually because activity log didn't surface it.

## 🚨 NEW HIGH (2026-05-05-d) — Auto-Analyze action history table + per-action undo — ✅ TRACKED IN POLISH_BACKLOG H-1 as of 2026-05-12

**Tracked in:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_POLISH_BACKLOG.md` §H-1 as of 2026-05-12 W#1 graduation. Original entry below preserved for ROADMAP-level visibility; future status updates land in the polish backlog.

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-05-d when director surfaced as a question: "Where is the table where I can check all the actions taken by the AI model during auto-analyze and how can I interact with those actions and undo specific actions." Investigation confirmed feature does NOT exist today.

**The gap (verified by code investigation 2026-05-05-d):**

1. **`useEmitAuditEvent()` is a Phase 2 stub** (`src/lib/workflow-components/use-workflow-context.tsx:193-206`): explicitly marked "Phase 2 stub"; implementation is a no-op:
   ```javascript
   const emitAuditEvent: (eventType: string, payload: object) => Promise<void> =
     useCallback(async () => {
       // No-op in Phase 1.
     }, []);
   ```
2. **No persistent audit table in Prisma** (`prisma/schema.prisma`): no `AuditEvent` or similar table. `RemovedKeyword` (only event-like model) is for soft-archives only, not an audit log.
3. **Activity log during runs is in-memory only** (`AutoAnalyze.tsx:77-81, 1159-1161`): `LogEntry[]` lives in component local state; cleared when overlay closes; never persisted.
4. **Undo exists only at workflow level** — destructive `<ResetWorkflowButton>` (type-the-project-name confirmation) wipes all workflow data. No per-action undo.

**In practice:** the 2026-05-05-d D3 resume run executed hundreds of AI ops across 86 batches + 8 consolidations. On-canvas results persist, but there's no "this is what happened during the 5:02 PM run" record. Director cannot retrospectively review or surgically undo any individual op (e.g., reverse a specific bulk-archive item).

**Work split (each is its own item under this parent entry):**

1. **DB schema design.** New `AuditEvent` table (eventType, payload JSON, projectWorkflowId, sessionId, batchNumber, timestamp, actionableUntil, undone bool, undoneAt, undoneBy). Consider partition strategy + retention policy.
2. **Emission wiring.** Wire `useEmitAuditEvent()` from no-op to real DB insert in every operation-applier path (`operation-applier.ts`). Confirm coverage across all op types (CREATE_TOPIC, MERGE_TOPICS, MOVE_KEYWORD, ARCHIVE_KEYWORD, ADD/REMOVE_SISTER_LINK, etc.).
3. **UI surface.** New "Action History" tab in AST panel — scrollable list of all ops, filters by run / batch / op type, expand-row for full op detail.
4. **Per-action undo (DESIGN BEFORE BUILD).** Some ops are easy reverses (ADD_SISTER_LINK ↔ REMOVE_SISTER_LINK); others compose nontrivially (CREATE_TOPIC + N MOVE_KEYWORD ops to it; ARCHIVE_KEYWORD + downstream consolidation effects). Need design session before implementing — surface to director then.

**Effort estimate:** 3-5 sessions total. Could parallelize with W#1 graduation since this work is largely additive.

**Why HIGH priority:** without action history + undo, users have no surgical recovery path from over-aggressive AI decisions. The D3 run's bulk-archive consolidation #8 (117 archives) is a concrete example where review-after-the-fact would be valuable. Currently the only recovery is full workflow reset.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-05-D STATE block; `WORKFLOW_COMPONENTS_LIBRARY_DESIGN.md` (Phase 2 stub mention of `useEmitAuditEvent()`).

## 🔍 NEW MEDIUM (2026-05-05-d) — Auto-Analyze cost forecasting + credit-balance check (consolidates two findings) — ✅ TRACKED IN POLISH_BACKLOG M-2 as of 2026-05-12

**Tracked in:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_POLISH_BACKLOG.md` §M-2 as of 2026-05-12 W#1 graduation. Original entry below preserved for ROADMAP-level visibility.

**Status:** Two findings from 2026-05-05-d D3 resume session consolidate into one feature:

**Finding A — Anthropic API credit exhaustion mid-run (NEW operational risk).** Batch 85 failed all 3 retries at 8:06:52 PM with `HTTP 400: Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API. Please go to Plans & Billing to upgrade or purchase credits.` Run halted ~36 min until director topped up credits at ~8:42:28 PM. Batch 85 attempt 4 succeeded post-resume. **No proactive warning** before failure; pre-flight runner does NOT check credit balance vs. estimated run cost.

**Finding B — Cost forecasting feature director request.** Auto-Analyze overlay shows running cumulative cost but no estimated remaining or estimated total. Director requested both surfaces.

**Proposed combined implementation:**

**Sliding-window cost estimator algorithm:**
- `avg_batch_cost` = average of last 10 successful batches' costs (sliding window — auto-adapts as canvas grows; falls back to first-3 average until 10 batches have run)
- `batches_remaining` = `total_batches - completed_batches`
- `avg_cons_cost` = sliding average of all consolidations so far, or $0.55 fallback until 2+ cons have fired
- `cons_remaining` = `floor(batches_remaining / cons_cadence)` (default cadence 10)
- `estimated_remaining_cost` = `(avg_batch × batches_remaining) + (avg_cons × cons_remaining)`
- `estimated_total_cost` = `current_cumulative + estimated_remaining`

**Display in Auto-Analyze overlay header (updates after each batch):**
*"Spent: $X.XX | Est. remaining: $Y.YY | Est. total: $Z.ZZ"*

**Credit-balance integration:**
- Query Anthropic balance at run start + every 10 batches via the appropriate Anthropic SDK endpoint
- Display "Available credits: $A.AA" alongside the cost estimates
- Warn yellow if `estimated_remaining > available × 0.5`
- Warn red if `estimated_remaining > available × 0.8`
- Abort with clear message if `estimated_remaining > available` (don't launch a run that can't complete)

**Why sliding-window matters:** D3 resume's per-batch cost grew $0.46 → $0.63 (early → late) due to canvas growth (195 → 242 topics). Fixed-average estimator would have under-projected by ~30% by run end. Sliding window auto-adapts.

**Effort:** ~50-100 LOC in `AutoAnalyze.tsx` + small Anthropic balance query helper. Probable single-session build.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-05-D STATE block "🚨 New findings surfaced this session" #1 and #2; `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md` §6 D3 RESUME COMPLETED outcome (cost trajectory data).

---

## 🔍 NEW MEDIUM (2026-05-05-d) — Late-run validation-retry rate telemetry — ✅ TRACKED IN POLISH_BACKLOG M-3 as of 2026-05-12

**Tracked in:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_POLISH_BACKLOG.md` §M-3 as of 2026-05-12 W#1 graduation. Original entry below preserved for ROADMAP-level visibility.

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-05-d during D3 RESUME COMPLETED 86-batch run.

**The finding:** earlier batches (1-30) missed at most 1 keyword on first validation attempt and recovered on retry. Late batches at canvas ≥235 (specifically batches 78 and 80) missed 5 keywords each on first attempt; cost doubled twice ($1.316 + $1.187). Retry recovered cleanly both times.

**Hypothesis:** at >150k input tokens with a large TSV input, the model occasionally drops keywords from the output payload. Earlier batches had smaller inputs and consistent 1-or-fewer-miss validation behavior; the 5-miss pattern at canvas ≥235 may indicate an emergent behavior that wasn't visible at smaller scale.

**Recommended fix posture:** **instrument first, decide if behavioral fix is needed later.** Add a mid-run dashboard metric "validation retry rate per 10 batches" (sliding window). If it crosses 30% in any 10-batch window, surface as a yellow warning in the activity log. Don't try to fix the underlying model behavior yet — measure first across multiple D3-class runs to see if it generalizes or was specific to this canvas/run shape.

**Effort:** ~30-50 LOC instrumentation. Behavioral fix (if needed later) would be its own session — possibly batch-size adjustment at large canvas, or input-shape audit.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-05-D STATE block "🚨 New findings surfaced this session" #3.

---

## 🔍 NEW LOW (2026-05-05-d) — Improve per-batch celebrity-homograph detection

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-05-d during D3 RESUME COMPLETED 86-batch run.

**The pattern:** consolidation #4 (after batch 40) archived 31 celebrity+bursa homograph keywords in one bulk pass. Consolidation #8 (after batch 80) archived 117 in one pass. All were keywords like "[celebrity name] bursa" referring to the Turkish city of Bursa, not the anatomical bursa.

**Two interpretations:**
1. Per-batch model is too lenient with celebrity-bursa pairings when seen in isolation; the consolidation model with whole-canvas view recognizes the pattern and bulk-archives.
2. Volume-sorted queue puts these keywords late, so they entered the canvas late and got archived by consolidation rather than per-batch.

**Either way, it's working** — consolidation does what consolidation is supposed to do. But 117 archives in one consolidation pass is expensive (cost $0.699, ~3 min) and slow vs. catching them per-batch.

**Recommended fix:** add a "celebrity-name homograph" few-shot example to the per-batch V4 prompt (`docs/AUTO_ANALYZE_PROMPT_V4.md`). Example pattern: "Queries combining a well-known person's name with a homograph term (e.g., 'X bursa' where bursa could refer to the Turkish city) — archive unless the query has additional context indicating the medical condition." Test on a small Bursitis Test 2 follow-up run.

**Effort:** ~15-30 LOC prompt edit + small validation run. Low priority — current behavior produces correct results.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-05-D STATE block "🚨 New findings surfaced this session" #4; D3 RESUME COMPLETED activity log (consolidations #4 + #8).

## 🔍 W#2 polish backlog (NEW 2026-05-08-b entries; EXTENDED 2026-05-08-c with P-3 through P-6; P-7 + P-8 added 2026-05-08-d; P-4 + P-5 + P-7 + P-8 ✅ SHIPPED at code level 2026-05-08-d AND ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-09 in attempt #5; P-9 + P-10 added 2026-05-09 attempt #5; P-2 + P-9 + P-10 ✅ SHIPPED at code level 2026-05-10-b in W#2 polish session #8; P-9 + P-10 ✅ DEPLOYED + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED on vklf.com 2026-05-10-c (W#2 → main deploy session #2) + ✅ RE-CONFIRMED on vklf.com 2026-05-10-d (W#2 → main deploy session #3 — ALL 19 STEPS PASSED on fresh sideload after rebase-merge); **P-2 ✅ DEPLOYED 2026-05-10-c to main + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-10-c on local extension build via corrected sequence (W#2 polish session #9) + P-2 doc updates ✅ MERGED to main 2026-05-10-d via deploy session #3 — P-2 considered fully verified per byte-identical-bundle reasoning** (mapFetchTransportError code path is in extension JS that runs identically regardless of vklf.com vs local — fetch URL `https://vklf.com/api/...` and offline TypeError → PlosApiError mapping happens client-side; no server-side dependency); **P-3 BROADER scope (selectedProjectId + selectedPlatform) ✅ SHIPPED at code level 2026-05-10-e in W#2 polish session #10** (commit `49d396e` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; new `UserExtensionState` record-type; new `/api/extension-state` route; new `extension-state-sync.ts` orchestrator; popup App.tsx wired; 13 new sync tests; 233/233 ext tests pass; schema landed in prod via `prisma db push` 1.16s; W#2 → main deploy + cross-device browser-verify pending the next deploy session))
