P-9 + P-10 surfaced during 2026-05-09 Waypoint #1 attempt #5 (live-page Highlight Terms 500KB cap too aggressive — blocks Ebay + Walmart pages, fires repeatedly on Walmart heavy-SPA + AlreadySavedOverlay banner intermittent on Walmart detail pages). **Shipped + browser-verified:** P-4 + P-5 + P-7 + P-8 ✅ both code AND browser-verified 2026-05-09; P-6 ✅ shipped + deployed + browser-verified 2026-05-09-b; P-3 narrowed Highlight Terms ✅ shipped + deployed + browser-verified 2026-05-10-b; **P-9 + P-10 ✅ shipped + deployed + browser-verified 2026-05-10-c + re-confirmed on fresh sideload 2026-05-10-d**; **P-2 ✅ shipped 2026-05-10-b + deployed 2026-05-10-c + browser-verified 2026-05-10-c on local extension build via corrected sequence + doc updates merged to main 2026-05-10-d**. **Shipped at code level + push pending next W#2 → main deploy session:** P-1 ✅ (W#2 polish session #11 2026-05-10-f, commit `d715cde`). **Stays open:** none. (P-3 broader scope shipped 2026-05-10-e + deployed 2026-05-10-f to main but verification deferred — see Active Tools W#2 row Next Session.)

**(P-1) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-10-f at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #11; commit `d715cde`). vklf.com auth: silent token refresh + retry on 401.** Pre-fix: when a vklf.com user came back to the site after >1 hour (the Supabase access-token TTL), the page surfaced a 401 'Invalid or expired token' error directly (e.g., `Could not load Projects (401): {"error":"Invalid or expired token"}`); user had to manually sign out + sign back in to recover. **Fix shipped 2026-05-10-f:** `src/lib/authFetch.ts` refactored to a tested factory shape — new `makeAuthFetch({supabase, fetchFn})` factory accepts deps for unit-test substitution; production export `authFetch` is bound against a lazily-initialised Supabase singleton + global `fetch`. Lazy init required because the Node test runner imports `authFetch.ts` without `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` set in env, and an eager `createClient()` at module scope crashed the test process. Wrapper behavior: (1) read cached session, throw "Not authenticated — no active session" if no session at all (existing behavior preserved); (2) build `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header + first fetch attempt; (3) NEW — if status === 401, call `supabase.auth.refreshSession()`; (4) NEW — if refresh succeeds AND a new `access_token` exists, rebuild headers with the new token + re-fire the fetch ONCE; return the retry's response (no further retries — second 401 is final); (5) NEW — if refresh fails (`error` or no new token), return the original 401 unchanged. Non-401 statuses (200/404/500/etc.) and the no-session-at-all path are unchanged. **Failure-path forced-picker per Rule 14f:** director picked Option (a) — return original 401 to caller as-is when refresh ALSO fails, so existing error handlers at the 76 call sites behave exactly like today; minimal change, zero caller-code edits, no global navigation side-effect from a low-level fetch wrapper. **Rule 15 autonomous pick noted in commit:** refresh-failure caused by network-offline gets the same treatment as refresh-failure caused by expired refresh token — both return the original 401. **Test coverage shipped (`src/lib/authFetch.test.ts` NEW):** 7 tests via `node:test` + `node:assert/strict` covering 200 happy path, 401-refresh-success-retry-200, 401-refresh-success-retry-401-no-loop, 401-refresh-fails-returns-original, 500-no-refresh, no-session-throws, POST-body-and-caller-headers-preserved-across-retry. **Verification:** **400/400 src/lib tests pass** (was 393; +7 authFetch tests); `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run build` clean (51 routes, no change); `npx eslint src/lib/authFetch.ts src/lib/authFetch.test.ts` clean; project-wide `npx eslint src` 13 errors / 39 warnings — exact baseline parity (zero new errors/warnings introduced). **Push status:** push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping` does NOT redeploy vklf.com (only push-to-main does). Deploy + browser-verify of the 1-hour-token-expiry scenario rolls into the next W#2 → main deploy session along with the deferred P3B verification of P-3 broader scope. **Cross-references:** `src/lib/authFetch.ts` (the factory + production export); `src/lib/authFetch.test.ts` (7 tests); `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-05-08-b informational entry (Finding 2) on deploy-session verification-scope gap (the slip that produced the original P-1 capture); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #2 row (where the 401 surfaced) + the new "Polish session #11" section appended this session for the next deploy-session browser-verify.

**(P-2) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #8) + ✅ DEPLOYED 2026-05-10-c to `main` (W#2 → main deploy session #2, commit `d2e2115`) + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-10-c on local extension build via corrected sequence captured today (W#2 polish session #9). Corrected test sequence:** sign in normally with WiFi ON (popup loads project list cleanly via cached JWT) → close popup → turn OFF system WiFi (NOT DevTools Network → Offline; the system-level WiFi toggle is the corrected trigger) → re-open popup (ProjectPicker remounts → useEffect fires → `listProjects()` → fetch throws `TypeError("Failed to fetch")` → `mapFetchTransportError` converts to `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable — check your connection.')` → ProjectPicker's `.catch` renders the friendly red box) → red error box reads exactly **"Couldn't load your projects (0): Network unreachable — check your connection."** matching `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts:62` `mapFetchTransportError` + `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/ProjectPicker.tsx:32` PlosApiError-branch rendering. Replaces the original `P2-1..P2-5` "sign in WHILE offline" sub-table from session #8 because that approach doesn't cleanly exercise the P-2 code path — Supabase auth itself needs network so the test fails before reaching the project-list fetch. **Extension offline-error handling — `authedFetch` doesn't catch fetch's TypeError (NEW 2026-05-08-b; STATUS UPDATED 2026-05-09 attempt #5 — gap STILL APPLIES per code-read but UX is not catastrophically broken because a higher-level component catch in the popup surfaces the raw error message readably; SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b — `authedFetch` now wraps `fetch()` in try/catch via new exported `mapFetchTransportError(err)` helper that converts native `TypeError("Failed to fetch")` → `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable — check your connection.')`; AbortError DOMException + other shapes re-thrown unchanged; new `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.test.ts` with 6 tests covering all conversion cases — TypeError variants, AbortError, generic Error, PlosApiError idempotent, primitives/null).** During Cluster E S2-3 verification 2026-05-08-b, director set DevTools Network → Offline and tried switching projects in the popup; no errors surfaced because switching projects operates entirely on cached `chrome.storage.local` data and triggers no API call. The intended path (`listProjects` failing on first popup load) wasn't tested. Code-side polish gap identified by reading `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts:48-62`: `authedFetch()` doesn't catch `fetch()`'s native `TypeError: Failed to fetch` that occurs in offline scenarios. The TypeError propagates uncaught through `listProjects()`. Original concern was that this would surface in popup as a stack trace or blank state. **Re-verify outcome 2026-05-09 attempt #5 (S2-3 with stronger system-WiFi-off trigger):** popup actually showed a friendly red error box reading the underlying network error message — meaning a higher-level catch in the popup (likely in `ProjectPicker.tsx` or App.tsx) IS catching the TypeError and rendering it readably. **However, code-read 2026-05-09 confirms `authedFetch` itself (api-client.ts:42-56) STILL has no try/catch around its `fetch()` call.** So P-2 remains a real polish improvement: (a) cleaner user-facing message ('Network unreachable — check your connection' vs raw 'Failed to fetch'); (b) retry capability in the wrapper layer rather than every component handling network failures separately; (c) consistent error type (`PlosApiError(0, ...)`) for downstream code paths. ~5-10 LOC fix in `authedFetch` to wrap `fetch()` in try/catch + convert TypeError → `PlosApiError(0, 'Network unreachable — check your connection')`. **Cross-references:** `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #2 row (where S2-3 was deferred) + Waypoint #1 attempt #3 row (further deferred) + Waypoint #1 attempt #5 row (S2-3 re-verified — friendly error appeared, P-2 stays open as polish improvement); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts:42-56` (the `authedFetch` code site).

**(P-3) Per-user-per-project extension state moves to PLOS DB (NEW 2026-05-08-c — director directive; HIGHLIGHT TERMS PORTION ✅ SHIPPED + DEPLOYED + BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-10-b on vklf.com (commit `16d4351` code on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` 2026-05-10; fast-forward merged + pushed to `main` 2026-05-10-b; cross-device sign-in test confirmed terms appear on a different Chrome profile = canonical proof of server-side persistence); remaining scope of selectedProjectId + selectedPlatform server-side stays OPEN for a future session).** Highlight Terms portion shipped per director Option-A 3-session-split 2026-05-08-d session #7. Schema: new `UserProjectHighlightTerm` model with `@@unique([userId, projectId, term])` + `@@index([userId, projectId])`; live in prod via `npx prisma db push` (additive, 1.05s). API: GET + PUT (replace-whole-list inside one $transaction) at `/api/projects/[projectId]/extension-state/highlight-terms`. Extension: new `highlight-terms-sync.ts` orchestrator with DI seam (server-first reads with cache fallback + one-time auto-migration of pre-existing local terms on first authenticated load + offline read-fallback + write-failure inline error with optimistic-update rollback); content-script unchanged — chrome.storage.local stays as a per-installation MIRROR cache so the shipped P-5 module's `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener keeps firing. 13 new sync tests via dependency-injected fakes (205/205 ext pass; was 192/192). **Browser-verify path when deployed:** install/reload extension → sign in → pick Project that previously had local Highlight Terms → confirm terms appear (one-time migration silently pushed them) → add/remove a term → toggle DevTools Network → Offline; reload popup → confirm sync warning + cached terms still show → sign in from a different Chrome profile / different laptop → confirm same terms appear. **Cross-references:** `prisma/schema.prisma` UserProjectHighlightTerm model; `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/extension-state/highlight-terms/route.ts`; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/highlight-terms-sync.ts` + `.test.ts`; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts` (listHighlightTerms + replaceHighlightTerms); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/App.tsx` (SetupScreen wiring); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/popup-state.ts` (mirror-cache role); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-10 entry. **REMAINING P-3 broader scope ✅ SHIPPED at code level 2026-05-10-e (commit `49d396e` on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` in W#2 polish session #10):** Schema-shape Read-It-Back per Rule 18: director picked single `UserExtensionState` record-type with two scalar columns (`selectedProjectId`, `selectedPlatform`) per user; preserves today's chrome.storage.local behavior 1:1 (switching project still clears platform). New `/api/extension-state` route (verifyAuth-scoped, NOT verifyProjectAuth — user-scoped state; server enforces refined "switching project clears platform" invariant on PUT, with the migration case preserved by treating prior-null as "no switch"; stale-pointer cleanup on GET; project-ownership double-check on PUT for non-null selectedProjectId). New `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/extension-state-sync.ts` orchestrator mirroring highlight-terms-sync.ts pattern (DI seam; loadExtensionState with server-first + cache-fallback + one-time auto-migrate; saveExtensionState with server-first then mirror). Popup App.tsx wired with optimistic update + rollback pattern + new extStateSyncWarning + extStateSaveError UI surfaces. Schema landed in prod via `npx prisma db push` 1.16s (additive; flag flipped Yes during build, back to No after push). 233/233 ext tests pass (was 220, +13 sync tests); ext build 641.42 kB; root build 51 routes (was 50). **Browser verification deferred to next W#2 → main deploy session per ROADMAP Active Tools W#2 row (a.9) RECOMMENDED-NEXT.** Cross-device sign-in test = canonical proof of correctness (same shape as P-3 narrowed Highlight Terms 2026-05-10-b). **Cross-references:** `prisma/schema.prisma` UserExtensionState model; `src/app/api/extension-state/route.ts` (GET + PUT + OPTIONS); `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.ts` (ExtensionStateDto + Get/Replace request/response); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/api-client.ts` (getExtensionState + replaceExtensionState); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/extension-state-sync.ts` + `.test.ts`; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/popup-state.ts` (mirror-cache role + new getExtensionStateCache + setExtensionStateCache); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/App.tsx` (sync helpers wired); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-10-e entry; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` cross-device sign-in verification entry. **(Original capture preserved below for historical record.)** **(P-3 broader scope ORIGINAL capture):** Director's standing principle surfaced 2026-05-08-c during Waypoint #1 attempt #3 reload (when `Remove + Load unpacked` wiped `chrome.storage.local` including the per-Project Highlight Terms the director had configured): *"When using the competition scraping extension, the highlight words should be highlighted on the page that the user is on. ... no matter where the user logs in, they can pick up where they left off. The same goes for other similar data so that no matter where the user logs in, they can pick up where they left off."* Scope: per-user-per-project extension state currently in `chrome.storage.local` (Highlight Terms per Project, last-used Project picker selection, last-used Platform per Project, future similar data items) should move to PLOS DB so signing in from any device / any Chrome profile preserves state. **Alternatives ruled out:** `chrome.storage.sync` (Chrome-account-scoped only — fails the cross-browser-instance test); status quo `chrome.storage.local` (per-installation; fails any reinstall). **Implementation sketch:** schema additions (likely `UserProjectExtensionState` table or fields on existing tables) + new API endpoints + extension state-reads switch from `chrome.storage.local` to PLOS API via the background messaging proxy that landed 2026-05-08-c. Estimated multi-session work — schema migration + API + extension refactor. Not actionable as a single polish bundle item; likely deserves its own dedicated session(s). **Cross-references:** `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-08-c append-only entry per Rule 18 mid-build directive Read-It-Back; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #3 row (where the directive surfaced).

**(P-4) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-08-d (code-level) + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-09 attempt #5 Amazon sponsored-ads SSPA-redirect detection (NEW 2026-05-08-c).** Director observed during Waypoint #1 attempt #3 Step S3-7 walkthrough: *"The + icon shows for organic search results but not the sponsored ads (which should also show the + icon)."* Root cause: Amazon's sponsored ads route through `/sspa/click?ie=UTF8&spc=...&url=%2Fdp%2F{ASIN}...` patterns where the actual `/dp/{ASIN}` lives URL-encoded inside the `url=` query parameter; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/platform-modules/amazon.ts:matchesProduct` ASIN_RE only matches literal path-level `/dp/` or `/gp/product/`, so sponsored-ad links get NO `+ Add` button. **Fix shipped 2026-05-08-d:** new `decodeSspaInner(href)` reads `pathname === '/sspa/click'` cases, pulls `searchParams.get('url')` (auto-decoded once), prepends `${protocol}//${host}`, and feeds the result back through ASIN_RE. Both `matchesProduct` and `canonicalProductUrl` branch on the SSPA path. 9 new tests in `amazon.test.ts` using two real captured sponsored-ad URLs (sp_atf placement → ASIN B0DWJTLNYT; sp_mtf placement → ASIN B0716F3NFG) covering happy path + edge cases (missing/empty `url=` param + non-SSPA URLs that happen to have a `url` query param + `/gp/product/` inside SSPA). **Synergy with P-6:** when P-6 (Sponsored Ad checkbox) ships, the URL-add overlay opening for an SSPA-detected sponsored ad would have the checkbox pre-checked. Both paths converge on the same boolean column. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/platform-modules/amazon.ts` (decodeSspaInner site); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/platform-modules/amazon.test.ts` (SSPA test block); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #3 row (origin) + attempt #5 row (browser re-verify when added).

**(P-5) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-08-d (code-level) + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-09 attempt #5 Live-page Highlight Terms application (NEW 2026-05-08-c — director directive).** Director surfaced expectation during Waypoint #1 attempt #3 Step S3-7 walkthrough: *"Secondly, the highlight words are not being highlighted on the amazon results page."* Then clarified scope: *"When using the competition scraping extension, the highlight words should be highlighted on the page that the user is on."* Pre-fix state: zero live-page highlight code in `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/`; popup UI persisted Highlight Terms per-Project to `chrome.storage.local` but no content-script reading. **Fix shipped 2026-05-08-d (Rule 18 design Read-It-Back done before coding; director approved design before coding started):** new file `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts` (~240 LOC) + `.test.ts` (~120 LOC unit tests on regex + colormap pure logic). Reads `highlightTerms:<projectId>` from `chrome.storage.local` via existing `getHighlightTerms()`. Match algorithm: case-insensitive + word-boundary anchored (\b — `cat` does NOT match `category`) + multi-word phrase support with literal spaces broadened to \s+ (matches non-breaking-space + line-break variations) + longest-first ordering on the alternation so multi-word terms beat their substrings. DOM walking: TreeWalker over text nodes only, skipping subtrees rooted at script/style/noscript/textarea/input/iframe/svg/math/code/pre + contenteditable elements + our own `.plos-cs-*` UI (so highlights never appear inside the URL-add form or floating + button). Wraps matches with `<mark class="plos-cs-highlight">` styled inline per-term using existing `getContrastTextColor()` for foreground auto-flip (light backgrounds → black text; dark → white). Live updates: `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener fires when popup edits terms (popup-side ↔ live-page sync without page refresh); MutationObserver hookup via existing orchestrator observer (shares one observer; debounced 250ms; runs on SPA navigation + infinite-scroll new tiles); SPA navigation refresh via existing `popstate` hook. Performance safeguards: 50-term soft cap with `console.warn` + truncate; 500KB body-text cap with skip + warn (Amazon search results typically ~50KB); initial pass via `requestIdleCallback` with `setTimeout(...,0)` fallback. Cleanup wired into orchestrator teardown. Wired into `orchestrator.ts` after `ensureStylesInjected()`; failures don't break the rest of orchestrator (try/catch around init). **NOT done in P-5 (could revisit as future polish):** highlighting inside contenteditable / textarea (typing would corrupt); de-duplicating overlapping multi-term matches (today: adjacent highlights — could compute non-overlapping intervals); per-host opt-out. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts` + `.test.ts` (code site); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (integration site); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/styles.ts` (`.plos-cs-highlight` CSS); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-08-c (origin directive) + 2026-05-08-d (fix shipped + design choices); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #3 row (origin) + attempt #5 row (browser re-verify when added).

**(P-6) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-09-b (commit `bc6816c`) + ✅ DEPLOYED to vklf.com 2026-05-09-b + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED LIVE 2026-05-09-b — "Sponsored Ad" checkbox in URL-add form + PLOS-side tag (NEW 2026-05-08-c — director feature request).** Implementation per scope: (a) schema added; (b) shared types extended; (c) API routes wired (GET/POST/PATCH on /urls + /urls/[urlId]); (d) extension `url-add-form.ts` got checkbox + new `defaultIsSponsoredAd?: boolean` prop wiring P-4 synergy via new optional `PlatformModule.detectsAsSponsored()` (Amazon impl + 7 new tests; orchestrator passes the signal at form-open time); (e) PLOS-side viewer got new "Sponsored" column at position 2 (sortable + funnel + amber pill badge), new `BooleanFilter` tri-state primitive (All / Sponsored only / Non-sponsored only with URL-query `?sponsored=true|false`), new `EditableBooleanField` primitive on URL detail page (one-click toggle with optimistic update + rollback). **Browser-verified live on vklf.com:** Walmart end-to-end ALL ✅ (column / detail toggle / badge / filter All-Sponsored-NonSponsored / sort); Amazon extension SSPA auto-pre-check ✅; Amazon save with checkbox checked → badge appears ✅; Amazon organic save → em-dash ✅. **Post-deploy regression caught + fixed same session (commit `8115138`):** column-filter popover (Sponsored + the other 6) got clipped on short tables by the wrapper's `overflow-x: auto`; switched FilterPopover from `position: absolute` to `position: fixed` with viewport-anchored top/left from trigger rect; benefits all 7 column filters. **Original capture context (preserved):** Director surfaced during Waypoint #1 attempt #3 Step S3-10 walkthrough: *"Along with the 3 different fields that the user can fill out when saving a url, there should be a button to check that says 'Sponsored Ad'. This should be shown as a small tag in the PLOS side in Competition Scraping & Deep Analysis UI as well."* Scope: (a) Schema add `isSponsoredAd Boolean @default(false)` to `CompetitorUrl` table — additive change; schema-change-in-flight flag flips Yes during build then back to No. (b) Shared types `CreateCompetitorUrlRequest`, `CompetitorUrl`, `UpdateCompetitorUrlRequest` get `isSponsoredAd?: boolean`. (c) API routes POST/GET/PATCH on /urls accept/return the field; toWireShape updated. (d) Extension `url-add-form.ts` adds checkbox below the 3 free-text fields; `api-client createCompetitorUrl` payload includes flag. (e) PLOS-side viewer URL list renders small badge for sponsored=true rows; URL detail page (slice (a.3)) gets inline-editable toggle; column filters (slice (a.4)) add Sponsored boolean filter. **Synergy with P-4:** now that P-4 (SSPA detection) ships 2026-05-08-d, the form's checkbox would be auto-pre-checked for SSPA-detected URLs, with manual override always available. Implementing P-6 only needs to add auto-pre-check behavior, not invent a new column. Estimated ~60-90 min mid-session work + schema migration. **Cross-references:** `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-08-c append-only entry per Rule 18 mid-build directive Read-It-Back; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #3 row; `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/url-add-form.ts` (the form site); `prisma/schema.prisma` CompetitorUrl model.

**(P-7) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-08-d (code-level) + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-09 attempt #5 URL-add overlay positioning — don't occlude the product listing being saved (NEW 2026-05-08-d — director directive).** Director directive surfaced during Waypoint #1 attempt #4 Step S3-12 walkthrough: *"When the overlay opens to allow the user to add a url, it shouldn't open directly on top of the very product listing for which the url is being added because many times the user may need to look at the product listing to type in the product name, type or brand."* Pre-fix: URL-add overlay opened centered in the viewport via the backdrop's `display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center` layout, fully occluding whatever was in the viewport center — typically the product card the user just clicked the "+" on. **Fix shipped 2026-05-08-d:** added optional `triggerRect?: DOMRect | null` to `UrlAddFormProps`; threaded through `FloatingAddButton.onClick(href, triggerRect)` callback contract update + `currentLinkRect` storage at button-show time (via `link.getBoundingClientRect()`); new `computeFormPosition()` in `url-add-form.ts` places the form on the side of the viewport opposite the trigger's horizontal center (left half → form right; right half → form left), anchored near top of viewport with 16px margin, clamped for narrow viewports. Falls back to centered layout when `triggerRect === null` (right-click context-menu fallback path — no trigger element to position relative to). **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/url-add-form.ts` (computeFormPosition + form positioning); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/floating-add-button.ts` (currentLinkRect storage + onClick contract); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (handleAddRequest threading); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-08-d append-only entry per Rule 18 mid-build directive Read-It-Back; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #4 row (where directive surfaced — added end of attempt #5 session).

**(P-8) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-08-d (code-level) + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-09 attempt #5 ✓ already-saved icon punches through URL-add overlay — z-index conflict (NEW 2026-05-08-d).** Director observed during Waypoint #1 attempt #4 Step S3-16 walkthrough: *"if a listing that is next to it had its url added previously, its checkmark shows up on top of the new overlay (which it shouldn't)."* Root cause: yesterday's `f4226ca` ✓-icon visibility-boost set `.plos-cs-saved-icon` z-index to `2147483647` (max int32) so the icon would beat Amazon's chrome — but this also equals the `.plos-cs-form` z-index, AND `.plos-cs-saved-icon` has `position: relative` which creates its own stacking context. Two stacking contexts at max-int z-index → DOM-order / sub-context-rendering wins, in this case the saved-icon punches through the open URL-add overlay. **Fix shipped 2026-05-08-d:** layered z-index tiers in `styles.ts` — page-overlay tier `999990` (saved-icon + add-button + dismiss × + overlay-banner) sits above host page chrome; modal-backdrop tier `999998` covers the page-overlay tier when modal opens; modal-content tier `999999` is above its own backdrop. Amazon's chrome max-z-index is ~5000-10000, so 999990 has ~100× headroom. Single-file CSS change, ~5 lines effective. **Browser re-verified 2026-05-09:** with overlay open on a product card next to a saved-✓ product, the dimmed backdrop covers the entire page including the ✓ icon — no punch-through. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/styles.ts` (z-index tier definitions in CSS + leading comment block documenting the tier system); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B` 2026-05-08-d append-only entry per Rule 18; `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #4 + #5 rows.

**(P-9) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #8) + ✅ DEPLOYED 2026-05-10-c to `main` + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-10-c on vklf.com — ALL 9 STEPS PASSED across 4 platforms (Ebay search/detail + Walmart search + chrome://extensions Errors panel zero-new-cap-warning entries + soft 50-term-count warning still fires + Amazon/Etsy spot-checks no regression + Walmart live-edit updates within ~1-2s). Highlight-terms 500KB body-text cap too aggressive — blocks Highlight Terms on Ebay + Walmart pages; fires repeatedly on Walmart heavy-SPA pages (NEW 2026-05-09 attempt #5; data point added 2026-05-09-b: Walmart search confirmed at 656,627 bytes via chrome://extensions Errors panel: `[PLOS] page text length (656627) exceeds highlight cap (500000); skipping highlight pass.` on `https://www.walmart.com/search?q=bursitis` — director re-confirmed P-9 status during P-6 session #6 wrap-up; SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b — chunked/incremental highlight pass via `requestIdleCallback` + cap REMOVED entirely. New exported `processInChunks<T>(items, processItem, options)` generic helper (default chunk size 500 nodes ≈ 10-15ms/chunk; pluggable `yieldFn` for testing; honors `CancellationSignal` between chunks). `applyHighlightsTo` is now async + chunked. `startLiveHighlighting.refresh()` rewritten for last-wins cancellation — each new refresh cancels in-flight pass before starting; latest DOM/term state always becomes eventual visible state. Removing the cap auto-resolved the backlog's recommended (e) dedupe warning + (f) skip-if-unchanged measurement — no cap → no warning → no `document.body.textContent.length` re-measure overhead. 9 new tests added to `highlight-terms.test.ts` for `processInChunks`).** Surfaced during Waypoint #1 attempt #5 Ebay (S3-26) + Walmart (S3-28) walkthroughs. P-5 (live-page Highlight Terms shipped 2026-05-08-d) included a 500KB body-text safeguard that skips the highlight pass on pages exceeding the cap, intended to avoid main-thread freeze on very large pages. Three related symptoms with the same root cause: (1) Highlight Terms don't appear on **Ebay search results** because page text exceeds cap (~1.5MB body text); (2) Highlight Terms don't appear on **Ebay listing detail pages** either (~1.58MB body text — confirmed via Console diagnostic 2026-05-09); (3) Highlight Terms don't appear on **Walmart search results** (~636-675KB) AND the cap fires **~20+ times per Walmart page-load** because Walmart's heavy React re-renders + dynamic ad loading trigger the orchestrator's MutationObserver re-scan repeatedly even with the 250ms debounce — each re-scan re-runs the highlight pass which re-runs the cap check + re-measures `document.body.innerText` (an expensive operation). chrome://extensions Errors panel accumulates one entry per cap-fire on Ebay; ~20+ per Walmart page-load. **Amazon search results stay below cap (~50KB) so the issue doesn't surface there.** Etsy pages stay below cap so highlights work cleanly on Etsy. **Fix options:** (a) bump the cap to ~5MB (covers Ebay's typical sizes; risky for very-large pages); (b) make cap user-configurable per-platform (good default + escape hatch); (c) **switch to chunked/incremental highlight pass via requestIdleCallback** that handles arbitrary-size pages without main-thread freeze (best UX; biggest engineering — likely best long-term answer); (d) lower the log severity from `console.warn` → `console.debug` (Chrome's Errors panel only catches warn+ severity); (e) only emit the warning ONCE per page-load (deduplicate via a per-page-load Set or flag); (f) tighten the MutationObserver debounce on heavy-SPA platforms OR skip the cap re-check if page text length hasn't changed significantly since last check (avoid the perf cost of re-measuring body text on every re-scan). **Recommended combination:** (c) for the underlying perf concern + (e) + (f) for the noise + perf. Estimated ~90-120 min for (c) standalone or ~30-50 min for (e)+(f) interim fix. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/highlight-terms.ts` (the 500KB cap site + console.warn site); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (MutationObserver debounce site); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #5 row (origin).

**(P-10) ✅ SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (W#2 polish session #8) + ✅ DEPLOYED 2026-05-10-c to `main` + ✅ BROWSER-VERIFIED 2026-05-10-c on vklf.com — ALL 10 STEPS PASSED (direct paste of saved Walmart URL → banner within ~1s + auto-dismiss; SPA navigation to saved Walmart product reliably 5/5 times across different products; unsaved-product correctly suppresses banner; unsaved → saved navigation re-fires banner; refresh re-fires; manual × dismiss correctly suppresses re-fire; Amazon + Ebay + Etsy spot-checks no regression). AlreadySavedOverlay banner intermittent on saved-URL navigation, observed on Walmart heavy-SPA pages (NEW 2026-05-09 attempt #5; SHIPPED 2026-05-10-b — three orchestrator changes: (1) `maybeShowDetailOverlay()` dedupes by `location.href` via new `lastOverlayUrl` closure variable (set BEFORE recognition match so navigate-away-and-back correctly re-fires); (2) new `scheduleDetailOverlayCheck()` 150ms-debounced cancellable timer ensures only the final URL gets a banner check after pushState bursts; (3) MutationObserver-based SPA URL change detection via new `lastObservedUrl` closure variable — checks `location.href !== lastObservedUrl` inside the existing 250ms-debounced rescan callback and triggers the overlay-check debouncer if changed. **Critical technical wrinkle that drove the design:** Chrome content scripts run in an **isolated world** with their own `window.history`, so patching `pushState` in content-script context does NOT intercept host-page React Router calls (Walmart, etc.). Backlog's suggested fix #2 (intercept `history.pushState`/`replaceState`) doesn't work cross-context; the MutationObserver is the cross-context-safe signal since SPA navigation always causes immediate DOM mutation. Initial-load detail-overlay check at orchestrator startup also routes through the debouncer — gives Walmart's React time to settle URL after content-script load. Cleanup function clears any pending overlay-check + rescan timers on teardown. Per existing extension test convention (DOM-touching code verified end-to-end in browser, only pure logic unit-tested), no new unit tests added for these orchestrator changes — relies on next waypoint walkthrough for browser verification across Amazon + Ebay + Etsy + Walmart).** Director observation during Waypoint #1 attempt #5 S3-28 Walmart walkthrough: *"the green overlay that is supposed to tell me that the url is already added doesn't show sometimes."* The AlreadySavedOverlay banner (✓ This URL is already in your project · <Project Name>) appears RELIABLY on Amazon + Ebay + Etsy detail pages but INTERMITTENTLY on Walmart detail pages. Likely root causes worth investigating in the fix: (a) **storage-hydration race** — content script's saved-URL-check fires BEFORE the saved-URL list has loaded from `chrome.storage.local` for that platform; check sees empty list → no match → no banner; (b) **Walmart SPA navigation** — Walmart's React routing uses `history.pushState()` directly to navigate between pages; the orchestrator listens for the `popstate` event, which fires on back/forward but NOT on programmatic `pushState`; SPA navigations between Walmart pages don't trigger the URL-change check; (c) **initial-page-load timing** — the canonical URL extraction may not be stable at the moment the banner-check fires (e.g., URL changes from `/search?q=...` to `/ip/{id}` as page loads). **Fix sketch:** (1) wrap the saved-URL-check in a small wait-for-storage-hydration delay or use `chrome.storage.onChanged` to fire the check whenever storage updates; (2) intercept `history.pushState` + `history.replaceState` (monkey-patch on `window` or use a polyfill like 'history-events') in addition to `popstate` so SPA navigations are caught; (3) re-fire the banner-check on `document.readyState` transitions ('interactive' → 'complete'). Estimated ~30-50 LOC + careful regression testing across Amazon + Ebay + Etsy + Walmart to confirm no regressions on platforms that already work. **Cross-references:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/already-saved-overlay.ts` (the overlay component); `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/orchestrator.ts` (popstate listener + URL-change detection); `COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md` Waypoint #1 attempt #5 row (origin).

## 🔍 NEW MEDIUM (2026-05-04-c) — `[FLAKE]` telemetry visibility puzzle

**Status:** DISCOVERED 2026-05-04-c during D3 retry batch 1's pool-exhaustion event; investigation deferred to a small standalone session.

**The puzzle:** the flake-counter telemetry shipped 2026-05-04 (`src/lib/flake-counter.ts` + 38 `recordFlake` call sites across 19 DB-backed routes) should have logged a `[FLAKE]` line for every catch-block error. During the 2026-05-04-c D3 batch 1 pool-exhaustion event, ~28 errors fired within ~0.1 sec. Director's Vercel runtime logs paste showed all ~28 with full stack traces but **NO line beginning with `[FLAKE]`** visible. PATCH /keywords/[keywordId] WAS in the wired list per the 2026-05-04 STATE block (+2 recordFlake calls per the manifest).

**Three possibilities:**

1. **Vercel UI display issue.** The runtime logs view may show only the first console.error per request (the existing `console.error('<X> error:', error)`); the second console.error from `recordFlake` (called BEFORE the existing one) may be hidden in a collapsed per-request view. Director's paste might have been from a high-level summary view that shows one line per request. Test next session: click into a single failed request to expand its full log; look for `[FLAKE]` line.

2. **Flake-counter not firing on these endpoints.** Would be a real wiring bug. The 2026-05-04 work was supposed to be exact 1-to-1 across all 38 catch blocks. Re-verify the deploy actually shipped the wiring (deployed bundle inspection on Vercel; OR `git diff` between 2026-05-04 commit and current main).

3. **Format issue.** `recordFlake` calls `console.error` which Vercel routes to runtime logs; possible but unlikely given the helper's unit tests cover this.

**Investigation plan (next session, standalone):**
- (i) Read Vercel logs at the next live error event using expanded per-request view; look for `[FLAKE]` lines.
- (ii) If absent in expanded view: re-confirm `recordFlake` calls present in deployed bundle (check Vercel deploy artifacts OR confirm via `git log --oneline src/lib/flake-counter.ts src/app/api/`).
- (iii) If present in bundle but not in logs: write a small targeted test that triggers a PATCH error (e.g., posting an invalid keyword update) and confirm the `[FLAKE]` line appears in dev-server output.

**Why MEDIUM, not HIGH:** the underlying functionality (errors propagate to user; existing console.error captures full stack trace) is unaffected. The visibility gap only affects our ability to grade the rate-fix's effectiveness empirically — important but not user-facing.

**Cross-references:** `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-2026-05-04-C STATE block "Open question" section; `PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md §10` "📊 Platform-wide flake-rate telemetry" entry (the original 2026-05-04 work).

## 🔔 SCALE SESSION 0 — OUTCOME C FIRED 2026-04-28 (build path activated)

The empirical validation pre-step of `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md` (Scale Session 0) ran 2026-04-28. Director ran a full-Bursitis V3 Auto-Analyze on Sonnet 4.6 (151 of 281 batches completed before stopping) plus a separate Opus 4.7 cost test at run start.

**Empirical findings:**

1. **Sonnet 4.6 hit the 200k context wall at batch 151** — input grew from 19,929 tokens (empty canvas) → 220,091 tokens (canvas of ~700 topics). The wall was reached well before the run could complete; an additional ~130 batches would have been needed.
2. **Opus 4.7 was economically prohibitive** — director's cost test at run start showed per-batch costs approached or exceeded $1, vs. ~$0.30–$0.85 on Sonnet 4.6. Director switched back to Sonnet 4.6.
3. **Per-batch cost on Sonnet 4.6 grew monotonically** with canvas size (~$0.20 → ~$0.85), driven entirely by input-token growth (the prompt body itself stays cached at ~18k tokens). Total run cost: ~$70–80 for 54% completion.

**Trigger conditions met (per `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md §0`):**
- Outcome C: V3 + Opus 4.7 still hits wall or has unacceptable cost — **YES, fired**.
- Threshold (b): a production project's canvas exceeded ~600 topics under standard 200k window — **YES, the Bursitis run reached ~700 topics**.

**Effect on roadmap:**
- **Scale Session B is now the next-priority forward action** for Workflow #1 build work (per `INPUT_CONTEXT_SCALING_DESIGN.md §6` locked plan): schema migration adding `intentFingerprint` column + applier extension + AI-generated backfill script.
- Scale Sessions C, D, E follow B in sequence per the locked plan.

**Three additional findings from the same run that are NOT scaling-related (separate sections below):**
- 🚨 **HIGH-severity canvas-blanking bug** (batches 70 + 134) — wiring layer intermittently sent ~20k tokens of input (no canvas state) instead of full canvas; model rebuilt from scratch; reconciliation flipped 84 keywords/event to Reshuffled status; 168 keywords across the two events stuck in Reshuffled forever because batch queue is fixed at run-start. NOT polish — see "Canvas-blanking bug" section below.
- 4 new Phase-1 polish items (Mid-run batch queue refresh + Skeleton View on canvas + AST split-view row alignment + Topics table row numbers) — see "NEW Phase-1 polish item" entries below.
- 2 new architectural design items (action-by-action feedback + second-pass refinement workflow; intelligent hybrid cost/quality strategy) — see new sections below.

- **Phase 1g-rebuild:** Atomic canvas rebuild
- **Phase 2 (rebrand):** PLOS Rebrand + 3-tier navigation + Think Tank + Admin Notes (PMS + Think Tank)
- **Phase D (documentation overhaul):** 11-document handoff system with Document Lifecycle Management (DLMS)
- **Phase M Checkpoints 1–4 (2026-04-16):** Database schema refactor (Project + ProjectWorkflow + workflow-data tables on projectWorkflowId)

- **Phase M Checkpoint 7 (2026-04-17, chat `7e0b8456-...`):** Refactored Keyword Clustering from `/keyword-clustering` (dual-state page with built-in Projects List) to `/projects/[projectId]/keyword-clustering` (single-state, Project pre-picked from URL). Moved `components/` folder alongside the new page wrapper. Wrote new 164-line `page.tsx` (down from 225) that reads projectId from URL, fetches Project name, shows friendly error on 404/403, and renders the workspace. Top-bar Back button returns to `/projects/[projectId]` detail page. Deleted old `/keyword-clustering` folder. - **Phase M Checkpoint 8 (2026-04-17, chat `fc8025bf-...`):** Admin Notes added to Dashboard and PLOS pages; broken Keyword Analysis card on `/plos` rewired to `/projects`. Physical changes: (1) `src/components/AdminNotes.tsx` — `SystemKey` type extended from `"think-tank" | "pms"` to `"think-tank" | "pms" | "dashboard" | "plos"` (line 34; only change to the component). (2) New file `src/app/dashboard/notes/page.tsx` (11 lines; mirror of PMS notes page — `system="dashboard"`, `systemLabel="Dashboard"`, `systemIcon="🚀"`, `backRoute="/dashboard"`). (4) `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — 📝 Notes button added to top-right flex container, immediately left of Sign Out (+17 lines). (5) `src/app/plos/page.tsx` — Keyword Analysis card `route` changed from `"/keyword-clustering"` to `"/projects"`; 📝 Notes button added to top bar, grouped with Sign Out in a right-side flex wrapper (+5 net lines). `npm run build` passed cleanly in 18.5s; 17/17 static pages, zero TypeScript errors, both `/dashboard/notes` and `/plos/notes` appear as routes in the build output. ### Checkpoint completion summaries

## 🎯 Post-Phase-M — Claude Code Migration (TOP PRIORITY) — user executes, no Claude chat needed

After Phase M is fully deployed and verified (DONE as of 2026-04-17), the user performs a ~30-minute offline migration:

1. **Install Claude Code** in Codespaces (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` or current equivalent — check https://docs.claude.com for current install command)
2. **Authenticate** (uses existing Anthropic account)
3. **Smoke test** — run `claude` in Codespaces terminal, ask a trivial question, verify file reading works
4. **Read `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_MIGRATION.md`** end-to-end — this is the user's orientation to the new methodology
5. **Ready for first Claude Code session** (Phase 1g-test kickoff)

This migration is logged as a roadmap item (this section) and captured in `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_MIGRATION.md`. No code changes. No Claude chat required during the migration itself.

**The final message of chat `75cc8985-...` (the chat that deployed Ckpt 9 + Ckpt 9.5) includes a "🚨 Ready to switch to Claude Code" section with exact steps.**

### First Claude Code session — Phase 1g-test kickoff
**After migration:** First real Claude Code session tackles Phase 1g-test — live-testing Auto-Analyze on Keyword Clustering. Starter prompt: `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER.md`. See `docs/KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` §6 for Phase 1g-test scope.

Walks the entire AST table; flips on-canvas-Unsorted/Reshuffled to AI-Sorted (heals Bug 1 silent placements + reabsorbs reshuffled keywords just re-placed); flips off-canvas-AI-Sorted to NEW `'Reshuffled'` status (Bug 2 reshuffle ghosts surfaced as visible alarm per director's Option B framing during drift-check). Trigger: `runLoop` validation flow detects HC3-only failure (every error starts "Missing " — no HC4/HC5 lost-data triggers) and fires salvage instead of full retry per director's Option A framing. Builds Change-6-template follow-up prompt at runtime with Q5-resolution wording (auto-archive language); calls `callApi`; salvage cost added to `batch.cost`; parses three blocks (DELTA ROWS, IRRELEVANT_KEYWORDS, REEVALUATION REPORT). Delta rows merged into `originalResult.topicsTableTsv` via refactored `mergeDelta(deltaTsv, baseTsv?)` (the optional second arg is new). IRRELEVANT_KEYWORDS POSTed PER-keyword to `/api/projects/[projectId]/removed-keywords` with `removedSource='auto-ai-detected-irrelevant'` + model's reason as `aiReasoning`. Archived ids removed from `batch.keywordIds`/`batch.keywords` + `onRefreshKeywords()` synced; re-validation; doApply if pass; falls through to retry on salvage error or unresolved missing. **Item #4 — P3-F8 four-function canvas-layout port** as new `src/lib/canvas-layout.ts` (321 lines) with all four functions as pure exports that mutate passed nodes in place. `calcNodeHeight(node)` uses browser canvas measureText with word-aware wrap matching HTML's `cvsWrap`; respects `userMinH`; SSR-safe fallback. `runLayoutPass(nodes, pathways, collapsed?)` is the 4-step holistic pass (reset roots → tree-walk type-aware placement → 60-pass overlap resolution → pathway separation). `autoLayoutChild(child, parent, relType, allNodes, collapsed?)` is type-aware (linear = align parent-left below all peer subtrees; nested = align parent-center+indent below nested siblings only); moves entire child subtree by delta. `separatePathways(nodes, pathways, collapsed?)` is horizontal pathway push-apart. Constants mirror HTML except NODE_W=220 + NESTED_INDENT=30 (kept React's existing values). Wired into `AutoAnalyze.doApply` step 7.5 — fires on every batch's apply per Q1 lockdown — and into `CanvasPanel.handleLinkClick` for type-aware auto-position on parent-child link form (coalesced single-server-PATCH for parent-link change + position changes). Build clean. Single commit `6c09e50` — **NOT YET PUSHED, awaiting director approval per Rule 9 deploy gate.** **Two NEW Phase-1 polish ROADMAP items captured during drift-check** (see below): "P3-F7 root-cause audit" (HC5 text-matching audit + canvas rebuild text-match audit + new HC6 "no keyword unlinks" check + Bursitis 49-ghost spot-audit) + "Keyword accounting + ghost detection panel" (history-of-every-keyword-ever-added compared against AST ∪ RemovedTerms; admin Ghost Keywords panel with Restore/Archive bulk actions). Both schedule with Session 4 or 5; not blocking.

### Phase-1 polish bundle (2026-04-26) — three items + one cosmetic fix DONE

Director picked option (a) post-Pivot-E and bundled three deferred Phase-1 items in one session. All shipped, all on vklf.com.

**Item 1 — Visual verification of canvas-layout engine.** Director used existing 40-topic populated canvas (cleanliness met the bar). Confirmed: no overlap, descriptions fit, type-aware placement works, pathway separation OK. One cosmetic bug found + fixed in same session: `+x more` keyword-count indicator was vertically clipped (bottom of letterforms cut off) on topic boxes that wrap content past the foreignObject's `KW_PREVIEW_H=36` boundary. **Fix:** removed the standalone `+x more` element entirely; folded the hidden-count info into the expand button label (button now reads `▼ N (+M)` when M keywords are hidden beyond the preview, where N = total). Two-attempt fix — first attempt (`950e4b5`) added CSS `white-space: nowrap` to address word-break-clipping which turned out NOT to be the actual cause; second attempt (`c891c36`) addressed the actual vertical-clipping cause. See `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-04-26 entry for the diagnostic-without-screenshot lesson.

**Item 2 — Direct-mode UI hint.** Conditional inline hint in `AutoAnalyze.tsx` under the API Mode dropdown. Visible only when `apiMode === 'server'` AND `est.nKeywords >= 100`. Wording: *"⚠ With N unsorted keywords (~M batches), batches may exceed Vercel's 5-min server timeout and fail mid-flight. Switch API Mode to Direct (browser → Anthropic) to avoid this."* Code comment notes the hint becomes obsolete after AWS migration (per Phase 2 server-side execution plan).

**Item 3 — Adaptive-Thinking 0-output warning.** Conditional inline warning in `AutoAnalyze.tsx` under the Thinking row (after the Stall input). Visible only when `thinkingMode === 'adaptive'` AND `nodes.length >= 50`. Wording: *"⚠ With N topics on the canvas, Adaptive Thinking can occasionally produce 0 output tokens (a fully wasted API call). If you see a batch fail with empty output, switch Thinking to Enabled with a Budget of 12000+."* Code comment notes V3 may have made this obsolete; revisit once empirical data from V3 runs confirms.

**Architectural-question raised mid-session — funnel-stage ordering of root-level topics.** Director observed during the visual verification that the AI does great semantic clustering (right keywords → right topics; right sub-topics under right parents) BUT root-level (depth-1) topics on canvas appear in the order the model emitted them, not in conversion-funnel arc order (awareness → consideration → decision → treatment). V3 prompt's Step 7 asks the model to think about funnel ordering, but the operation vocabulary deliberately excludes position operations (PIVOT_DESIGN.md §1.5), so the model has no mechanism to express that ordering. Captured as new Phase-1 polish item below (`Funnel-Order Pass`). Director picked Option A: defer build to a future session, capture design summary now, continue today's bundle.

**Director-flagged process slip:** Claude's first "+x more" fix attempt was a guess based on the symptom description ("cut off horizontally") without first asking for or seeing a screenshot. Term was ambiguous (Claude read it as "right side clipped"; director meant "cut along a horizontal line through the middle"). First attempt didn't address the actual cause; second attempt (after director uploaded a screenshot to `docs/cutoff.png` for diagnostic, since deleted) succeeded. Captured in `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-04-26 entry — pattern: when fixing a UI bug without ability to see the rendered output, ask for a screenshot or specific verbal disambiguation BEFORE coding.

**Empirical-threshold validation — new follow-up item below.** Director's call: the 100-keyword and 50-topic thresholds are placeholders; needs validation against real-batch-run data.

### NEW Phase-1 polish item — Empirical validation of UI-hint thresholds (raised 2026-04-26)

Both UI hints from the Phase-1 polish bundle (Direct-mode hint, Adaptive-Thinking warning) currently fire at placeholder thresholds (100 unsorted keywords / 50 canvas topics) chosen by Claude as round-number defaults without empirical justification. Director's request: validate each threshold against real-batch-run data and adjust if needed.

**Direct-mode hint threshold validation:** the hint warns server-mode users that batches may exceed Vercel's 5-min timeout. Need data on: at what unsorted-keyword-count does a server-mode V3 batch actually exceed 5 min wall-clock? Per Pivot D real-world data, V3 batches are 5-7 min — meaning even small batches in server mode are likely to fail. The threshold should probably be much lower than 100 (perhaps 1 — i.e., always warn in server mode). Validation: capture wall-clock per server-mode batch across the next 3-5 runs; pick the keyword count at which 5-min ceiling is reliably hit; adjust threshold.

**Adaptive-Thinking warning threshold validation:** the warning addresses a V2-era bug where Adaptive Thinking on a large prompt produced 0 output tokens. V3 outputs are smaller; bug may not trigger at all on V3. Validation: across the next 3-5 V3 runs with Adaptive Thinking enabled, log canvas size at run-start and watch for any 0-output failures. If no failures across all runs, remove the warning entirely (and the hint code). If failures appear, find the canvas-size threshold at which they reliably occur.

**Where to store the data:** a small append-only log captured in this doc under a "Hint-threshold validation runs" sub-section; rows are `{date, run-id, server-mode? , unsorted-kw-count, canvas-topic-count, batch-wall-clock-min, adaptive-thinking?, 0-output-failure?}`. After 3-5 entries, claim the threshold and update the hint code.

**Estimated effort:** zero net effort — the data collection happens during natural test runs; threshold update is a 1-line code change per hint when the data warrants it.

### NEW Phase-1 polish item — BATCH_REVIEW screen Apply button feedback (raised 2026-04-27)

When admin clicks the Apply button in the BATCH_REVIEW screen, the button currently stays visually unchanged during the apply operation. Admin can't tell if the click registered. There's no visual feedback during the apply step (which can take several seconds for canvas rebuild + reconciliation). If the apply fails (validation error, network issue, etc.), the button stays in its same state, masking the failure too.

**Director's design (2026-04-27):**
- During apply: button becomes **disabled** + **faded color** (not clickable; visually distinct from normal state)
- On apply **success**: button + entire BATCH_REVIEW overlay dismiss automatically (current behavior, but now visually clear that "click → fade → success → dismiss")
- On apply **error**: button returns to normal enabled state (overlay stays open so admin can retry or skip)

**Implementation note:** straightforward React state change in the BATCH_REVIEW component. ~30-60 min of code work. Captured as polish, not architectural.

### NEW Phase-1 polish item — BATCH_REVIEW screen show operations as scannable tables (raised 2026-04-27)

The BATCH_REVIEW screen currently shows new topics created (per Pivot D commit `d624556` fix) and the analyzed keywords list — but not the full operation set in a way admin can scan quickly. Without operation visibility, "Review each batch" cannot fully serve its design purpose: the admin can see WHAT was created but not the structural changes (existing-topic edits, splits/merges/moves, sister-link changes, JUSTIFY_RESTRUCTURE payloads) the model emitted alongside.

**Director's design (2026-04-27):** render the batch's operations as scannable tables, NOT prose. Distinct tables for:
- (a) **New topics created** — columns: title, description, parent, relationship
- (b) **Keyword placements** — columns: keyword text, target topic, primary/secondary
- (c) **Existing-topic modifications** — UPDATE_TOPIC_TITLE / UPDATE_TOPIC_DESCRIPTION / MOVE_TOPIC / MERGE_TOPICS / SPLIT_TOPIC / DELETE_TOPIC, each with reason
- (d) **Sister-link changes** — ADD_SISTER_LINK / REMOVE_SISTER_LINK
- (e) **JUSTIFY_RESTRUCTURE payloads** prominently flagged when present (high-stability-topic modifications)

**Goal:** admin can scan a batch's full effect in seconds, not parse paragraphs. Surfaced during 2026-04-27 V3 small-batch test when director observed the screen was insufficient for validation.

**Implementation note:** moderate effort. The `processBatchV3` function already returns parsed operations; the BATCH_REVIEW UI component needs to organize them by operation type and render each group as a table. ~2-4 hours of code work depending on table styling polish. Captured as polish, not architectural.

### NEW Phase-1 polish item — Search-volume display on canvas topic boxes + cross-tool display convention (raised 2026-04-27)

**Behavior on canvas topic boxes (W#1 only at this layer):**

(a) **Each topic box** shows TWO total-volume figures:
- Total search volume across all PRIMARY keywords on that topic, in the **primary keyword color**
- Total search volume across all SECONDARY keywords on that topic, in the **secondary keyword color**

(b) **For each keyword displayed:**
- In the topic box's keyword-preview area (the first few keywords shown directly on the topic): the volume appears in **parentheses to the right** of the keyword text
- In the expand-arrow overlay (the box that opens when admin clicks ▼ on the topic): same — volume in parentheses to the right of each keyword

(c) **Bold formatting based on Auto-Analyze panel's volume threshold:**
- Keyword volume **≥ threshold** → **bold**
- Keyword volume **< threshold** → not bold
- Applied in BOTH the topic box preview AND the expand-arrow overlay

**Out of scope at this layer:** W#1 tables (AST / MT / TIF / KAS / TVT). The director explicitly confirmed canvas-only for the W#1 implementation.

**Cross-tool display convention (PLATFORM-LEVEL DIRECTIVE, NEW 2026-04-27):** the same display convention applies anywhere topics + keywords are surfaced in downstream workflow tools — Therapeutic Strategy (W#3), Conversion Funnel & Narrative Architecture (W#5), and any future workflow that displays topics + keywords. The same volume-totals + per-keyword-volumes-in-parens + bold-by-threshold convention follows the data into those tools. Forward-pointers added to W#3 and W#5 ROADMAP entries per `HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 21 (pre-interview directive scan) so the convention is surfaced as the first item of those workflows' Workflow Requirements Interviews when they happen.

**Implementation note:** W#1 canvas implementation is moderate scope (CanvasPanel topic box rendering + expand-arrow overlay + per-keyword chip rendering — multiple files touched). The cross-tool convention is captured as a directive but does not require any W#1-time code work for downstream tools — those tools will implement when they're built.

**Open question to resolve at implementation time:** does this display convention also belong codified in `PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md` as a "shared display conventions" subsection so it's discoverable independently of ROADMAP entries? Director's call when implementation lands.

### Phase-2 server-side execution — explicit tie-back to Direct-mode UI hint (added 2026-04-26)

Per existing Phase 2 plan ("🚨 Server-side execution of AI jobs"), AI jobs will move from browser-direct to server-side persistent workers when the platform leaves Vercel (likely AWS migration). The Direct-mode UI hint added in the 2026-04-26 polish bundle exists *only* as a workaround for Vercel's 5-min serverless timeout. Once server-side execution is live and not bound by a 5-min ceiling, server-side becomes the preferred default for AI jobs and Direct mode is retained only as an escape hatch (admin ad-hoc testing, debugging, privacy-sensitive scenarios). The Direct-mode UI hint can be removed at that point. Code comment in `AutoAnalyze.tsx` (next to the hint conditional) notes this dependency.

### Infrastructure TODOs — all 3 RESOLVED in Pivot Session E (2026-04-25)

All three items deferred from Pivot Session D were resolved in Pivot Session E. Kept here for traceability.

1. ✅ **`keywordScope` activity-log label drift** — RESOLVED. Activity Log now emits the dropdown label ("Unsorted + Reshuffled") rather than the raw enum.

2. ✅ **`CanvasNode.id` global-PK design issue** — RESOLVED via the UUID migration (Option D). `CanvasNode.id` and `Pathway.id` are now `String @id @default(uuid())`. The latent bugs in `/canvas/nodes` POST and `/canvas/pathways` POST that band-aided around the integer-counter race are gone by construction. The original framing of this TODO listed Options A/B; Option D went beyond that and is strictly better given director's "data loss is OK" disclosure (no production canvas data exists outside Bursitis test data).

3. ✅ **`handleCancel` / `handleResumeCheckpoint` in-progress batch cleanup** — RESOLVED. `handleCancel` flips any `in_progress` batch to `failed`; `handleResumeCheckpoint` downgrades restored `in_progress` to `queued`.

### Phase 1g-test follow-up Part 3 Findings (2026-04-20 session) — critical issues surfaced

These are the problems director and Claude Code identified during the 2026-04-20 session, captured in detail so the next session doesn't re-derive them. Every item links to the solution design already discussed — implementation details are in the session plan further below.

**P3-F1 — Mode A qualitatively superior; Mode B silently overwrites Mode A's better work.** Director confirmed Mode A output is markedly better structurally than Mode B. But once the reactive switch fires, Mode B can modify topics Mode A created, masking Mode A's quality in the final admin-reviewable output. Admin currently has no way to distinguish which mode produced which part of the tree. **Fix design:** Changes Ledger with per-action provenance (mode/model/batch/settings); admin quality scoring (1-5) per action rolled up per mode; Mode A "protected" mode prevents Mode B overwrites of admin-marked-good Mode A actions; final review "mode difference" view.

**P3-F2 — Mode A quietly reshuffling topics under the hood, invisible to validation.** "0 removed" fires every batch, but canvas count oscillates (80→81→80→82→81→80...). "Unusually high: N new topics" warnings grow from 27 to 82. The model renames/splits/merges topics while validation checks only for by-name disappearance. **Fix design:** explicit topic-delete mechanism in model's output contract (DELETE row with keyword-reassignment + sub-topic-reparent payloads); "batch changes report" in output (Added/Renamed/Merged/Split/Deleted with reasons); Changes Ledger makes changes visible and reviewable; stability scoring with friction gradient (see `MODEL_QUALITY_SCORING.md`) discourages re-evaluation of already-good topics without justification.

**P3-F3 — Batch 52 Mode B "Lost 6" core keywords including "bursa".** Mode B is not a complete safety net. Delta merges can silently drop previously-applied keywords. The safety check caught it and triggered retry; director cancelled during retry 2. **Fix design:** salvage-ignored-keywords mechanism (targeted follow-up for "Missing" keywords); full-batch retry retained for "Lost" (Lost = structurally broken response, targeted follow-up won't fix). Also ties to stable topic IDs + Changes Ledger for audit.

**P3-F4 — Director's "bursa / Turkey-city" insight — homograph keywords force the model into bad behavior.** Bursitis keywords include "bursa" (fluid sac) but also "bursa" (Turkish city) + "bursa iş ilanları" (Turkish "Bursa job listings"). Current prompt forces the model to place every batch keyword under some topic. For irrelevant homographs, the model either (a) invents an awkward topic, (b) drops the keyword (safety check catches), or (c) shoves it under a loosely-related topic (quality corruption — invisible). **Fix design:** "Irrelevant Keywords" floating topic as a runtime safety valve the model can use freely; admin reviews Irrelevant Keywords topic in human-in-loop and either confirms irrelevance (→ Removed Terms) or corrects placement. Pending Deletion canvas region uses the same pattern for obsolete topics.

**P3-F5 — Model is not comprehensive in topic-chain creation.** Director's example: for keyword "bursitis pain in older women", the model should create placements in "bursitis pain" (primary) + "bursitis in women" (with upstream "Who does bursitis affect?" → "How bursitis affects different sexes differently") + "bursitis in older people" (with upstream "How does bursitis affect a person by age"). Current prompt's Steps 3-5 describe the process but model is being lazy under output-length pressure. **Fix design:** add Step 4b Comprehensiveness Verification to the Initial Prompt (see `AUTO_ANALYZE_PROMPT_V2_PROPOSED_CHANGES.md` Change 3) — model must self-check facet extraction against placement count per keyword; omitted facets require explicit justification in Reevaluation Report.

**P3-F6 — "Unusually high: N new topics" is misleading because it counts renames as new.** The code's diff detection compares response-row topic names against pre-existing canvas topic names. Renames ("Bursitis causes" → "Causes of bursitis") are counted as "new topics." Multiplied across 80+ rows per batch, the count becomes alarming noise. **Fix design:** (primary) stable topic IDs so renames are explicit RENAME operations, not name-mismatches; (fallback) fuzzy/semantic matching layer for interim detection. Also: detailed activity log per batch breaking down "Renamed: X / Restructured: Y / Truly new: Z / Unchanged: W" instead of a single "N new topics" count.

**P3-F7 — Keyword being left out of batches (director's AST Table observation).** Director sees "Unsorted" status interspersed with "AI Sorted" status in the AST Table after a run, and checked that the Unsorted keywords don't appear in Topics or Analysis tables. Theories: (a) batches are pre-decided at run start and orphaned keywords are missed permanently; (b) AI is not analyzing them; (c) status isn't updating despite canvas verification passing. **Fix design:** investigate batching code (Session 2 investigation scope). Add redundancy: tool independently re-scans AST post-batch and flags any keyword that should be AI-sorted but isn't; re-queues missed keywords into subsequent batches; logs to activity log AND Changes Ledger.

**UPDATE 2026-04-24 (Session 2 diagnosis):** Root cause identified as TWO-WAY sync drift between `Keyword.sortingStatus` and `CanvasNode.linkedKwIds`. Two distinct bugs share one architectural flaw (two sources of truth updated unidirectionally, no reconciliation). **Bug 1 — "silent placements" (58 kw):** `doApply` step 11 at `AutoAnalyze.tsx` line 1179 iterates only `batch.keywordIds` when marking AI-Sorted, but step 9 at line 1147–1165 updates `Keyword.topic` for every keyword matching any text in the AI's response. Mode A's full-table view places prior-batch keywords as [p] primary in later batches → step 9 fires (topic updated) → step 11 doesn't (not in batch.keywordIds) → silent placement. **Bug 2 — "ghost AI-Sorted" (74 kw):** split into (a) 49 reshuffle casualties where step 11 only ADDS to AI-Sorted, never REMOVES — later canvas rebuilds remove kw from canvas but status stays stale; (b) 25 linkedKwIds-carryover ghosts via the `existing?.linkedKwIds || []` fallback at line 1003 — when AI response has empty `kwRaw` for a node, node inherits prior linkedKwIds → step 11 marks inherited kw AI-Sorted (if in batch.keywordIds) while step 9 never touches them. **Theory (a) from original P3-F7 entry partly validated (batches are pre-decided at buildQueue time), but not the core bug — core issue is status/canvas drift from unidirectional updates.** Director's two-part fix direction agreed: primary stack via root-cause fixes already in Sessions 3-6 (salvage-ignored-keywords + prompt changes + stable topic IDs + stability scoring + Changes Ledger) + backup via post-batch reconciliation pass in Session 3 scope. Per director: "Whatever fix we apply here should be a backup to that primary fix." See `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-SESSION-2 STATE block for full detail + Session 3 block below for implementation scope.

**P3-F8 — Canvas layout regressions.** Overlapping nodes, descriptions extending outside node boxes, wrong ordering/placement. These were fixed in the HTML tool (`keyword_sorting_tool_v18.html`); regressed during React migration. `resolveOverlap` function exists in the React tool (per `PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md` line 388) but appears insufficient. **Fix design:** Session 2 diagnostic pass — compare HTML tool's layout code to React tool's `resolveOverlap`. Director will upload HTML tool to repo root after 2026-04-20 session wrap-up.

**UPDATE 2026-04-24 Session 2b (diagnosis complete):** Root cause is a single architectural gap — the React port migrated canvas *rendering* (node cards, connectors, drag, zoom, single-node overlap nudge) but did NOT port the HTML tool's four-job *layout engine*. The four missing jobs: (1) `cvsNodeH` content-driven node-height calculation using canvas `measureText` (React has `NODE_H=160` constant; `h` from DB, never recomputed — direct cause of description overflow); (2) `cvsPushDownOverlaps` holistic 4-step pass (reset → tree-walk `layoutChildren` → 60-pass overlap resolution → pathway separation) called after every structural change including every Auto-Analyze batch apply (React has no equivalent; only `resolveOverlap(nodeId)` which fires on drag/resize only — direct cause of overlapping nodes and wrong order after Auto-Analyze batches); (3) `cvsAutoLayoutChild` type-aware auto-positioning when parent-child links are formed (React has no equivalent — direct cause of wrong linear-vs-nested placement); (4) `cvsSeparatePathways` horizontal push-apart for overlapping pathway borders (React has no equivalent). Bonus gap #5: `baseY`/`y` separation for clean collapse/expand restoration (React has only `y`). **Director's fix direction agreed (Q1/Q2/Q3 in Session 2b):** one-shot port of all four functions in a single Session 3 commit; layout pass runs after every Auto-Analyze batch (not just run-end); pathway separation included (NOT deferred). Item #5 (`baseY`/`y`) defers to a follow-up session. Full diagnostic detail in `KEYWORD_CLUSTERING_ACTIVE.md` POST-SESSION-2b STATE block.

**P3-F9 — Cost tracker missing failed-attempt costs + too-small UI + no estimated-vs-actual.** Known issue carried from 2026-04-18. Director added: need manual "Add API Cost" button with rows per entry, total cost display per tool per project, both estimated and actual with variance. See new roadmap item "Cost Ledger" below.

**P3-F10 — Claude's Q4 framing error (logged in CORRECTIONS_LOG).** Claude framed Mode A cost/time as "wasted" relative to Mode B in Q4 analysis, assuming quality parity between modes. Director correctly pushed back: quality matters more than speed/cost for a product-launch hierarchy. Claude acknowledged the error openly; the "functional prerequisite" claim downgrade above is the direct correction.

**P3-F11 — Auto-Analyze settings don't persist across hard refresh (API key, initial prompt, primer prompt, model).** Known; promoted to active work. Move from localStorage-only to `UserPreference` DB table with auto-save on change + load-on-mount.

**Session 3a — DONE 2026-04-24 (deployed; director visual-verification pending):**

**Session 3b — DONE 2026-04-25 (committed `6c09e50`, NOT YET PUSHED, awaiting director approval per Rule 9):**

Walks entire AST table (not just batch.keywordIds): on-canvas + Unsorted/Reshuffled → AI-Sorted; off-canvas + AI-Sorted → NEW `'Reshuffled'` status (per director's Option B framing during drift-check — every off-canvas flip is alarming, not silent). Each off-canvas flip emits structured aaLog line. New `'Reshuffled'` status: yellow `.ast-pill-r` badge across AST + RemovedKeyword display + TIFTable; type union extended; column is String so no schema migration; AutoAnalyze "Unsorted only" scope label renamed to "Unsorted + Reshuffled" + filter picks up both.

Delta rows merged via refactored `mergeDelta(deltaTsv, baseTsv?)` (new optional 2nd arg). IRRELEVANT_KEYWORDS POSTed per-keyword to `/api/projects/[projectId]/removed-keywords` with `removedSource='auto-ai-detected-irrelevant'` + `aiReasoning`. Archived ids dropped from batch + parent state synced; re-validates; doApply if pass; fall-through-to-retry on salvage error.

All pure functions mutating passed nodes array in place. Constants mirror HTML except NODE_W=220 + NESTED_INDENT=30 (React values preserved). Wired into `AutoAnalyze.doApply` step 7.5 (per Q1 — every batch) and `CanvasPanel.handleLinkClick` (autoLayoutChild on link-form). Existing `resolveOverlap(nodeId)` retained for drag/resize. baseY/y collapse-restore deferred to follow-up.
- Build clean (22.5s, 17/17 pages, zero TypeScript errors). Commit `6c09e50` 9 files (+786/-38), 1 new file (`src/lib/canvas-layout.ts`). **PUSHED 2026-04-25 in Session 3b verify** with director's explicit Rule-9 approval, alongside the prior unpushed `8afcb9f` (Session-3a doc updates) and the Session-3b doc-updates commit `aa7eb4b`.
